Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

name: Adam Schiff
subject: Louis DeJoy may be gone, but the problem remains
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| Trump’s plan to let his cronies take over the work and profit off it would devastate the millions of Americans counting on the services of a fully functional and independent USPS.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=8536%2E5257348%2EWMybR6)
Derek,
For years, you and thousands of other grassroots supporters have joined me in calling for USPS Postmaster General Louis DeJoy to improve the Postal Service — or resign.
DeJoy’s leadership of the USPS led to slower services, mail delays, overworked postal workers, and so much more.
**Louis DeJoy finally resigned from the USPS, but the damage is already done — and what follows is sure to be far worse.**
Just before he resigned, DeJoy announced that he would work with DOGE to fire 10,000 employees from the Postal Service. And now, Donald Trump and his ultra-wealthy advisors want to privatize the Postal Service and end years of American support for this vital American service.
The USPS provides critical support for seniors who depend on the Postal Service for medicine and Social Security checks, small businesses who depend on it to deliver their packages, and voters who depend on it to cast their ballots by mail.
**Trump’s plan to let his cronies take over the work and profit off it would devastate the millions of Americans counting on the services of a fully functional and independent USPS.**
**[Join me in opposing Trump’s plans to privatize the USPS and sell it to the highest bidder. We need to protect the Postal Service from DOGE and an administration hell-bent on running it into the ground.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1023?c0=engagement-202505-USPS.CSIXDv&refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20250504_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=8536%2E5257348%2EWMybR6)**
[PROTECT THE USPS](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1023?c0=engagement-202505-USPS.CSIXDv&refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20250504_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=5&akid=8536%2E5257348%2EWMybR6)
---
Thanks for stepping up today.
— Adam

name: Jason Miyares
subject: 100 years in jail.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Miyares for Virginia |
body:
| | | Peter, Last month, my office helped secure a conviction against cop killer Michael Donivan White.

**The sentence for his combined crimes?_[_100 years in jail._](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/AtT3Y0Tq9zZhMaJNnKjdISWHjJ00LcL_AfoDUqIDeVPQ1xgIiUiNrcxhvYAMq2ayiCEoxe-1nk56qJY-mrSFj6rQdRkgNaQFp6CfJcT7GHf7dFNeinPLgnveJK066TXNoQVwg1-2l7uaRmrgUkjUk5Iytpc2yanWmLbF47CuVV2OvyvfCWCcgnMWLbm5wzacUYGwrKF97e4F4751mded6N_F-hfNbntygPBDuD6Q30GTVwwvBIFFWNC8nx2rwlWohUuT9d-N6_FuzQwz7jlv6Pj-srJ1A2Vz2JKnDS53TLwWiYv2ZI5HtZJp7xS3QarCesEwTV1pzhq9I5Tf_AUHfuYJJwbaiw7rFFy9DrtY89xvsmxRRR10uiomtND6fvPFROiq61_CDgoHDroO_E_YMrjzNCPuvt2dVvBL4aQTwEn9BafYnj1G-aP5t_LHGtvKLz6rgNqO91G4FLBSKUur/XCGdV0Fx84J8fQWBdx7sGkZj6kUdSrms/8)_**

It’s a matter of justice. But it also sends a much-needed message.

_To those who seek to harm the brave men and women who wear the badge—know this: _

_We will find you. _
_We will arrest you. _
_We will prosecute you. _

_And if you take an officer’s life, you will never see the light of day again—that’s a promise._
__
---
[](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/cbLtTaT_SD7xdX3HK3JK2jzvFBcCwqQ0qLzbbfpYengzYmi0LKF8Mnjcg6_MhYkKvPymaH2o_8Kj4mHZPyXiBHCXtLCXjr5X45--fM9l9gQbTupCKaHPCf2jGJW-BW0wEyj1M9snCAff89LAVS4dCXap57HqHJIRTjUd5fgyBseaj4_uIWjQEBT2RHXgQOz5QIO46CXONLFQEF6tw8ippj6UP0cUSHesq8UDbUlTlKktjl6jKohNIBJnYi4sFrapA98S4YeIxHPy1lEZWXczNs2MdhtshF5tPtiiC7eQbm3AbpPC_mT8Y2g_sPZPaA4zyeUF3mlsdISGsFJ_dIcCu0Lv_tn2Qrepzz18dzpRzU4XhNXopqGKnPF-hpUX8VEGKcbik1gRkHJ4NoDVaOH1n-KWNXG17iquKihK6tBMDFjxcHjkPdzoQ_q8ZyrX9S8fMA5vM6ItgAZk5pV3MAIY/tZXWT1vMrG3Kwoq0_ToAqFHAMFc9LFJB/8)
---
There are few careers in this country where the mere fact of going to work puts your own life at risk.

**Working with those in law enforcement on an almost daily basis, I can tell you that while the media may have forgotten the sting of calls to defund and devalue our police, they have not.** Too often they feel it is a thankless job with the public made entirely unaware of the sacrifices required.

_**Let’s show them that we have their backs, Peter.**_
---

---
Thank you,Attorney General Jason Miyares

Now, I hope you will join me in standing up for what's right, no matter the odds. = | = | = | = | = | = The fate of our little galaxy, our beautiful Commonwealth, is up to each of us. May the Fourth be with you, Abigail Spanberger * | | Paid for by Spanberger for Governor Spanberger for Governor P.O. Box 3121 Glen Allen, VA 23058 United States | = =

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 5/4/2025
body:
Final Week of Session, Major Identity Theft Bombshell, and Gala Tickets Drop Tomorrow
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**May 4, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear Readers,
It’s the final week of Colorado’s legislative session—and we’re bracing for a storm. Expect a flood of late-night votes, backdoor deals, and bills moving faster than anyone can track. In fact, just a heads up, HB25-1312 is headed to the Senate Floor for debate TOMORROW (Monday, 5/5). We’ll be bringing you frequent updates all throughout this week—both here and on social media. Make sure you're subscribed and following along.
Now, onto this week’s top stories.
We just dropped a [jaw-dropping new exposé](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) by guest columnist Bob Cooper. It uncovers what may be the **largest identity theft scandal in Colorado history**.
Thousands of Coloradans—many elderly—are showing up in federal donation records with tens of thousands of political contributions made in their names. We’re talking six figures... often from people in assisted living or fixed-income housing. The names appear repeatedly—sometimes 20 or 30 times a day—[linked to ActBlue and Democrat campaigns](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). The kicker? Most of them probably have no idea. This story is far from over.
Meanwhile, our third installment of **[The COvid Chronicles](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** []() is out. In just 14 days during April 2020, Colorado saw a rapid descent from lockdowns to tyranny: neighbors snitching, cops handcuffing dads for playing catch with their kids, and government officials monitoring phone movements. It’s not just a history lesson—it’s a warning. What happened in Colorado can happen again unless we hold the line on liberty.
And speaking of big moments... **🎟 Tickets for the Mountain Majesty Gala go on sale TOMORROW!**
We’re honored to welcome Eric Trump as our keynote speaker for the June 21st event. This is going to be a night to remember. Space is limited—so be sure to check your inbox tomorrow and secure your spot.
Colorado is at a crossroads—from political corruption to the erosion of civil liberties. But truth matters. Your voice matters. And we’re here to keep shining a light.
In liberty and truth,
Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Mesa County residents and national advocates call on Governor Polis to release Tina Peters at virtual rally](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When free speech and election transparency collide with political pressure, citizens are often caught in the middle. Mesa County residents and national election integrity advocates gathered virtually on April 24 to rally support for Tina Peters, the former Mesa County clerk and recorder now incarcerated at La Vista Correctional Facility in Pueblo, Colorado.
__
**[Clock runs out on social media bill: lawmakers shield themselves and Polis from historic override](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Without casting a single “no” vote, Colorado lawmakers on April 28 killed a bipartisan attempt to override Governor Jared Polis’ veto of a social media regulation bill. Just days earlier, the Senate had voted 29–6 to override the veto of Senate Bill 25-086, marking the state’s first successful chamber override of a policy bill in more than a decade.

**[Doctors, parents, pastors say HB25-1312 “crosses line” as Senate advances bill after midnight](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) HB25-1312, also known as the “Kelly Loving Act,” proposes major changes to how Colorado law handles gender identity. After more than eight hours of emotional testimony that went past 1:00 a.m., the Senate Judiciary Committee advanced the bill on a 5–2 party-line vote.

|
---
**LEGISLATIVE SESSION**
**[“Drawing the line”: School boards warn HB25-1312 oversteps on parental rights, brings policy chaos](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) More than 70 school board members and education leaders have signed a letter urging lawmakers to reject HB25-1312, also known as the Kelly Loving Act.
**[Final week at the Capitol: Democrats target TABOR, transparency and immigration enforcement](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[DENVER7](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) With under a week to go before they adjourn this year’s legislative session, Colorado lawmakers still have a lot of work ahead of them. Dozens of bills are still under consideration, including several controversial proposals.

**[“Sitting on a powder keg”: Opponents slam SB25-276 for shielding illegal aliens, risking federal funds](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [CBS COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A bill aimed at protecting undocumented immigrants in Colorado has created a firestorm at the Colorado State Capitol. Supporters say it’s about protecting the constitutional rights of everyone regardless of citizenship. Opponents, including Teller County Sheriff Jason Mikesell, say it would protect dangerous criminals.
**[Colorado braces for special session over bloated Medicaid spending amid federal pressure](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) There are now only seven days left in Colorado’s legislative session. But lawmakers and other state officials have for weeks been bracing for the possibility of coming back to the Capitol later this year to deal with potential federal cuts to Medicaid likely to be included in Congressional Republicans’ still-being-written budget proposal.

**[Colorado GOP lawmakers and sheriffs press AG Bondi to examine SB3 for 2A infringements](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX 31 NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi was sent a formal letter on Monday urging a Department of Justice investigation into the state of Colorado’s gun laws, specifically citing a newly signed law that Second Amendment advocates have called an infringement on their rights.
__
**[READ MORE LEGISLATIVE SESSION UPDATES](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**STATE**
**[Polls show Republican momentum, Democrat doubts as Colorado’s 2026 battle lines form](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Unaffiliated voters now make up nearly half of Colorado’s electorate – and both parties are fighting to find a winning message. With the 2026 midterms on the horizon, several new polls offer an early glimpse into Colorado’s shifting political terrain.

**[DOJ sues Colorado and Denver over sanctuary policies ‘tying hands of law enforcement’](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX 31 NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The U.S. Department of Justice is suing the state of Colorado and the city of Denver for laws and statutes the federal government says are “sanctuary laws.”

**[Yadira Caraveo’s former aides say they were mistreated, traumatized by Colorado congresswoman](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) U.S. Rep. Yadira Caraveo’s behavior last year while serving in Congress and running for reelection was so frightening and traumatizing to staff that aides proposed a safety plan requesting that sharp objects be removed from the Colorado Democrat’s offices.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[FEMA cuts $32 million from Denver’s immigrant shelter grants under Trump directive](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The Trump administration has terminated roughly $32 million in grant funding to Denver earmarked for the city’s response to the illegal immigration crisis, saying such spending no longer aligns with the federal government’s enforcement priorities.

**[Trump signs executive order cracking down on ‘sanctuary’ cities, threatens their federal funding](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday instructing the Justice Department and the Department of Homeland Security to create a list of all sanctuary cities failing to adhere to federal immigration laws, providing them a chance to abandon their sanctuary status.

**[How Trump sealed the border in his first 100 days](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[DAILY WIRE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The United States endured a never-before-seen surge in illegal immigration under President Joe Biden, watching as hordes of migrants charged border barriers and towns across the country were reshaped virtually overnight by mass migration.

**_[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Was it the people’s town hall — or a political playbook in action?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) On April 26, more than 100 residents gathered at the Moxi Theater in downtown Greeley for a two-hour town hall organized by progressive groups, including Vote Save America PAC. Billed as “The People’s Town Hall: Where is Gabe?” – the event gave attendees from across the political spectrum a chance to speak up about representation, leadership, and what they want to see from elected officials.

**[Denver vs. Aurora: Homelessness reports reveal two competing visions](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[WESTWORD](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The first state-funded homelessness report to look at the crisis across Colorado came out on Monday, April 28, with an estimate that nearly 53,000 people are experiencing homelessness statewide. The recommended solution sets it apart from another report published just a couple of weeks ago.
**[Nearly 10 pounds of fentanyl powder seized in drug trafficking operation on Colorado’s Western Slope](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[CBS COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Seven people have been arrested, accused in a drug trafficking operation in Garfield County on Colorado’s Western Slope. According to investigators, approximately four pounds of methamphetamine, three ounces of cocaine, nine-and-a-half pounds of fentanyl powder, and approximately 220 fentanyl pills, along with a firearm, were seized.

**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Since 2021, inflation has been higher in Colorado than in any other state, mostly because of the cost of owning or renting a home. But this is also because the state legislature enjoys imposing new fees (you aren’t allowed to call them taxes) on seemingly anything they can think of._

**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Living in Colorado for nearly four years, I’ve grown to love its sweeping landscapes and spirited community. But the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act, passed by the House in 2024 and under Senate review in 2025, alongside the REAL ID Act’s looming May 7, 2025, deadline, has exposed a personal struggle that highlights a broader issue._

clean_body: Final Week of Session, Major Identity Theft Bombshell, and Gala Tickets Drop Tomorrow | | | | May 4, 2025 SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST By Heidi Ganahl | | Dear Readers, It’s the final week of Colorado’s legislative session—and we’re bracing for a storm. Expect a flood of late-night votes, backdoor deals, and bills moving faster than anyone can track. In fact, just a heads up, HB25-1312 is headed to the Senate Floor for debate TOMORROW (Monday, 5/5). We’ll be bringing you frequent updates all throughout this week—both here and on social media. Make sure you're subscribed and following along. Now, onto this week’s top stories. We just dropped a by guest columnist Bob Cooper. It uncovers what may be the largest identity theft scandal in Colorado history. Thousands of Coloradans—many elderly—are showing up in federal donation records with tens of thousands of political contributions made in their names. We’re talking six figures... often from people in assisted living or fixed-income housing. The names appear repeatedly—sometimes 20 or 30 times a day—. The kicker? Most of them probably have no idea. This story is far from over. Meanwhile, our third installment of []() is out. In just 14 days during April 2020, Colorado saw a rapid descent from lockdowns to tyranny: neighbors snitching, cops handcuffing dads for playing catch with their kids, and government officials monitoring phone movements. It’s not just a history lesson—it’s a warning. What happened in Colorado can happen again unless we hold the line on liberty. And speaking of big moments... 🎟 Tickets for the Mountain Majesty Gala go on sale TOMORROW! We’re honored to welcome Eric Trump as our keynote speaker for the June 21st event. This is going to be a night to remember. Space is limited—so be sure to check your inbox tomorrow and secure your spot. Colorado is at a crossroads—from political corruption to the erosion of civil liberties. But truth matters. Your voice matters. And we’re here to keep shining a light. In liberty and truth, Heidi Ganahl | | | TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK When free speech and election transparency collide with political pressure, citizens are often caught in the middle. Mesa County residents and national election integrity advocates gathered virtually on April 24 to rally support for Tina Peters, the former Mesa County clerk and recorder now incarcerated at La Vista Correctional Facility in Pueblo, Colorado. Without casting a single “no” vote, Colorado lawmakers on April 28 killed a bipartisan attempt to override Governor Jared Polis’ veto of a social media regulation bill. Just days earlier, the Senate had voted 29–6 to override the veto of Senate Bill 25-086, marking the state’s first successful chamber override of a policy bill in more than a decade. HB25-1312, also known as the “Kelly Loving Act,” proposes major changes to how Colorado law handles gender identity. After more than eight hours of emotional testimony that went past 1:00 a.m., the Senate Judiciary Committee advanced the bill on a 5–2 party-line vote. | LEGISLATIVE SESSION More than 70 school board members and education leaders have signed a letter urging lawmakers to reject HB25-1312, also known as the Kelly Loving Act. With under a week to go before they adjourn this year’s legislative session, Colorado lawmakers still have a lot of work ahead of them. Dozens of bills are still under consideration, including several controversial proposals. A bill aimed at protecting undocumented immigrants in Colorado has created a firestorm at the Colorado State Capitol. Supporters say it’s about protecting the constitutional rights of everyone regardless of citizenship. Opponents, including Teller County Sheriff Jason Mikesell, say it would protect dangerous criminals. There are now only seven days left in Colorado’s legislative session. But lawmakers and other state officials have for weeks been bracing for the possibility of coming back to the Capitol later this year to deal with potential federal cuts to Medicaid likely to be included in Congressional Republicans’ still-being-written budget proposal. U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi was sent a formal letter on Monday urging a Department of Justice investigation into the state of Colorado’s gun laws, specifically citing a newly signed law that Second Amendment advocates have called an infringement on their rights. | STATE Unaffiliated voters now make up nearly half of Colorado’s electorate – and both parties are fighting to find a winning message. With the 2026 midterms on the horizon, several new polls offer an early glimpse into Colorado’s shifting political terrain. The U.S. Department of Justice is suing the state of Colorado and the city of Denver for laws and statutes the federal government says are “sanctuary laws.” U.S. Rep. Yadira Caraveo’s behavior last year while serving in Congress and running for reelection was so frightening and traumatizing to staff that aides proposed a safety plan requesting that sharp objects be removed from the Colorado Democrat’s offices. | NATIONAL The Trump administration has terminated roughly $32 million in grant funding to Denver earmarked for the city’s response to the illegal immigration crisis, saying such spending no longer aligns with the federal government’s enforcement priorities. President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday instructing the Justice Department and the Department of Homeland Security to create a list of all sanctuary cities failing to adhere to federal immigration laws, providing them a chance to abandon their sanctuary status. The United States endured a never-before-seen surge in illegal immigration under President Joe Biden, watching as hordes of migrants charged border barriers and towns across the country were reshaped virtually overnight by mass migration. | LOCAL On April 26, more than 100 residents gathered at the Moxi Theater in downtown Greeley for a two-hour town hall organized by progressive groups, including Vote Save America PAC. Billed as “The People’s Town Hall: Where is Gabe?” – the event gave attendees from across the political spectrum a chance to speak up about representation, leadership, and what they want to see from elected officials. The first state-funded homelessness report to look at the crisis across Colorado came out on Monday, April 28, with an estimate that nearly 53,000 people are experiencing homelessness statewide. The recommended solution sets it apart from another report published just a couple of weeks ago. Seven people have been arrested, accused in a drug trafficking operation in Garfield County on Colorado’s Western Slope. According to investigators, approximately four pounds of methamphetamine, three ounces of cocaine, nine-and-a-half pounds of fentanyl powder, and approximately 220 fentanyl pills, along with a firearm, were seized. | COMMENTARY Since 2021, inflation has been higher in Colorado than in any other state, mostly because of the cost of owning or renting a home. name: Sara Jacobs
subject: DOGE is going after Social Security
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em]
Folks,
While Republicans focus on handing out tax breaks to billionaires and Donald Trump focuses on instigating a constitutional crisis, Elon Musk is up to his own antics – recently announcing that Social Security is “the big one to eliminate” for DOGE.
This comes after he called Social Security “the biggest Ponzi scheme of all time” and falsely claimed that undocumented immigrants and long-deceased Americans are among those receiving benefits.
Let’s be really clear: Social Security is a hugely successful program that allows millions of hard-working Americans to retire with dignity and receive the benefits they earned after paying into the program for decades. It’s also one of our most effective anti-poverty programs in the United States!
I may be young compared to my fellow Members of Congress, but even I am old enough to remember that Republicans used to shy away from these wildly extreme proposals that would gut programs that seniors rely on like Social Security and Medicare!
Now that Musk has made it clear that Social Security has a massive target on its back, it’s more important than ever that we make it unmistakably clear to the GOP that this is NOT an acceptable move for the American people.
I’ve said it before and I’ll say it again – a strong public outcry does matter right now.
So today I’m asking: Will you sign my petition to pass my Delete DOGE Act so we can stop Elon Musk and protect Social Security? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/nZo7FQ33c0Cinl3c-cnlpQ2]
SIGN THE PETITION [https://secure.ngpvan.com/nZo7FQ33c0Cinl3c-cnlpQ2]
I’ll never stop fighting to protect Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, and other critical programs that millions of people rely on.
Thanks for speaking up today.
-Sara

Congresswoman Sara Jacobs
California’s 51st District

| _Bad news from DC, team_ : House Republicans just voted to advance a measure that would rollback California clean air protections. Republicans have been desperate for **ANY** way to rollback our progress on cleaner air. T _hey even threatened to withhold federal aid for the Los Angeles wildfires until our clean air protections were repealed._ **[Now, they have won this initial vote in the U.S. House. That’s why we are asking you to sign our petition calling for the Senate to REJECT this backwards proposal.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107086570/543603731/1206169991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjkwOTRhNS01NTI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5DZB712mQzmTxYaKjmNfAwDYXnT_qH4QHJklJ1RTKV0=&emci=a3b8754d-c827-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=6f9094a5-5529-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=560478)** [SIGN NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107086571/543603732/1206169991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjkwOTRhNS01NTI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5DZB712mQzmTxYaKjmNfAwDYXnT_qH4QHJklJ1RTKV0=&emci=a3b8754d-c827-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=6f9094a5-5529-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=560478) **Simply put, it seems like MAGA Republicans are hellbent on making California smoggy again.** The vote to rollback our clean air protections is a victory for fossil fuel interests and a loss for Californians who just want to breathe clean air and live healthy lives. If the GOP succeeds in rolling back these protections, more people will continue to get sick from exposure to extreme ozone pollution. This is unconscionable and also a violation of our rights as a state. California Strong Senator, Adam Schiff said it well: _“California’s authority and responsibility to regulate its own pollution is enshrined in federal law and has been repeatedly approved on a bipartisan basis. We will fight this latest attack on California’s power to protect its own residents, and I will urge my colleagues in the Senate to recognize the severe implications of proceeding with this violation...”_ **[But we aren’t just sitting around hoping for the best. We are joining Adam Schiff in this fight by calling on all Democrats to sign our grassroots petition urging the Senate to REJECT this attempt to rollback clean air protections. Will you add your name?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107086572/543603733/1206169991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjkwOTRhNS01NTI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5DZB712mQzmTxYaKjmNfAwDYXnT_qH4QHJklJ1RTKV0=&emci=a3b8754d-c827-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=6f9094a5-5529-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=560478)** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107086573/543603734/1206169991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjkwOTRhNS01NTI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5DZB712mQzmTxYaKjmNfAwDYXnT_qH4QHJklJ1RTKV0=&emci=a3b8754d-c827-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=6f9094a5-5529-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=560478) Thank you!

clean_body: Bad news from DC, team: | = | Bad news from DC, team : House Republicans just voted to advance a measure that would rollback California clean air protections. Republicans have been desperate for ANY way to rollback our progress on cleaner air. T hey even threatened to withhold federal aid for the Los Angeles wildfires until our clean air protections were repealed. Simply put, it seems like MAGA Republicans are hellbent on making California smoggy again. The vote to rollback our clean air protections is a victory for fossil fuel interests and a loss for Californians who just want to breathe clean air and live healthy lives. If the GOP succeeds in rolling back these protections, more people will continue to get sick from exposure to extreme ozone pollution. This is unconscionable and also a violation of our rights as a state. California Strong Senator, Adam Schiff said it well: “California’s authority and responsibility to regulate its own pollution is enshrined in federal law and has been repeatedly approved on a bipartisan basis. We will fight this latest attack on California’s power to protect its own residents, and I will urge my colleagues in the Senate to recognize the severe implications of proceeding with this violation...” Thank you! \- CADEM | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. name: Elise Stefanik
subject: My new leadership position
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC
body:
I want you to hear the big news directly from me.
| **__A Personal Note from Rep. Elise Stefanik on her New Leadership Position__**
---
Peter,
---
I just received the honor of being appointed to the position of Chairwoman of House Republican Leadership for the 119th Congress!
---
**This position has been used by prior Republican House leadership teams to deliver for the American people in critical moments for our country, such as the 1994 Contract with America and the 2010 Tea Party wave election.**
---
_I look forward to picking up the baton to help guide our House GOP Majority through another such moment as we execute on the mandate from the American people to pass the President’s America First agenda._
---
We’re truly on the brink of ushering in a new “Golden Age” for our country!
---
But at such a critical moment, with the very fate of America on the line, advancing the President’s agenda won’t be my only responsibility...
---
**_...he’s also entrusted me with using this new position in House Republican Leadership to protect our razor-thin majority._**
---
We simply cannot let the Democrats FLIP the House and rob us of this historic opportunity to Make America Great Again!
---
_With the Left preparing to spend BILLIONS against us in the midterms, I have no choice but to do everything in my power to close the gap on their record-breaking war chest as quickly as possible._
---
**That is why, in my capacity as the _NEW_ Chairwoman of House Republican Leadership, I immediately authorized a 500% MATCH on all gifts for the next hour to help us kick off my first 24 hours in this new role with our biggest fundraising surge of the year! **
---
_If (and only if) you’re in the position to do so, I ask that you give any amount you can afford to take advantage of this match._
---

name: Peter Ohtaki
subject: Final chance - action required re: Sunset Magazine property
body:
Unfortunately, State Senator Josh Becker’s bill to repeal Builders’ Remedy failed to pass the Democratic-controlled State Senate last week. Sen. Christoper Cabaldon (D-Napa) said the Builder’s Remedy | | | [](https://engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=681838523c90c03b7180da39&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mainstreamrevolution.com%2F%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68%26ss_email_id%3D681838523c90c03b7180da39%26ss_campaign_name%3DFinal%2Bchance%2B-%2Baction%2Brequired%2Bre%253A%2BSunset%2BMagazine%2Bproperty%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-05-05T04%253A02%253A31Z&w=6619ff0a31aa07375378c596&c=b_68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68&l=en-US&s=MlnYTmSL1MDw7LTxOT9VB6dO7Ic%3D)
---
| Unfortunately, State Senator Josh Becker’s bill to repeal Builders’ Remedy failed to pass the Democratic-controlled State Senate last week. Sen. Christoper Cabaldon (D-Napa) said _the Builder’s Remedy “is intentionally supposed to be something that is so awful that cities and counties will do what we want them to do ...It is serving its purpose._ ”**ACTION REQUIRED:** The State Historical Resources Commission (SHRC) will vote Friday 9am on whether to add Sunset Headquarters to the National Registry of Historic Places. Please email CalSHPO.shrc@parks.ca.gov or join the [Zoom](https://engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=681838523c90c03b7180da39&u=https%3A%2F%2Fus02web.zoom.us%2Fwebinar%2Fregister%2FWN_PFnp1980SxOKZvwn08CqNw%23%2Fregistration&w=6619ff0a31aa07375378c596&c=b_68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68&l=en-US&s=3pb_G_bPdOShWuot7Hce6Qvhm2M%3D) meeting. Here’s what I’m writing:**Public comment #VII. A. 1 - Sunset Headquarters, Menlo Park, CA** Dear Commissioners, I can think of no place besides Sutter’s Mill or the _Hollywood_ sign, which evokes the cultural identity of the California lifestyle and promise than the _Sunset Magazine_ Headquarters in Menlo Park. _Sunset Magazine_ monthly showed middle class suburban living in California with gardens alive with native draught-tolerant flowers and plants. PBSSoCal wrote _[Sunset Magazine: the Bible of Western Living](https://engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=681838523c90c03b7180da39&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.pbssocal.org%2Fhistory-society%2Fsunset-magazine-the-bible-of-western-living&w=6619ff0a31aa07375378c596&c=b_68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68&l=en-US&s=TmCZfnK0HIdwRBSKYHXCHz2Mcoc%3D)_. Homeowners nationwide bought the Sunset _Western Garden Book_ each year, and annual Sunset Celebration brought foodies and gardeners together each year at its Menlo Park HQ until just before the pandemic.Your vote can either preserve the site of Western living or result in three 30+ story high rises, the tallest between San Francisco and San Jose, due to the state’s Builders’ Remedy law.
---
## Housing, Builders’ Remedy and Local Control
---
Peter Ohtaki, October 12, 2024
---
> _Hi Peter-
> We were all talking about the horrible proposal for development on the Sunset Magazine property and we were all pretty clueless - LOL. I said, you know who would know something - Ohtaki. :-) So you were on our minds - hope you are well and what is the deal with that development anyway? :-)_
Yes, the [N17 proposed development](https://engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=681838523c90c03b7180da39&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwillowpark.life&w=6619ff0a31aa07375378c596&c=b_68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68&l=en-US&s=IHcchLhkKWc0ZdBe5NhYTQGLOYo%3D) on the former Sunset magazine HQ is outrageously huge and out of character for Menlo Park - two 460 feet high rises (~40 stories) like in Manhattan plopped into the mid-Peninsula. Over the last decade, the State of California government has taken away local control over zoning to encourage high-density housing everywhere. _**"Builders Remedy” law allows high-density development over objections and zoning of local communities**_ Since some cities have not met their Regional Housing Needs Allocation (RHNA), SB330 Builders Remedy law allows a high-density development with 20% affordable units, in this case high rises, which exceed the city’s zoning height restrictions if the city’s Housing Element hasn’t been approved by the state’s Housing and Community Development (HCD) agency. N17, owned by the son of a Russian oligarch, applied 2 weeks before Menlo Park’s current Housing Element was approved.Unfortunately, there’s nothing cities can do about it, unless we can pressure our state representatives to repeal the Builders Remedy law that encourages these monstrosities! See recent [Daily Post editorial.](https://engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=681838523c90c03b7180da39&u=https%3A%2F%2Fpadailypost.com%2F2024%2F08%2F26%2Fopinion-the-50-story-elephant-of-menlo-park-politics%2F&w=6619ff0a31aa07375378c596&c=b_68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68&l=en-US&s=jzkgNNUIwAtGRF00rlGyYxUPYi4%3D)
---
| [](https://engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=681838523c90c03b7180da39&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mainstreamrevolution.com%2Fblog%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68%26ss_email_id%3D681838523c90c03b7180da39%26ss_campaign_name%3DFinal%2Bchance%2B-%2Baction%2Brequired%2Bre%253A%2BSunset%2BMagazine%2Bproperty%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-05-05T04%253A02%253A31Z&w=6619ff0a31aa07375378c596&c=b_68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68&l=en-US&s=ltqrrMvom_UJEVmNXfHb3zmvnR0%3D)
---
_**Housing Element and RHNA mandate**_ California requires all cities, towns and counties to zone areas for additional housing in a **Housing Element** every 8 years under the **Regional Housing Needs Allocation (RHNA)**. The state’s Department of Finance estimates population growth statewide, then Housing & Community Development (HCD) requires regional governments, such as the Association of Bay Area Governments (ABAG) to allocate additional housing to each city, town and county. _**Transit-oriented housing makes sense**_ Many cities have increased the density and height of housing near public transit corridors like the Caltrain line on the Peninsula between San Francisco and San Jose. Cities are also converting light industrial warehouse areas built in the 50s and 60s into mixed use areas with office space, housing and retail - called “live-work-play” zones. I was the first Mayor to pass a Housing Element for Menlo Park in over 20 years in 2013. We rezoned 16-acres of empty car dealerships along El Camino Real into mixed use including 680 housing units - mostly apartments walkable to the Caltrain station. I also chaired our General Plan update the rezoned over 500 acres of warehouses into a live-work-play mixed use zone with up to 4500 housing units near Facebook’s headquarters.To give cities more flexibility implementing zoning for housing, I partnered with State Assemblyman Rich Gordon to introduce legislation (AB1690, 2014) which passed with overwhelming bipartisan support in Sacramento and was signed into law by Governor Brown._**California’s top-down approach to housing is an unfunded state mandate**_ No funding is provided by the state for the infrastructure to support the additional housing, such as classrooms, water or transportation, so cities require developers to pay impact fees, which increase the cost of housing. Also, the Sustainable Communities Strategy (SB375, 2008) requires housing to be located near jobs - what’s called the **jobs-housing mix** \- to prevent urban sprawl and reduce GHG emissions from car commuting. However, many employees don’t want to live next to their jobs.The Housing Element is a **top-down unfunded mandate that’s a huge burden on California cities - driven by Dept. of Finance projections for continuing population growth. But people are moving out of California!** So why are we aggravating our residents?And then in 2021, Governor Newsom signed into law Senate Bill 9, authored by State Senator Scott Weiner (San Francisco):_**SB9 took away the primary role of city councils to govern land use to quadruple the density of our neighborhoods**_ SB9 forces increased density in residential neighborhoods, by eliminating single-family zoning to allow each lot, regardless of size, to be subdivided into 4 units. SB9 law took away our local zoning control and allows 4 housing units on your neighbor's lot, resulting in overcrowded classrooms and traffic. High-density housing makes sense on transit corridors and near jobs, not in our neighborhoods. So forget about Transit-Oriented Housing or Jobs-Housing Mix, let’s just densify our charming neighborhoods. Who’s going to fund the additional classrooms, transportation and water infrastructure?_**SB9 will erode the American Dream**_ The goal of buying a single family house in a nice neighborhood is a big draw for immigrants like my grandparents - the American Dream. Some council members believe that single family housing is exclusionary. I beg to differ. We must protect our family-friendly neighborhoods. _**Let’s restore Local Control**_ I encourage concerned Peninsula residents to [**support Lydia Kou**](https://engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=681838523c90c03b7180da39&u=https%3A%2F%2Flydiakou.org%2F&w=6619ff0a31aa07375378c596&c=b_68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68&l=en-US&s=bmWk77ULYU4Ouc_BChNAaogs86Y%3D), former Palo Alto mayor and Democrat running to challenge State Assemblyman Marc Berman (who voted for SB9) and Menlo Park City Council candidate **Greg Conlon**. Let’s restore local control over zoning and sensible housing that doesn’t change the character of our communities.
---
[View original post](https://engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=681838523c90c03b7180da39&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mainstreamrevolution.com%2Fblog%2F2024%2Fhousing%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68%26ss_email_id%3D681838523c90c03b7180da39%26ss_campaign_name%3DFinal%2Bchance%2B-%2Baction%2Brequired%2Bre%253A%2BSunset%2BMagazine%2Bproperty%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-05-05T04%253A02%253A31Z&w=6619ff0a31aa07375378c596&c=b_68182511fe3c3e2f5033cf68&l=en-US&s=juu_4TqkNJpQrqVTrEmlIEsEydM%3D)
---

#### **Frozen Odds -**
**Alaska — vast, untamed, and unpredictable. Beneath its snow-covered peaks and icy rivers lies a political battlefield unlike any other. This is the story they didn’t want you to know.****Powerful names — Lisa Murkowski, Hansjörg Wyss, Arabella Advisors, and George Soros — echo through the corridors of influence, shaping the future of the Last Frontier. From the manipulation of Ranked Choice Voting to the backdoor funding of initiatives like the Minimum Wage Increase, Alaska has become a testing ground — a proving ground — for tactics designed to tilt democracy itself.****Groups like Salmon State, Outdoor Alliance, and the Alaska Venture Fund, Alaskans For Better Elections, No on 2, etc. have operated quietly, weaving a web of influence across the state's political landscape. What seemed like grassroots efforts were anything but — and the connections go far deeper than anyone imagined.****This isn't just about Alaska. It’s a warning to the rest of America.****Help bring this story to life, flying experts to Alaska, traveling the state for interviews. We have already raised nearly 40% what we expected for the entire film to cost.****A huge Thank You,****Phil Izon**

name: The Flyover Team
subject: Escape Mainstream Media Spin in Just 15 Minutes
body:
I've got something exciting and new for you: The Flyover Podcast is launching, and I want YOU to join our exclusive focus group.

| | | [](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zg4ndqe9jbgi1t0ag2mrlcf5173h2bt3i1hr7k0o_rp22sh2s8i6eob3c5gm8o31cjh2bs)
---
Friend, Are you fed up with mainstream media outlets like MSNBC, CNN, and The New York Times constantly pushing their agendas instead of giving you the straight facts? We were too—so we did something about it. Introducing [**The Flyover Podcast**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z8d5dlq63qo034eol4m019vh5vs5hedrn82e21o0_rp22sh2s8i6eob3c5gm8o31cjh2bs): your new go-to source for clear, unbiased news delivered straight to your ears. We cut through the noise, skip the spin, and deliver fact-based coverage that empowers **YOU** to decide what to think. Each episode gives you a concise, 15-minute breakdown of the day’s most important stories—without hidden agendas or sensationalism. Break free from biased media. Join us for a refreshing alternative that respects your intelligence and your time. Listen for FREE now to [**The Flyover Podcast**](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zqa1lhhhc3u5i54s825akre7rk0ipqppts18md2o_rp22sh2s8i6eob3c5gm8o31cjh2bs) and reclaim your news experience! subject: He tortured animals. Now he’s impeaching Trump.
body:
A radical leftist Democrat made millions of dollars torturing animals. Now he’s leading the charge to impeach Trump.

| | | | |
---
Fellow patriot,
**Shri Thanedar - an unhinged leftist millionaire and Member of Congress from Michigan - just introduced articles of impeachment against President Trump.**
**_[If that’s all you need to know, please chip in whatever you can to help make sure we can keep saving our country.>>](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aqs8/?__vbtrk=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_**
[DEFEND PRESIDENT TRUMP](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aqs8/?__vbtrk=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aqs8/?__vbtrk=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
_Shri Thanedar_
_Radical Leftist & Animal Abuser_** ____**
Who is Shri Thanedar? Let me tell you about who he is:

Before he got to Congress, he got rich abusing dogs. Yes, really _._
He ran a pharmaceutical lab that**tested experimental drugs on innocent _ beagles_ and _monkeys_.**
When the company collapsed, he abandoned the facility and he**left the dogs locked in cages to either _starve_ or ******_die_ **from the side effects of the drugs being tested on them.******
Former employees of the lab had to climb fences and break back in just to feed them while animal welfare groups sued to try to save the lives of the innocent animals.
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aqs8/?__vbtrk=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**Shri Thanedar and the modern Democrat Party have no policy agenda.** They have no platform. They only want to **_leave the border open_** to flood our country with more would be illegal immigrant Democrat voters, **_obstruct bringing manufacturing and jobs back to America_** , and **_weaponize government power_** against their political enemies.
[DEFEND PRESIDENT TRUMP](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aqs8/?__vbtrk=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
If we let people like Shri Thanedar lead the charge then we aren’t going to have a country anymore. **_[This battle is about stopping the Democrat Party from replacing leadership with vengeance](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aqs8/?__vbtrk=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**
_**[Please stand with us today. Help stop Shri Thanedar and defend President Trump.>>>](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aqs8/?__vbtrk=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg4NDM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**_
_In Strength,_
**House GOP**

name: History Facts
subject: Why red snow fell on Boston in the 1930s
body:
The Dust Bowl wasn’t entirely confined to the actual Dust Bowl states.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc69ojuawmsxs218tojw8hz5mhay0s94rqfl31m1q44317kbdutpuempfnilcmil9ip29ugbpesxy9yd5a90wv1qteup1w08itx7nym2r4a3kusjryurv9awdwp54g6d1szhfq0ppufobfqmgxd&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

# [During the Dust Bowl, some dust clouds reached as far as Boston, causing red snow.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [U.S. History](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| T he Dust Bowl wasn’t entirely confined to the actual Dust Bowl states. Oklahoma, Texas, Kansas, Colorado, and New Mexico were certainly the most affected by the extreme drought that ravaged the Great Plains in the 1930s, a natural disaster that followed overcultivation and proved disastrous for both the land and the people living on it. But some of the [dust storms](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) that resulted were so extreme that their clouds reached cities more than 1,500 miles away on the East Coast. Boston, Massachusetts, even saw [red snow](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) due to red clay soil becoming concentrated in the atmosphere.
---
One of the worst storms hit the Great Plains region on April 14, 1935, which became known as [Black Sunday](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). What started as a sunny morning quickly turned into an oppressive haze that dropped temperatures more than 25 degrees in an hour and turned the sky black. This “black blizzard” displaced an estimated 300,000 tons of topsoil, an agricultural disaster that led to further hardship and a number of casualties. Woody Guthrie immortalized the event in his song [“The Great Dust Storm”](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=2wjxy76qd9q8jnfx5amro6jp0sq09k76yqei0yw9wkyg8ej2jzole0scgih5vo91vcjz5rojfj4v99g8y8f1xble6dg5jgptgdizgsrjms91or2w3vuxo6pxt4w74855skn6fgw5dlonduxgiye2v1xsyr6w9m5zssj5q5k1seqnv4h7opu39a1lj5wj298rn8masafvuxd5a1muzl3ku4ktyapetheb92ojm3ud9armjzjeo8hmrz9g3ilm5thnefy7alpesx049m43bjmrs4nh9nw311&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) from the album _Dust Bowl Ballads_ , which included the line, “It fell across our city like a curtain of black rolled down / We thought it was our judgment, we thought it was our doom.”
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## Most “Okies” weren’t from Oklahoma.
Anyone who’s read [_The Grapes of Wrath_](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) knows that “Okie” was a pejorative term for people fleeing the Dust Bowl — even if they weren’t from Oklahoma. The catchall insult applied equally to folks from Texas, New Mexico, Colorado, Nebraska, and Kansas and could be found on signs such as this one outside a California diner: “Okies and dogs not allowed inside.” Most people who fled the Dust Bowl didn’t venture that far west, though, more often migrating to nearby states in the hopes of restarting their farms in unaffected areas. John Steinbeck’s novel won both the National Book Award and Pulitzer Prize on its way to becoming one of the most celebrated books of the 20th century, but not everyone was pleased with it. [Lyle Boren](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=svsa3k&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com), who represented Oklahoma in Congress, described _The Grapes of Wrath_ as “a lie, a black, infernal creation of a twisted, distorted mind.”

clean_body: The Dust Bowl wasn’t entirely confined to the actual Dust Bowl states. | T he Dust Bowl wasn’t entirely confined to the actual Dust Bowl states. Oklahoma, Texas, Kansas, Colorado, and New Mexico were certainly the most affected by the extreme drought that ravaged the Great Plains in the 1930s, a natural disaster that followed overcultivation and proved disastrous for both the land and the people living on it. But some of the that resulted were so extreme that their clouds reached cities more than 1,500 miles away on the East Coast. Boston, Massachusetts, even saw due to red clay soil becoming concentrated in the atmosphere. One of the worst storms hit the Great Plains region on April 14, 1935, which became known as . What started as a sunny morning quickly turned into an oppressive haze that dropped temperatures more than 25 degrees in an hour and turned the sky black. This “black blizzard” displaced an estimated 300,000 tons of topsoil, an agricultural disaster that led to further hardship and a number of casualties. Woody Guthrie immortalized the event in his song from the album Dust Bowl Ballads , which included the line, “It fell across our city like a curtain of black rolled down / We thought it was our judgment, we thought it was our doom.” | By clicking a link in this email, you're agreeing to our and . Thank you for supporting our advertisers! | By the Numbers | | People who left Dust Bowl states during the Great Depression 2.5 million | | Words in the novel The Grapes of Wrath 169,481 | Words in the novel The Grapes of Wrath 169,481 | | Boston’s population in 1930 781,188 | | Acres of former farmland rendered useless by 1934 35 million | Acres of former farmland rendered useless by 1934 35 million | | | Did you know? ---|---|--- Most “Okies” weren’t from Oklahoma. Anyone who’s read knows that “Okie” was a pejorative term for people fleeing the Dust Bowl — even if they weren’t from Oklahoma. The catchall insult applied equally to folks from Texas, New Mexico, Colorado, Nebraska, and Kansas and could be found on signs such as this one outside a California diner: “Okies and dogs not allowed inside.” Most people who fled the Dust Bowl didn’t venture that far west, though, more often migrating to nearby states in the hopes of restarting their farms in unaffected areas. John Steinbeck’s novel won both the National Book Award and Pulitzer Prize on its way to becoming one of the most celebrated books of the 20th century, but not everyone was pleased with it. , who represented Oklahoma in Congress, described The Grapes of Wrath as “a lie, a black, infernal creation of a twisted, distorted mind.” Recommended Reading | | | ---|---|--- | | | ---|---|--- Thank you for supporting our advertisers! | | Discover something new History Facts is part of Optimism, which publishes content that uplifts, informs, and inspires. | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | ---|--- | ---|--- A daily digest of history's most fascinating happenings. | Want to unsubscribe? | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- Copyright © 2025 History Facts. ## Bond Election Highlights
Voters determined the fate of over [$41 billion in proposed bond spending](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykz7GOtCAUBeCngW4MIAoUFH8z3fztluQqVyWL4sqNM_P2G-cVNjnVycmXnK9HeEDKaZv_w4qeXlMJGz5rRiI8ApVCyw6VMG0nVkozUCrbUYISqruJKyFCyu-gefSDiE5y9NLoXmtlneSL7-0k3aAdgHbamDbC6LQcp6EV0VnZ8-QvTHSik1JIZZuuNQDWOIsqDspJpgXGROVoaEHCF9Qpv8uJRzOWlWe_EO2Vtf-YujN1j0DQfFbNXM6rYOr-Pazj7Wc9Z374uD9Tzqn2hmkxr5Dyx6G_vT-9-g0AAP__X6JufQ) across the state on Saturday. While the Texas Bond Review Board has not yet compiled a final tally of results, there are several notable outcomes from the weekend's elections. In Celina ISD, two bond proposals [totaling nearly $2.3 billion](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2upDAQhE9jMo_sBgYICCZYZ2_TDVEP7oGW_DOym-G926_YK6xUWZVKVd-fr-ULOXDafmOkWb5feUl01kAiVBbJWfY3ViFOH6rCGwrnVPICBnptLi0eOfwsXePnp_GTbWi2Q3fvOhgn2-zzOAC20PUTEhBBP7VE9rkOOPRwN-294fkqM73prTUWxlvfDojjMI0E_gmTVZ0hz5LLTXYS-sb6Cj_5Q-W25tiEeRd5V9U-FDgF7jzPW6zp8hQ4SvqoCtx1SoHLslNR4EC3T_06kue06UhYj0J6Q05VxxwpyRE1J_3CKnor-bxiKwVOqOu65xy05yqFV1HgsOjHw_4aXLc1Zfbvk0Pgeh9UZ7aIHP7NlP-D-5nhbwAAAP__IPmP3g) were approved by about 70% of voters. Celina is among the fastest-growing cities in the country, and the district expects its student enrollment to increase from 5,300 to about 16,000 over the next few years. In nearby Melissa ISD, an $875 million bond package passed with support from more than 65% of voters. ## Earthquake Hits West Texas
A [5.4-magnitude earthquake](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL-OszAQxJ_GdEb2Yv4VLr6GLl97JdrgBawzOGcvIXn7E3mFk7ZYaaSZ-c3XbbyhD35f_uNGll9zHHc6cyBmSiPHyOsDM5Pfn5TZL8g-7imOoKCW6rrRoQ_v0RTO3pXrdUFWt6YxBrpeF6tVDsBNUBnXNTP1FYBW0wx1UzvShrrC28tM1arWWmnoyrpqEbu27wjcHXotjCLnOaaSV2J6YZ7DOz4plVPcimBX5kcW1T8Bg4DhPM9yuueL4tIFDNcrYKilkRsuu-fDkSRMvP4c-E0yIXOgLNORMMgcD17pYk673OmUG738FOVJmeUnXcBQJOsepw_B56YVRi0b-vCpw38b8WnhNwAA__9GZIl8) rattled West Texas on Saturday evening, followed by two aftershocks of magnitudes 2.2 and 2.9. It ranks among the strongest recorded earthquakes in Texas history. The earthquake hit Culberson County at 7:47 p.m., at a depth of 3.9 miles. Within two hours, the U.S. Geological Survey received nearly 1,500 reports from people who felt the earthquake, some as [far away as Amarillo](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1ygzAQhJ9GdGGkM38qVKShc9qUzNk64MayhNEZm7fPkC51ZrbZLfab-b7Pwxk5cJy-8E5O3mMaIr1yIBFaB0lJ5gWzEMeNsvCEwimuaQAN9Yc-MnjksA9V4d1Fe2sKcqatmqqCzppidjDqxvhqBHtpTp2_ttjUaNu2M6MdLUHB7jjTta6N0Qa6sj61iF1rOwJ_AWtUpcmzpLWUmYTemMewp43W8pruRXCzyJLV6VNBr6AnXGV-PPFG5TNPuZzS9mfNR9soyoITKejlfeD58bop6P0-soKeo1DMLHuxOr-8OATOTasqPd2Rwy9W_idrc_ATAAD__56SgAQ). A 5.0-magnitude earthquake struck the same area on Feb. 14, and more than half a dozen earthquakes of similar intensity have hit the region in the past five years. Alexandros Savvaidis, a research professor at UT’s Bureau of Economic Geology, attributed the seismic activity to fault lines in the region and the [prevalence of wastewater injection wells](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUD3OozAQPY3pjGxjgilcpFi0TbbdEg14ACvGztqTkNx-Ra7wSVOM3pPe39_beAMffFz_wI6W3ksaIx4lIBHmkVKi7QGF0McXFvIrkE8xp1EJ1XJx3ujAh8-oK2cn4XpZoZWdvmitTC-rzS7t3EzNMhkwpp9kc9FCzGLpwLjJwOwqb08x0YpWSiGVqdumAzBdb1C5SfWSaYHOU8o1bUj4hrKET3phrue0V8FuRI_CmitTA1PDcRz1XuLJMTVg5M_C1HCWYmr4_jus0dPTIW-55giZtn9PuCMvG9yx8AML8a8Phz3FlRfKKa4n6iMvBIR884VS_jA1QObXq_zV_Y5dla17HD4EXy4d02LdwYdvSPrZtC-r_gcAAP__Q0OQVg).
## Paxton Investigating Toothpaste Companies
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is [investigating two major toothpaste manufacturers](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uhDAQhJ_GdCB7sfkpXKShu7Qp0d6xHKsYG-HNcff2EdfkASJNOfpG831dxgty4Hj_xJW8POc0RjpyIBHaR0lJlg2zEMcHZeE7Cqe4pxE0uFKfGSfk8BptMfmrnnpTkDetbayFrjfF4q1tZksaCFsHza12hkw9I2l0Wpu-LtifMO20M0Yb6CpXt4hd23cE0xV6o6ymiSXtlSwk9MQ8h1d60F7d0loEv4hsWdUfCgYFw3Ec1frKGDFKipzOkoLh-pM5Us4KBtyFb4EUDH__yje3_KZYbviUFEvQtXbQ9dW2bMXup-3gEDg3rbL6viKH97r8z9nDw28AAAD__9JigfU) over what he called “misleading, deceptive, and dangerous” marketing practices. Paxton is [requesting records](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO2zAMhp_G3hhIinSWBw1dvF3XjgZjMTZxsmRIbC55-8IBOnQuQHAh-YPf9-tz_kROnNefuFOQ573Mmb5bIhGqs5Qi24FNiPODmvCKwiXXMhtlHKiz5oicXrPtY7ipOOqegh7sh7XGj7rfAi5eE_qbu_tx8XbQymvv1H1wzl4X5XoOZ5hyymmttPEXdx0Q_TB6MvFmRt1ZRZGl1ItsJPTEdk-v8qB6Wcrep7CJHK27_ujM1JlpKyUmznQOOzOd2Z2Z1Nnet4AipWZ6wUqZKib4ogwHPqVk4NZ-U4N_aCHSjjk2kAJLSSsKwYFpL4kfBAescP4Cy8YpVspwLv7VBjvWLxLOa2emvoZ4fHNK3D6Gzqp1R05vBvk_849g_gQAAP__xgicIA) from the makers of Colgate and Crest toothpastes, whom he accused of producing ads and using flavors that may encourage overuse among children, potentially increasing their fluoride exposure. Paxton cited a U.S. government analysis published last August showing a [purported link](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuO6zAMRVcjdwok-l-oeI27vHZKg7Zpi4AiBRInn90PnGYWMADBhsA5vPfrOl-RA8fjP97IyWtPc6RnCSRCeZaUxN-xCHF8UBE-UDjFnGYw0Gpzzrwhh_fcVJtbzDbaipztm65pYBht5V1d1wNYAGxwwQ7Htmt3QqB-6VbbLbZid8JMa1prjYXh0tY94tCPA8G2wGhVY2hjSfkinoReWPbwTg_KlzXdquC8yL2o-p-CScGEuQitPvKK513B5AmDeAXT6VEwmXN9OPpIVDTuQln_ZtUcNZUVAwrHQ-98eNGnT-_hO2XeSMFUZbfdnxwCl65XjTluyOHzkPytxoeDnwAAAP__RfOIWw) between fluoride exposure and lower IQ among children. However, critics have suggested that the underlying study was poorly designed and lacked transparency. The investigation comes amid [growing concern](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOpTAMhk8TOlBiEghFim3SzbZbooQYiBQIIp7HcPsVr3kHGMmyXFif_X__vsYvF1Pcl79uQ0M_cx53vEpCIjxHypnWwxXCuL-wUFwcxbyfeQQOquZPjcHFdI-yCsbzMIgKjehlJyXoQVSrUY77rlXBKzWh172clG_1LPzgOxcmrKJ5YFxxJQQXoBvV9s7pftAIwcMgmOQYIuWzoRUJf1yZ051feDZT3qpkVqKjsPYPA8vAXtfVfBdHObj7WWBgC-XzZmCfZAzskVOkOD3jc5mB5e8mGNg3vnZL_QmM9Zy-8xkD1h8f9ZS3w-0RH4puW80ltJz3DGx1mnBcMaVYup5Jvmwupver9DvBLwP_AwAA__9wp4-_) about the health effects of fluoride in public water systems. In March, Utah became the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, and Florida is poised to become the second.

clean_body: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is over what he called “misleading, deceptive, and dangerous” marketing practices. | | | | | | Monday, May 5, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1939, the near Fort Davis was dedicated. The project was funded by an $850,000 gift to the University of Texas from the estate of William Johnson McDonald, a banker from Paris, Texas. The Flyover Podcast —have you listened to our national podcast yet? If you haven't, you are really missing out. It's a quick, new way to get the same no-spin, common-sense news you trust from the Texas Flyover , now in audio and video. It's your daily companion for the top national headlines shaping the country—fast, clear, and bias-free. Whether you're driving, working out, or just catching up in the morning, it’s built for common-sense Americans like you. Click to listen or to watch. And please let us know what you think in the comment sections. We're sponsored today by , offering an investment opportunity in environmentally friendly plastic technology from Japan. Tune in to The Flyover Podcast! New episodes drop daily—watch on YouTube or listen on Spotify and Apple Podcasts, all in under 15 minutes. | | Bond Election Highlights Voters determined the fate of over across the state on Saturday. While the Texas Bond Review Board has not yet compiled a final tally of results, there are several notable outcomes from the weekend's elections. In Celina ISD, two bond proposals were approved by about 70% of voters. Celina is among the fastest-growing cities in the country, and the district expects its student enrollment to increase from 5,300 to about 16,000 over the next few years. In nearby Melissa ISD, an $875 million bond package passed with support from more than 65% of voters. In Hays CISD, totaling nearly $1 billion were approved, while nearly 70% of Bexar County voters approved a for the local community college district. Voters in and rejected community college bonds of $450 million and $325 million, respectively. Voters in and , on the other hand, approved school district bond proposals of $397 million and $290 million. Earthquake Hits West Texas A rattled West Texas on Saturday evening, followed by two aftershocks of magnitudes 2.2 and 2.9. It ranks among the strongest recorded earthquakes in Texas history. The earthquake hit Culberson County at 7:47 p.m., at a depth of 3.9 miles. Within two hours, the U.S. Geological Survey received nearly 1,500 reports from people who felt the earthquake, some as . A 5.0-magnitude earthquake struck the same area on Feb. 14, and more than half a dozen earthquakes of similar intensity have hit the region in the past five years. Alexandros Savvaidis, a research professor at UT’s Bureau of Economic Geology, attributed the seismic activity to fault lines in the region and the . Paxton Investigating Toothpaste Companies Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is over what he called “misleading, deceptive, and dangerous” marketing practices. Paxton is from the makers of Colgate and Crest toothpastes, whom he accused of producing ads and using flavors that may encourage overuse among children, potentially increasing their fluoride exposure. Paxton cited a U.S. government analysis published last August showing a between fluoride exposure and lower IQ among children. However, critics have suggested that the underlying study was poorly designed and lacked transparency. The investigation comes amid - about the health effects of fluoride in public water systems. In March, Utah became the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, and Florida is poised to become the second. | Flying together with our sponsor Japanese Water Soluble Plastic Has American Competition Japan’s RIKEN institute recently unveiled a plastic that dissolves in seawater within hours — a breakthrough in the fight against pollution. But Florida-based may have quietly surpassed it. With seven patents and a proprietary process called “molecular disintegration,” not just in seawater, but also in freshwater, rain, humidity, and soil. Unlike traditional solutions, these materials leave no microplastics behind and maintain the strength of conventional plastics. Already used in 3D-printing and flexible composites, Timeplast’s lineup is versatile and scalable. They’re on a mission to revolutionize multiple industries: Packaging 3D Printing Healthcare Agriculture Consumer Goods Marine Construction Retail & Events As you can see, the market potential is enormous ($1.3T in total). That’s just one reason why they’ve already attracted over 6,000 investors. This is your opportunity to join Timeplast as they lead a plastic revolution with global implications. | ➤ San Antonio: Former Under Secretary of the Air Force Gina Ortiz Jones and former Texas Secretary of State Rolando Pablos were the top two vote-getters in a field of 27 mayoral candidates. The runoff election is June 7. () ➤ Houston: At least one person was killed and as many as 14 others were shot at a house party early Sunday. The violence reportedly began when an uninvited guest was asked to leave. () ➤ Falls County: Gov. Greg Abbott declared a local state of emergency amid ongoing water quality concerns in the city of Marlin. Mayor Susan Byrd said she hopes the city can be approved for a portable water treatment plant. () ➤ College Station: State Rep. Brian Harrison (R-Midlothian) has asked the Department of Justice to investigate Texas A&M University over its “DEI courses and practices,” arguing they are unconstitutional and violate Supreme Court precedent and recent executive orders. () ➤ Austin: The Texas Legislature passed a law removing legal protections for adults who share sexually explicit or obscene material with children. Senate Bill 412 eliminates the “affirmative defense” that allowed defendants to claim they were providing educational instruction. () ➤ Harris County: District Attorney Sean Teare dismissed corruption charges against a former county official accused of directing contracts to favored companies in exchange for payment. Teare and County Judge Lina Hidalgo have characterized the charges as a political smear by the former district attorney. Millions of Americans feel like no matter how many stretches, supplements, or pills they take... their achy joints just never seem to get better.
Most doctors blame old injuries, poor genetics, and more often than not — just "getting older."
However, breakthrough Harvard research recently discovered something shocking:

And the worst “health food” of all of them is actually one extremely common type of protein that's likely in your refrigerator right now.
Researchers say this protein can actually trigger a vicious cycle of out-of-control inflammation in your joints, and even harm your immune system.

subject: Trump is eliminating Veterans Day. For real. Don't let him do it
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Sign: Demand Trump Scrap His Terrible Plan to Get Rid of Veterans Day](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tab010a08-7bc5-45d3-b6ba-49029eaedda8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T46fca976-65f8-4824-b5dd-88f194602649/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | **Donald Trump is getting rid of Veterans Day**. [For real](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf219fafa-6b35-41e8-bb06-6e9deb809ec8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9). It's a stunning slap in the face to tens of millions of Americans who have served their country in the military. Instead, he is replacing it with a day celebrating America's victory in World War One, because it's apparently more important to tell the Kaiser to "suck it" than to honor and thank those who put their lives on the line to defend us in every other war. Like the horribly inappropriate [mandatory march for his birthday](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4c451442-82c6-4b4a-af6d-875acdac7232/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9), it's yet more proof that Donald Trump couldn't give a rat's ass about our troops. Don't let him get away with it. [_**Add your name: Demand Trump scrap his plan to get rid of Veterans Day**_](https://default.salsalabs.org/T87600c63-b689-45b6-881f-54ead4151b2f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

Instead, he is replacing it with a day celebrating America's victory in World War One, because it's apparently more important to tell the Kaiser to "suck it" than to honor and thank those who put their lives on the line to defend us in every other war. Like the horribly inappropriate , it's yet more proof that Donald Trump couldn't give a rat's ass about our troops. name: Broad + Liberty Staff
subject: Good news for Philly collar counties
body:
Major initiative launched in Bucks County | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/431fd27dfbb7/broad-liberty-and-politicspa-announce-new-strategic-partnership-for-5858938?e=b7b3eddce2)
---
| | | | [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=d52e21ba7e&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
# **_Broad \+ Liberty_ Launches New Regional Bureau for Bucks County**
---
| | | | | You want facts, not fluff. This is _**your**_ kind of local news. Visit the new _Bucks County Independence_ website [here](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=eed516c9be&e=b7b3eddce2).
---
|
---
| Dear Readers,

Wherever we Americans come from, we tend to see our home as unlike anywhere else. This is often hyperbole, but not in Bucks County. It has been exceptional for as long as our nation has existed. Longer, even.

Founding Bucks in 1682, William Penn named it after Buckinghamshire, his family’s English home county. On Christmas of 1776, General George Washington assembled his troops in what is now Upper Makefield Township and led them across the Delaware River to attack Hessian outposts in Trenton.

Two and a half centuries later, Bucks remains nationally consequential.

During last year’s presidential campaign, The Washington Post presciently called it “the biggest swing spot” in Pennsylvania. _The Washington Times_ dubbed it “ground zero in a must-win battleground state.” Last November, it became the only southeastern Pennsylvania county to back a Republican presidential candidate in 12 years, despite experiencing a blue wave on the local level the previous year. No one knows its political future for certain.

This unique place merits a media outlet dedicated to covering its affairs vigilantly, factually, and thoughtfully, free of tired journalistic prepossessions. We at _Broad + Liberty_ and WBCB 1490 are thus launching The Independence to bring you a sharp source of news and commentary relevant to citizens from Bensalem to Riegelsville.

A project this ambitious demands the the best editorial team in our region to deliver this coverage. With our editors’ and correspondents’ many combined decades of experience and manifold accomplishments, we confidently offer just that. Expanding on our earlier efforts, we present a web publication that values freethinking, patriotism, and integrity — made especially for the 646,000 people who call this beauteous corner of Pennsylvania home.

In this era of coarseness and razzle-dazzle, it’s also worth noting what we will not feature: sensationalism or intemperance for their own sake. Too much shrill, radical noise rings through the media atmosphere already. Bucks Countians deserve clarity, not distortion.

But while being gracious, we will not be shy. _Broad + Liberty_ readers and WBCB listeners know our record as watchdogs. We will continue to call out folly and corruption on the part of area officials and institutions wherever we find them.

As we journey through this extraordinary place, we hope you’ll join us.
With regards, **Terry Tracy** — CEO | Broad + Liberty
**Brad Vasoli** — Senior Editor | The Bucks County Independence
---
|
---
| | | |
---
| _Copyright © 2024 Broad + Liberty, All rights reserved._
You're getting Broad + Liberty updates due to your interest in our site -- and stories for free thinkers in the Philly region and beyond.

**Our mailing address is:**
Broad + Liberty
323 West Front Street
Suite 200
Media, Pa 19063

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/profile?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a3b749423c&e=b7b3eddce2&c=fb7556dbb0) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a3b749423c&t=b&e=b7b3eddce2&c=fb7556dbb0).

body:
People ask me all the time what the future of Independent politics looks like in the United States. Recently, I've been responding with two words: "Mike Duggan."

# [Mike Duggan](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=162627208&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyNjI3MjA4LCJpYXQiOjE3NDY0NDY2NDgsImV4cCI6MTc0OTAzODY0OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.kRhll7eZNUN5D4M3J6fQJNXzVoTmAtp9LC4foRYxb5M)
