name: Chip Roy
subject: I’m not going to lie to you, Peter.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chip Roy for Congress
body:
| | | [](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/WMkZE4tvYvVNIHlRXYFBAZpK340Fc3HTTeiLIotYZNogufxlo_69wagF7Ev2hsCQbo2UBzHpqblw87v0UY7fy0e4HGe160xSq6mTqcFTyOzVNxTS4kC_zE-W3BnDEBnU88y5PD3PkLR0IaAzJjSnDUB9OYkxFknsX_46Vbs4EXQAXsTqwnIKqRjNqgAGgZNYqvqNvJBRHr5QAEOTdDKtq0SgjOEzrQVbsZDdVn2YGzZOyjMs7fuu31BkrniwV4XDPluHKCKJQwsnuSwRGZ98k5DwhXbqrvQD4S2LtHs7-AezxkswT3iOXj4He90mhJJQJ1Jte2rTrPLVlo0VQ89h3VJIo4IeAUMlRA2Sw2m6kwnD5JWQaiLsUgbYxPnIPJMep384WWMxvBo2ngyePtCIAlce0_dX7hPvRiW8ed9g8F7EJzO5QU9rTRdGhiBXLxjeoX6gpjebfS_sshAzVX5z9mKJeJ2EVV_H0uJ7Tyd7DhUoigde3wxnGNc/O2KXE0AVxkGCSKv81FV6IvbQfW17e82A/8)
---
** ****I’m pissed, Peter.** ** ** You’re bombarded every single day — texts, emails, pop-ups — all begging you to take a survey, sign a petition, or chip in a few bucks to “stop the Democrats.” ** ** And what actually happens, Peter? **NOTHING.**** ** The same RINO cowards fold like cheap suits. The spending gets worse. The lies keep piling up. And you — the one actually footing the bill — are left betrayed and ignored.** ** They take your hard-earned dollars and then turn around and vote for the same damn policies that wrecked your savings, crushed your community, and left you wondering if _anyone_ in D.C. actually gives a damn about you. ** **_**You want the truth?**_** ** When they scam you out of a donation, they pat themselves on the back, head to some fancy cocktail party, and LAUGH at you behind closed doors. **They don’t respect you. They don’t fear you. And they sure as hell don’t represent you.** I’ve had it. With the lies. The games. The sellouts. # ACTION ALERT: VOTE NO ON HB 1787
|
---
HB 1787 by Representative Donna Howard would require the state health department to promote the HPV vaccine - a liability-free pharmaceutical product - on college campuses. **TAKE ACTION NOW BY URGING MEMBERS OF THE HOUSE CALENDARS COMMITTEE TO SUPPORT TO OPPOSE HB 1787!**
**1- Email the Committee Members**

|
---
| | [ Click here to generate an email to all committee members! ](mailto:todd.hunter@house.texas.gov,toni.rose@house.texas.gov,terry.canales@house.texas.gov,stan.gerdes@house.texas.gov,cody.harris@house.texas.gov,ana.hernandez@house.texas.gov,ann.johnson@house.texas.gov,jeff.leach@house.texas.gov,janie.lopez@house.texas.gov,ramon.romero@house.texas.gov,carl.tepper@house.texas.gov?subject=Vote%20NO%20on%20HB%201787&body=Dear%20Representative%2C%0A%0AI%20am%20writing%20to%20you%20today%20to%20ask%20you%20to%20please%20keep%20HB%201787%20from%20advancing%20to%20the%20House%20floor.%0A%0AThis%20bill%20would%20focus%20on%20the%20taxpayer-funded%20promotion%20of%20a%20for-profit%2C%20liability-free%20pharmaceutical%20product%20as%20an%20HPV%20prevention%20strategy%20rather%20than%20providing%20education%20about%20detection%2C%20which%20is%20the%20best%20way%20to%20avoid%20cervical%20cancer.%0A%0AThank%20you%2C%0A%5BYOUR%20NAME%5D)
---
|

___The button above_ _will populate with a sample message. You are welcome to use it as is_** _by signing your name._**
_If this button does not work on your device, please copy and paste all of the emails below in the TO: field of your draft to send just one email to all committee members at once:_____[](mailto:gary.vandeaver@house.texas.gov)[todd.hunter@house.texas.gov](mailto:todd.hunter@house.texas.gov); [toni.rose@house.texas.gov](mailto:toni.rose@house.texas.gov); [terry.canales@house.texas.gov](mailto:terry.canales@house.texas.gov); [stan.gerdes@house.texas.gov](mailto:stan.gerdes@house.texas.gov); [cody.harris@house.texas.gov](mailto:cody.harris@house.texas.gov); [ana.hernandez@house.texas.gov](mailto:ana.hernandez@house.texas.gov); [ann.johnson@house.texas.gov](mailto:ann.johnson@house.texas.gov); [jeff.leach@house.texas.gov](mailto:jeff.leach@house.texas.gov); [janie.lopez@house.texas.gov](mailto:janie.lopez@house.texas.gov); [ramon.romero@house.texas.gov](mailto:ramon.romero@house.texas.gov); [carl.tepper@house.texas.gov](mailto:carl.tepper@house.texas.gov)[](mailto:lauren.simmons@house.texas.gov) [](mailto:lauren.simmons@house.texas.gov)_[](mailto:kevin.sparks@senate.texas.gov)_**SAMPLE EMAIL MESSAGE:
SUBJECT: Vote NO on HB 1787**
Dear Representative, I am writing to you today to ask you to please keep HB 1787 from advancing to the House floor.

name: KamalaHarris.com
subject: Tell Congress: NO military parade for Trump’s birthday
body:
Last week, news broke that Donald Trump was planning to hold a military parade - on his birthday - through the streets of Washington, D.C.
# [](https://act.kamalaharris.com/go/2780?t=1&akid=561%2E2563979%2EgHRFC4)

Last week, news broke that Donald Trump was planning to hold a military parade -- on his birthday -- through the streets of Washington, D.C.
Here is the truth:
This parade would not be a national celebration of service -- it would be a shameful celebration of one man's insatiable ego.
But also, this is a parade that would cost tens of millions of dollars according to news reports, and at a time when Trump and Elon Musk are looking to cut veterans health care and housing benefits to pay for tax cuts for billionaires.
It's a disgrace.
This isn't the first time Trump has wanted a military parade for himself. During his first term he tried and tried again. But each time he was stopped because of public outcry.
So here's what we want to do:
[**Please sign our petition telling Congress you OPPOSE any effort to hold a military parade honoring Donald Trump in Washington, D.C. and that you OPPOSE any effort to cut veterans' health care and housing benefits.**](https://act.kamalaharris.com/sign/Oppose_militaqry_Parade//?source=em250505&refcode2=561.2563979.gHRFC4&akid=561%2E2563979%2EgHRFC4)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.kamalaharris.com/sign/Oppose_militaqry_Parade//?source=em250505&refcode2=561.2563979.gHRFC4&akid=561%2E2563979%2EgHRFC4)**
The troops have no voice in this fight -- they have to follow their orders. We are their voice in this fight.
Thank you for adding your name.
-- Team Kamala
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.kamalaharris.com/sign/Oppose_militaqry_Parade//?source=em250505&refcode2=561.2563979.gHRFC4&akid=561%2E2563979%2EgHRFC4)**

Paid for by Harris Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Harris for President, the Democratic National Committee and the State Democratic Parties in these states: AK, AL, AR, AZ, CA, CO, CT, DC, DE, FL, GA, HI, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MA, MD, ME, MI, MN, MO, MS, MT, NC, ND, NE, NH, NJ, NM, NV, NY, OH, OK, OR, PA, RI, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT, VA, VT, WA, WI, WV, and WY.
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. Thank you so much for being a supporter. But we don't want to bother you. If you’d like to unsubscribe, you can **[click here.](https://harris.actionkit.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=2&akid=561%2E2563979%2EgHRFC4)**

subject: Bernie Sanders is LEADING the fight >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
You need to see this:
| **🚨 BREAKING: DEMOCRATS FIGHT BACK! 🚨** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89814?t=6&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF) Bernie Sanders is LEADING the charge to take on Trump and save our Democracy. We’re _THRILLED!_

**Trump and MAGA Republicans are already attacking him relentlessly, so we’re sending Sen. Bernie Sanders a MASSIVE letter thanking him for all his amazing work!**

[**You’ve been PERSONALLY selected to sign, friend, so please don’t let us down: Add your name to thank Senator Sanders now! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89814?t=7&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF) | [**Donate $3 to sign the card now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89814?t=8&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF)
---
TO: Sen. Bernie Sanders
FROM: A proud Democrat
Subject: Thank you. **Dear Senator Bernie Sanders,**

__Thank you for all your hard work leading the Anti-Trump resistance!__

It’s easy to feel helpless with MAGA Republicans in charge of the House, Senate, and White House, but you keep us hopeful that we can fight back.

**I know it isn’t easy fighting off Trump and the GOP’s escalating threats to take you down, so please know that Democrats _across the country_ stand with you.**

Your leadership is absolutely critical right now, and we hope more Democrats follow your lead.

[**I can’t thank you enough for all of your work to stop Donald Trump and save our Democracy! So I’m signing this letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show my gratitude and to keep electing incredible Democrats who will save our democracy from MAGA extremists like Donald Trump. »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89814?t=9&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89814?t=10&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=19753%2E878179%2ErD7fZF)

---
|
Team,

As you know, the first 100 days of Trump’s second term has been a _dumpster fire.

_ But just like we saw with the January 6 insurrection, **_when things go bad for MAGA, it only makes them more dangerous:_****
****
**
---
| | [ ](https://rpprrv.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEzMjg2NTcsXCJsXCI6MTUzNTgxODI3NDgwNjE4Nzc2LFwiclwiOjE1MzU4MTg2NjU2MzE0Mjc4Mn0iLCJzIjoiMjZmYTVmMzBiNmM0N2QwMSJ9)
---
| ******
** It's the same old playbook. When MAGA’s policies are exposed for being harmful to working-class Americans - **they fan the flames of hate instead** \- _aiming to sow division between neighbors, families, coworkers, and communities.

_**But hateful rhetoric like Marjorie Taylor Greene’s makes the work our canvassers do even more dangerous** \- and we need to prepare our staff on the ground for potential acts of political violence while they organize deep in GOP territory.

In the FL special elections, **National Ground Game PAC did the work the Democratic Party never does** \- _going door to door in deep red communities talking to Republican leaning neighbors and convincing them to flip their vote blue._ Even then, our canvassers had several serious incidents- and we suspect it may only get worse.

Here are ways we keep our canvassers safe:

* Facilitating transportation to and from our turfs with Ubers and chartered vans so that our staff always has a way to leave threatening situations.

* Professional security at our larger events.

* Train our staff on best practices to stay safe while knocking on doors and how to deescalate in dangerous situations.

[Can we count on you to donate to help train and equip our canvassers to be safe while knocking doors in red districts?
](https://rpprrv.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEzMjg2NTcsXCJsXCI6MTUzNTgxODI3NDg2OTEwMjM1LFwiclwiOjE1MzU4MTg2NjU2MzE0Mjc4Mn0iLCJzIjoiYjU1NWM5ZGEyZGE2ODhiNSJ9)

clean_body: | | Hateful rhetoric like Marjorie Taylor Greene’s makes the work our canvassers do even more dangerous - and we need to prepare our staff on the ground for potential acts of political violence while they organize in GOP territory. | | | | | Team, As you know, the first 100 days of Trump’s second term has been a dumpster fire. But just like we saw with the January 6 insurrection, when things go bad for MAGA, it only makes them more dangerous: | | | **** It's the same old playbook. When MAGA’s policies are exposed for being harmful to working-class Americans - they fan the flames of hate instead \- aiming to sow division between neighbors, families, coworkers, and communities. But hateful rhetoric like Marjorie Taylor Greene’s makes the work our canvassers do even more dangerous \- and we need to prepare our staff on the ground for potential acts of political violence while they organize deep in GOP territory. In the FL special elections, National Ground Game PAC did the work the Democratic Party never does \- going door to door in deep red communities talking to Republican leaning neighbors and convincing them to flip their vote blue. Even then, our canvassers had several serious incidents- and we suspect it may only get worse. Here are ways we keep our canvassers safe: Facilitating transportation to and from our turfs with Ubers and chartered vans so that our staff always has a way to leave threatening situations. Professional security at our larger events. Train our staff on best practices to stay safe while knocking on doors and how to deescalate in dangerous situations. | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | __ | We won’t be scared away by MAGA. name: Team Yass
subject: Yet another useful tool the Trump administration is putting on the chopping block
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/79097/images/YA%20-%20Congress%20-%20Logo%20-%20Primary%201%20%5BRGB%5D_Full_Color_Coral.png]
Over 175 Democrat Members of Congress ‘Demand’ Trump Administration Preserve and Expand Free Tax Filing Program
— Sierra Sun Times, April 2025
Direct File is a free, easy-to-use tax filing program that has already delivered significant benefits to taxpayers. In 2024, during the program’s pilot phase, Direct File saved the average user $160 in tax return fees and hours of effort preparing their return. Users overwhelmingly love the program: 98 percent of Direct File taxpayers in 2025 were “satisfied” or “very satisfied” with their experience, a world-class figure.
Yet, new reporting indicates that the Trump administration “plans to eliminate the IRS’ Direct File program.”

Peter — The IRS’ Direct File program was historic and made it easier for people nationwide to file their taxes.

Every year, American taxpayers spend millions of dollars just to have their taxes prepared, leading to chaos and confusion around tax season. This program saved people hundreds of dollars individually, and 98% of Direct File users indicated they were satisfied with the program.

Yet the Trump administration has put this program on the chopping block.

That’s why Yassamin joined with more than 175 of her colleagues to demand that the Trump administration protect this program that allows people to save on their tax filing.

Will you stand with Yassamin and House Democrats and add your name right now? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/PeIhUYdlIEiplPG-9F-Vzg2]

SIGN ON [https://secure.ngpvan.com/PeIhUYdlIEiplPG-9F-Vzg2]

Direct File works for American taxpayers, Peter, and it’s up to us to provide seamless and innovative solutions to this time-consuming and complicated process.

Thanks for your support,

Team Yass
Yassamin Ansari for Congress
PO Box 13524
Phoenix, AZ 85002
United States

name: Austin Davis
subject: In order to serve, you have to understand
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Austin Davis
body:
Austin Davis is helping continue the progress we've made for Pennsylvanians by ensuring we keep Pennsylvania blue up and down the ballot. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/XrGKMtCcbE2djJaqLq6C9g2?unsubscribedata=kM0Qme7L4dnY%2B9gqnilzu%2BsjA3XWywkYf05smOejjLXSLj8SIMVKGbnPU49XOmPfJVE2R%2FwDdOszcU%2Fh0VdXptrLmN26VyFvToZMp5UTvMLmCsqZcfe4SKflI0SaBUir26G6GED1zSju9otOFQxIXYptG%2BGp5ugi4QN2lhQgHfWlij0ik%2FGOEcq%2BNqK6Jz0tFhSWqs56qeg4kTbC2OvbMN3mg8wZFEdM15BUQ2H%2F0RJbcIONnyI00ehAld8Duq3nDs4QCp%2B8%2B274nRijbHh%2FLX9IoOghe2fTOoEfqJdnBZgV5aW9UueYaLwfAyPaKo0LsxOw0ussjeKOGyr9dOMiSDdge9FEpeY4%2Fe6KJ%2FkYsvQchp8xliS%2FHtjj0yfKYc36wVQoLuKyVPm%2FVU0c2QqcyUPajwJRNcbsx%2FMGd2DBhW9qYkSr3EDxWHHHWR5nWDOi)

| | Peter, I have a saying: _The people closest to the pain, should be the closest to the power._ **I believe that in order to serve, you have to understand.** My mother was a hairdresser and my father was a union bus driver, but growing up, I watched both of my hard-working parents struggle to balance bills and raise kids. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107136334/543707273/627196029?amount=10&source=SBD_AD_EM_FR_050525_LIVEDIT_ATO_D1_S1_RES2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NzIyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MjllZmRhZi1kOTI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=roOkr0OBZLps_aYZQv26Z_FqCpjHSjoly7wLIH_1_mc=) **I know the struggle that the working class is facing because, Peter, I lived it.** And I don’t know about you, but I’m tired of billionaires who don’t know the first thing about economic insecurity telling us how to fix the problems our communities are facing. Throughout my career, I’ve fought to lift Pennsylvania’s working families out of poverty, and I know that Trump’s “plan” would be a disaster. Between his tariffs and DOGE’s reckless cuts to key government programming, the situation is dire. If the MAGA administration _actually cared_ about working families, they’d be **investing in public schools** , not dismantling the Department of Education. They’d be **defending Social Security** , not threatening to cut it. They’d be **supporting the working class** , not leaving them in the dust to fund tax breaks for the ultra-rich. **[Peter, I need your help. It’s up to us to keep standing up to Trump’s administration and fighting for Pennsylvania. By chipping in even just $10, you can make a difference in the strength of our message. Can you make a contribution today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107136336/543707277/627196029?amount=10&source=SBD_AD_EM_FR_050525_LIVEDIT_ATO_D1_S1_RES2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NzIyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MjllZmRhZi1kOTI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=roOkr0OBZLps_aYZQv26Z_FqCpjHSjoly7wLIH_1_mc=)** | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

clean_body: Austin Davis is helping continue the progress we've made for Pennsylvanians by ensuring we keep Pennsylvania blue up and down the ballot. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Peter, I have a saying: The people closest to the pain, should be the closest to the power. I believe that in order to serve, you have to understand. My mother was a hairdresser and my father was a union bus driver, but growing up, I watched both of my hard-working parents struggle to balance bills and raise kids. = I know the struggle that the working class is facing because, Peter, I lived it. And I don’t know about you, but I’m tired of billionaires who don’t know the first thing about economic insecurity telling us how to fix the problems our communities are facing. Throughout my career, I’ve fought to lift Pennsylvania’s working families out of poverty, and I know that Trump’s “plan” would be a disaster. Between his tariffs and DOGE’s reckless cuts to key government programming, the situation is dire. If the MAGA administration actually cared about working families, they’d be investing in public schools , not dismantling the Department of Education. They’d be defending Social Security , not threatening to cut it. They’d be supporting the working class , not leaving them in the dust to fund tax breaks for the ultra-rich. = | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you, Austin Davis * | | | ---|---|--- | get involved to support austin davis for PA Lieutenant Governor While serving as Lieutenant Governor, Austin Davis helped advance some of the most progressive changes the Commonwealth has seen in decades. Now, he's helping continue the progress we've made for Pennsylvanians by ensuring we keep Pennsylvania blue up and down the ballot. name: Voter Action Project
subject: Mobilizing to stop federal suppression
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=]
The Senate is standing up to stop the SAVE Act in its tracks, folks.
Defenders of voting rights know that non-citizen voting is already illegal, extremely rare, and that this bill is written in a way that could disenfranchise millions of eligible women across the country. Here's Colorado Senator Michael Bennet:
https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/SAVE_MB.jpg [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/SAVE_MB.jpg]
Opponents of voting rights in Congress -- and in the White House -- favor this bill because they know it could suppress millions of eligible voters, changing the composition of the electorate and potentially empowering them and their party for years to come.
Please, Team, join these brave Senators today. Fuel our efforts to stop this dangerous legislation with $25 or whatever you can and ensure millions of women's voices continue to be heard in our elections >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2]
If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=15.00&tknfa=pRhrBGUu7w_FYUSAJjqpZ6As3BtFDFPptcu4rDm9aLI=&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=25.00&tknfa=pRhrBGUu7w_FYUSAJjqpZ6As3BtFDFPptcu4rDm9aLI=&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=50.00&tknfa=pRhrBGUu7w_FYUSAJjqpZ6As3BtFDFPptcu4rDm9aLI=&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=100.00&tknfa=pRhrBGUu7w_FYUSAJjqpZ6As3BtFDFPptcu4rDm9aLI=&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=250.00&tknfa=pRhrBGUu7w_FYUSAJjqpZ6As3BtFDFPptcu4rDm9aLI=&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2?am=20.00&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=]
We know the SAVE Act was fast-tracked by opponents of voting rights to be in place before the next election, folks. This bill is a stunning assault on our fundamental right to vote, and we can not afford to let this anti-democratic legislation become law.
If you missed it: This bill would force every voter to present proof of citizenship to register, either a passport or a birth certificate that matches their current first and last name -- something millions of eligible American women don’t have. And that’s the point.
The bill would also obliterate popular methods of voter registration for all voters, including:
* Registration by mail

* 3rd party voter registration drives

* Online voter registration in states that allow it

* Automatic voter registration
Some estimates say that this bill could disenfranchise anywhere from 11 million to 65 million eligible Americans , especially women and those who live in under-resourced communities.
“ There’s never been an attack on voting rights out of Congress like this ,” said Alexander Keyssar, a professor of History and Social Policy at the Harvard Kennedy School, and a leading historian of voting rights. “ It’s always been the federal government trying to keep states in check on voting rights, for the most part. ”
The consequences of a bill like this passing would be simply catastrophic for our democracy. Chip in with $25 or whatever you can today to stop the SAVE Act and other voter suppression bills from becoming law >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/pdjfDAEu4UCpXPQPEnrW-A2]
https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0= [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2]
Donate Now [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=8xcO27yFuyu24fBb1HjIL7GrDL1i/p6pAgJUvMTHF+LvOUHXImSMJ18/lVfd1/Af/4uylojOdyTUb7JFhOc46PrzDm/2loAwsEHwdQO/fe0=]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=jkFsYPHDFpHiq34DCMiiD%2FmhunMZAxaDTew8RYUjEA%2BvmUD2GG0fUSJf0rhVxkLkIJT5Sk76U4kfK1nz%2BDqrRhFF6ZtC2qW9j9yyddxcOJQxZ9tDD5NSMsTGNfUJ4lyMN59biUiULI%2FUNHGuHoytt7lUZiESoKYOWrYrMfmfv57skvQcKadDZYOEXEx72GoQORj7SONvSvl1ABa5Pgw9mvmo9Z8%2Fd70nMMEkONSVJkWOnAFIOi2Q2vAbMnQ2DfVoRf02cgndBFi887NaEHtTWL4Xl29YG95YH77SIJc5cDXqZsPBtG2OIgm5BI%2FYErKpGuzl0DqkcuLXY6shrsfiNZD8y2h4C1hY4kQQtU4biUZkAGDAGBbl8uKoMpVx1z6g8oX8T%2FRVxhxP69LMJcAymRE4SMYZDwHaQyoCv8sz0BI%3D .

name: Team Crow
subject: One in four Coloradans will be harmed by the Republican budget plan
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Congress [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/logoJC%20(1).png]
Team —

Republicans want to rip health care away from children to give $150 billion to the Department of Defense led by incompetent people like Pete Hegseth.

That’s why Jason just voted no on the Republican budget in committee. Their budget would gut Medicaid and hurt working families.

One in four Coloradans depends on Medicaid. Veterans, working families, disabled Americans, and seniors — that’s who Republicans in Congress are harming with their federal budget.

Jason is fighting Republican attempts to take away our health care every day in Congress.

You can power his fight for Coloradans, our health care, and our future with a $5 donation today. Anything you can give will help us fend off the extreme MAGA agenda and elect more leaders who will protect crucial programs like Medicaid. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-may25?amount=5]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

body:
The legacy media isn't just biased; it's a propaganda arm for the Democrat Party, and it's time we bury it for good.

| |
---|---|---
|
# New Media MUST defeat the Mainstream Media
### The legacy media isn't just biased; it's a propaganda arm for the Democrat Party, and it's time we bury it for good.
| | May 5
---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9c6a8f7d-ac90-4c72-98d9-ee3f7ac82350?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**By Rudy Giuliani**
Folks, let's face it: the mainstream media has betrayed us. For years, they've been nothing but a mouthpiece for corporate elites and detached globalists who couldn't care less about the struggles of everyday Americans.
They've lied about President Trump for a decade, smearing him as a villain while ignoring his fight for the forgotten men and women of this country. They've slandered his supporters, calling you deplorable, racist, or worse. They covered up Joe Biden's obvious mental decline, pretending he was fit to lead while shoving Big Pharma's COVID vaccine and mask mandates down our throats--ads paid for by the same drug companies bankrolling their broadcasts. The legacy media isn't just biased; it's a propaganda arm for the Democrat Party, and it's time we bury it for good.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..jzNRhgVdDDQmePws4OEb0lwulh84TvmO9iJtjTMcAAA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=162907950)
Enter Substack, the final nail in the coffin of this corrupt media empire. Substack is a revolution--a platform where journalists can speak directly to you, no middleman, no censorship, no algorithms rigging the game. It's pure, unfiltered truth. Writers control their message, and readers get the raw, unspun story. This is how we take back the narrative from the elitists who've hijacked our airwaves. Substack empowers voices that the mainstream media has silenced, and it's changing the game for good.
**That 's why I'm urging you to support PolitiBrawl,** the largest political news Substack out there, amplifying conservative and populist voices to a rapidly growing audience. PolitiBrawl is a powerhouse, delivering hard-hitting content that speaks to the heart of America. Take Matt Miller's Man On The Street journalism--**[he dives into the chaos of radical left protests](https://substack.com/redirect/cf42e430-bb5c-43b6-a086-2e3c8b72b982?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , asking the tough questions the legacy media won't touch. Or consider PolitiBrawl's weekly columns, like my own **[America's Mayor Weekly,](https://substack.com/redirect/4be49505-a790-4b40-83e0-4b2bea00d6a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** where I break down the most important issues facing our nation. And don't miss Brian Patterson's **[daily debate breakdown videos,](https://substack.com/redirect/8e4ef3d8-9cb9-4723-8191-8990fbc76289?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** slicing through the noise with sharp analysis you won't find on CNN or MSNBC.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9c6a8f7d-ac90-4c72-98d9-ee3f7ac82350?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
PolitiBrawl is just getting started, but it's already a beacon for those fed up with the lies. The mainstream media wants to control what you think, feeding you a steady diet of half-truths and corporate talking points. Substack, and platforms like PolitiBrawl, give power back to the people--writers who tell it like it is and readers who demand the truth. This is our chance to rebuild a media that serves America, not the elites.
So, join the fight. Support PolitiBrawl today and take part in America breaking free from the media's stranglehold. Together, we can make sure the legacy media's days are numbered. Let's take back our country, one piece at a time.

BY: [@MattMackowiak](https://substack.com/redirect/8b9cc205-8c52-470d-ab16-b5c8f3f81da7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

_" If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator **Phil Gramm** (R-TX)_
* * *
**AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED**
Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to [ATX Pulse](https://substack.com/redirect/a02e1573-5c9b-4366-a5c4-bc9a90acf6c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4b88ba0e-385a-4c35-ba73-8eeb3159209f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here:_ [ATXpulsepremium.com](https://substack.com/redirect/affe1999-3793-44b4-8e1a-495beec0e192?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Read the most recent FREE subscriber email [here](https://substack.com/redirect/c4e8c89d-8492-4086-be95-6cfbc71ec13b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/affe1999-3793-44b4-8e1a-495beec0e192?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas governor signs $1 billion voucher bill in milestone for school choice supporters nationally** ," AP's **Nadia Lathan** \-- "Texas will implement [a $1 billion school voucher](https://substack.com/redirect/dfb5b734-af2e-4098-9312-ce67a50a635c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) program, one of the largest in the country, that uses public dollars to fund private school tuition under a bill Gov. **Greg Abbott** signed Saturday, capping off a yearslong effort by Republicans.

School voucher supporters have long targeted the state, where past efforts buckled for decades against resistance from Democrats and rural Republicans. Last month President **Donald Trump** called lawmakers before a key vote needed to finally get the bill to Abbott's desk.

Texas [joins more than 30 other states](https://substack.com/redirect/46c8a118-e690-4a7c-adb3-217d4b04ce99?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that have implemented a similar program, of which about a dozen have launched or expanded their programs in recent years to make most students eligible.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/b8399640-5abd-429c-b4a5-3116fe7b257a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
School vouchers have been Abbott's primary focus this year, coming off a 2024 election cycle in which he led a campaign to oust GOP lawmakers who voted against a similar bill last session.

Republican lawmakers and bill supporters say it will give parents more choice by letting them pull their children out of poor-performing public schools.

"Gone are the days that families are limited to only the schools assigned by government," Abbott said before signing the bill at the governor's mansion. "The day has arrived that empowers parents to choose the school that is best for their child."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/b8399640-5abd-429c-b4a5-3116fe7b257a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Democrats and Republicans in rural districts have criticized the program, saying it will drain financial resources from Texas' more than 5 million public school students and subsidize the private education of wealthy families.

Beginning next school year, Texas families can receive $10,000 per year to help pay for students' private school tuition. Children with disabilities can qualify for as much as $30,000 a year. The program will be capped at $1 billion for the first year and cover up to 90,000 students but could cost up to $4.5 billion a year by 2030." [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/c72e6479-9dad-48f1-ac76-98322d3e1ff6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**PLEASE NOTE** : You are receiving the PAID subscriber version of Must Read Texas -- FOR TODAY ONLY -- so you can SEE what you're missing as a FREE subscriber.
» Become a PAID subscriber [here](https://substack.com/redirect/b8399640-5abd-429c-b4a5-3116fe7b257a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
"**University of Texas chancellor is named president of the University of California** ," AP's **Nadia Lathan** \-- "**James B. Milliken** , who has led major public university systems in New York and Texas, was named president of the University of California on Friday.

Milliken comes to the role from the University of Texas, where he has served as chancellor since 2018. He has also held leadership roles at The City University of New York, the University of Nebraska and the University of North Carolina.

The UC system has 10 campuses, including Berkeley, Los Angeles and Davis. Milliken starts on Aug. 1. He will be paid nearly $1.5 million a year.

"The University of California is universally regarded as the preeminent public research university in the world, and I am deeply honored to have an opportunity to join the many talented faculty, staff, and campus leaders in their vital work," Milliken said in a UC press release. "It is more important than ever that we expand the education, research, health care, and public service for which UC is so widely admired and which has benefited so many Californians."

The announcement comes at a time when the Trump administration is [targeting federal funding](https://substack.com/redirect/51956159-1a23-4aeb-ae5b-25ac8c9b4962?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at universities in an attempt to get them to comply with the Republican president's political agenda.

The University of California, Berkeley, is among [dozens of colleges under investigation](https://substack.com/redirect/a8eb0305-d8d9-4c10-8ea8-43238cd066ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the U.S. Education Department over its ties to The PhD Project, a group aimed at diversifying the business world and higher education faculty. The Trump administration is also investigating Berkeley [over allegations of antisemitism](https://substack.com/redirect/ee79363a-eb90-4707-b0ed-1bc6b2e92b3f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) linked to pro-Palestinian protests against the war in Gaza that broke out last year across campuses nationwide.

In response, Berkeley said it has "an unwavering commitment" to fighting antisemitism.

The Trump administration also [launched probes](https://substack.com/redirect/72548b48-61cb-4f1e-a873-b1ba9408724b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) into three University of California campuses over their admissions policies to determine whether they comply with the U.S. Supreme Court's [decision to overturn affirmative action](https://substack.com/redirect/7e862fca-08f2-4967-95ab-894e19cbeb94?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in college admissions.

Milliken succeeds **Michael V. Drake** as UC president. Drake was the first Black person to serve in the role in the system's more than 150-year history. Drake announced last year that he would step down after serving as president since 2020, leading the system through the coronavirus pandemic, a graduate student strike and various campus protests." [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/89f758a7-994c-402c-9d5f-deb982fc3a18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

"**Severe storms and heavy rains expected across Texas this week as omega block hovers** ," Austin American-Statesman's **Brandi D. Addison** and**John Bacon** \-- "A pair of storm systems could fuel [heavy rain and flooding](https://substack.com/redirect/d3277e4b-caf6-40d5-b339-ca2a8cb598b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across sections of the nation from the Desert Southwest to the East Coast this week, and some areas are bracing for the possibility of tornadoes or hail the size of tennis balls, forecasters said.

"A slow-moving storm over the Central United States this week will act as the catalyst for a risk of severe thunderstorms and flooding rainfall," [AccuWeather](https://substack.com/redirect/640e4489-246d-4c3a-bf01-715fab4e66eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Meteorologist **Brandon Buckingham** said.

A congested atmospheric pattern [known as an omega block](https://substack.com/redirect/d3277e4b-caf6-40d5-b339-ca2a8cb598b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is to blame. The pattern forms a jet stream shaped like the Greek letter omega (Ω), often trapping a high-pressure system over the northern Plains, flanked by two closed low-pressure systems. As the block shifts or breaks down, those nearby low-pressure systems can bring stormy weather to the region.

Severe storms and heavy rainfall are expected across the state early this week as the omega block looms over the United States.

Portions of West Texas and far eastern New Mexico could see large hailstones and possibly isolated tornadoes late on Monday, AccuWeather warned. Travelers along Interstate 10 and 20 in Texas could face travel delays as storms ignite across the region.

By Tuesday, a heavy rain and thunderstorm pattern will sweep across eastern Texas, Oklahoma, Arkansas and Louisiana. Intense winds, hail, isolated tornadoes and flash flooding are possible Tuesday and Tuesday night, AccuWeather said.

In Central Texas, including Austin and San Antonio, severe weather is expected to arrive midday, with a brief pause overnight before storms resume tomorrow morning and continue into the evening.

"The main threats are large hail, damaging wind gusts, and heavy rain, with an isolated tornado also possible," according to the National Weather Service office in Austin.

Meanwhile, showers and storms are expected in the Dallas-Fort Worth area this afternoon, though the [National Weather Service](https://substack.com/redirect/9487ad03-94de-4404-ae1a-b7a273fb73ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) notes that "severe weather is not anticipated with this activity."

Like Central Texas, the Dallas-Fort Worth area is expected to see more storms on Tuesday, with the potential for large hail, damaging winds, a few tornadoes, and flash flooding. The greatest risk for severe weather is expected between 11 a.m. and 5 p.m. Tuesday.

Similar conditions are expected across much of the state, from West Texas in the Permian Basin to East Texas and up into the Panhandle, as the storm system gradually moves from the Rockies and Great Plains into the southeastern U.S.

Omega blocks can persist for several days to over a week, leading to prolonged periods of unusual weather, whether that means heat, dryness, or storms, depending on the location within the pattern.

Local forecasts show storms moving out of Texas by the end of this week, however." [AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/a0132a26-a93a-421f-b3a7-8433973483f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **STATE GOVERNMENT**

**" Texas AG Ken Paxton appeals $6.7M whistleblower judgment,**" Dallas Morning News' **Nolan D. McCaskill** -- "A whistleblower who was awarded part of a nearly $6.7 million judgment last month said**Ken Paxton** is appealing the award to stall the plaintiff's victory while the Texas attorney general runs for U.S. Senate.

A [Travis County judge in April awarded the sum](https://substack.com/redirect/9501b170-090f-48e7-b972-4cf76bfa1059?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to four whistleblowers who reported Paxton to law enforcement in 2020 for alleged criminal conduct. After the ruling, Paxton vowed to appeal.

An attorney representing Paxton's office filed the appeal Friday in Travis County district court.

The judgment awarded compensation for lost wages, pain and suffering, attorney fees, court costs and interest for**James "Blake" Brickman**, **David Maxwell** , **Mark Penley** and **Ryan Vassar**. The former Paxton aides sued the agency in November 2020 under the Whistleblower Act after their firings. The law prohibits retaliation against public employees who report a violation of law in good faith.

The judgment is more than double the [$3.3 million Paxton's office agreed to pay in a 2023 settlement](https://substack.com/redirect/ea149514-a650-45ca-8dce-4ae0b78e82e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the Legislature declined to fund. State lawmakers would need to approve funding for the state to satisfy the latest judgment.

Brickman, former policy director at the attorney general's office, said the appeal is another example of Paxton's "brazen dishonesty and shameless abuse of taxpayer funds" for personal gain.

A spokesperson for Paxton's office did not immediately respond to an emailed request for comment.

The whistleblowers accused Paxton of using his office to benefit **Nate Paul** , a real estate investor and campaign contributor. Paul was [fined $1 million last month and sentenced to four months of home confinement](https://substack.com/redirect/82b0b83d-b92d-4a9e-b9ff-7c3ec09f24fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after [pleading guilty in January](https://substack.com/redirect/18fb1ad6-f238-440d-b817-2ca8d1e29d55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to a federal charge of making false statements to a financial institution.

In return, the whistleblowers alleged, Paul renovated Paxton's Austin home and employed a woman Paxton was reportedly seeing.

[Paxton was impeached](https://substack.com/redirect/fc5d51d8-f860-4493-9099-51301263ba6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the Texas House in 2023 but [acquitted by the Senate](https://substack.com/redirect/18fcc60d-72e3-4555-b30f-dc2a50f1418a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after a two-week trial. The impeachment process cost Texas taxpayers $5 million, [an audit found](https://substack.com/redirect/db2842ad-eac3-4d3c-96ce-11521e55137b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

In a statement last month, Paxton referenced his acquittal and the cost of impeachment. He said a "liberal judge" wants taxpayers to waste more money on "a ridiculous judgment that is not based on the facts or the law."

In November, [the Texas Supreme Court said Paxton did not have to sit for a deposition](https://substack.com/redirect/d06c3023-a9f5-4d43-87f8-0990c1e7f782?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the whistleblower case after agreeing in January 2024 to end the lawsuit and [pledging not to contest the damages or liability](https://substack.com/redirect/10663ce4-163d-4f20-9540-6173b9e194a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Paxton [launched his Republican primary campaign](https://substack.com/redirect/8d9699ac-b9cf-45f0-a0ba-32ed4bb1a6cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against U.S. Sen. **John Cornyn** last month.

"Despite saying he would not appeal, Paxton is now doing exactly that," Brickman said. "This appeal has nothing to do with the facts of the whistleblowers' claims or how the law applies -- it is only to delay the finality of our victory while Paxton runs for higher office."" [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/695352aa-0af0-42cb-ab81-19f2ede50793?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

**" AG Paxton launches investigation into fluoride toothpaste marketing,**" KVUE's **Johann Castro** -- "Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** is launching an investigation into the makers of children's fluoride toothpaste over their marketing.

Paxton sent Civil Investigative Demands, or CIV's, to Procter & Gamble - who sells Crest - and Colgate-Palmolive - who sells Colgate, claiming that they illegally market their products to children.

The American Dental Association recommends no more than a pea-sized amount of fluoride for children between the ages of 3 and 6-years-old, but Paxton is claiming that the companies mislead parents to use far more than the recommended amount.

Paxton also said that the toothpaste manufacturers encourage kids to ingest toothpaste.

"I will use every tool available to protect our kids from dangerous levels of fluoride exposure and deceptive advertising," Paxton said in a Thursday news release. "Toothpaste manufacturers must follow state law to ensure that they aren't putting Texas families in peril through their false, misleading, and deceptive marketing, and these CIDs will help my office discover any potential wrongdoing."" [KVUE](https://substack.com/redirect/1bdbcaf0-e53c-4077-8964-a86a3f739b29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **2025**

**" San Antonio mayoral race heads to runoff between Ortiz Jones and Pablos**," via News 4 San Antonio -- "The race for San Antonio's mayoral seat is heading to a runoff as former Under Secretary of the Air Force **Gina Ortiz Jones** and former Texas Secretary of State **Rolando Pablos** vie for the position.

This election marks the first time a mayor will serve a four-year term after voters approved longer terms last November. The overall turnout for the election was just 9.26%, the lowest since 2013.

Ortiz Jones captured nearly 27% of the votes, maintaining her lead from early voting numbers. Pablos received 17%.

Because no candidates collected 50% of the vote, Ortiz Jones and Pablos will meet in a runoff election on June 7.

Ortiz Jones, who chose to spend election night with her team rather than holding a watch party, expressed her determination to continue her campaign efforts.

"We'll continue to work hard. We run through the tape again. I've got the best team. We're very excited about our message, how it's resonating all across this city, and we'll continue knocking on those doors, making those phone calls and telling people what's possible, right with the right leader leading the city," she said.

"The fact that no sitting city council member finished in the top three positions tonight is a clear sign that San Antonio voters are sick of business as usual, and are ready to get down to business to secure a more prosperous future for our children and grandchildren," Pablos said in a statement. "San Antonio now has a clear choice, and I am confident they will reject Gina Jones for what she represents: more of the same, failed leadership from the San Antonio political machine that has left poverty rates in stagnation and caused businesses to pass over the Alamo city due to the radical, misplaced policy priorities that are completely out of line with the values of San Antonians."

The winner of this year's election will also receive a salary increase to $87,800 per year, up from the nearly $62,000 salary previously paid to Mayor **Ron Nirenberg**." [News 4 San Antonio](https://substack.com/redirect/f8dcc8fe-8fc0-4a14-b1d3-f287ba73a490?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" SpaceX workers in South Texas vote yes to create City of Starbase**," Texas Tribune's **Berenice Garcia** and Texas Newsroom's**Lauren McGaughy** -- "**Elon Musk 's** wish to create his own city just came true.

On Saturday, voters living around SpaceX's rocket testing and launch facility in South Texas approved a measure to incorporate the area as a new city. Soon after polls closed at 7 p.m., Cameron County election officials confirmed that overwhelming support among early voters clinched the election in favor of creating the City of Starbase.

Unofficial results later in the night showed the election was a landslide: 212 voted in favor; 6 opposed.

After the county certifies the results, the new city will be official.

Musk celebrated the vote Saturday night with a single word posted to X: "[S T A R B A S E](https://substack.com/redirect/ae55febc-2809-4f36-8601-cda9fcf4febb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."

But there were no signs of a celebration at Starbase itself. After a gray, rainy Election Day, the soon-to-be-city had a quiet night. No fireworks. No street parties. A few folks gathered at the Astropub, a bar for SpaceX employees, late into the evening.

Only 283 people, those who live within the boundaries of the proposed city, were eligible to vote in the election. A Texas Newsroom analysis of the voter rolls showed two-thirds of them either [work for SpaceX](https://substack.com/redirect/855d8ca3-25ce-4aa9-b8f0-792dc6194f9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or had already indicated their support.

The three unopposed people who ran to lead the city also [have ties to SpaceX](https://substack.com/redirect/818552cc-b660-4340-824b-518e823e2b58?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

It's not clear if Musk, whose primary residence is here, cast a ballot.

The vote clears the way for Musk to try to capture more [control over the nearby public beach](https://substack.com/redirect/76c8d482-4472-40c6-b8c1-5cdad106a879?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which must be closed for launches. On Saturday afternoon, a few dozen activists who say the company has harmed sensitive habitats and ignored the cultural importance of the land rallied on the beach -- with an Elon Musk piñata in tow." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/18fbed83-41a5-4ec9-aa8d-9b7409d35aa8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **2026**

**" Elon Musk's attorney John Bash drops out of Texas Attorney General race**," KXAN's **Adam Schwager** -- "**John Bash 's **campaign to replace **Ken Paxton** as Texas Attorney General lasted for just 21 days.

"Today, I've made the difficult decision to leave the race for Texas AG," [Bash wrote on X](https://substack.com/redirect/6ec6d49e-bbf8-4404-9d8f-02f8a345ce16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Wednesday evening. "On Friday, our family had a health scare that threw into sharp focus how I should prioritize my time right now, and that is not running for office."

Bash announced his campaign on April 9, drawing headlines not only for being the first major candidate to enter the race, but for his ties to billionaire **Elon Musk**. Bash currently [represents](https://substack.com/redirect/e3ff4dde-23c2-40bf-8fff-0421c7ab6426?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Musk in a defamation case after Musk amplified social media posts falsely placing a California man at a clash involving far-right protesters near an Oregon Pride festival.

Bash spoke with [State of Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/5b30a339-c8d1-47ca-8dd5-43a144650144?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) two weeks ago, highlighting his record as the former U.S. attorney for the Western District of Texas -- contrasting it with the record of State Senator and oil and gas businessman **Mayes Middleton** , R - Galveston.

"I'm not a career politician. I've never run for office before," he said. "(Attorney General is) not a position you use to advance your political career. It's a real working position that needs to come up with legal strategies to keep our citizens safe, to keep them prosperous."

In his post announcing the end of his campaign, Bash slightly changed his tune about Middleton.

"The other candidate in the race, Senator [@mayes_middleton](https://substack.com/redirect/77b67162-a4f1-4d4f-b090-930aba261cc8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), has served Texas honorably in the Legislature, and I know him to be a good man," Bash wrote. Despite the compliments, he failed to endorse any candidates for the job. "I will be rooting for the next AG to represent our great State with distinction and to continue the impactful work of General [@KenPaxtonTX](https://substack.com/redirect/9261d95d-51b6-4d80-a61c-8fe51e4f8bf6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."

With Bash out of the race, Middleton is the only person publicly running to replace Paxton. The election is in November 2026." [KXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/184fd417-8458-45e7-8590-f06cf55cbc7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **TEXANS IN DC**

**" Chip Roy steps up pressure on Congress to cut Medicaid expansion,**" San Antonio Express-News' **James Osborne** -- "U.S. Rep. **Chip Roy** is ramping up [pressure on House Republicans](https://substack.com/redirect/333638de-8edd-4dd4-9be6-108fa04a467e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to make the larger cuts to Medicaid that many within his party oppose.

In a letter to members on Thursday, Roy and 19 other House Republicans, including U.S. Reps. **Keith Self** and **Michael Cloud** of Texas, called for pulling back on the expansion of Medicaid benefits to low-income working adults that Congress passed during the Obama administration and that have been adopted in nearly every state.

"If the House budget reconciliation package does not include structural Medicaid reform... we will be setting up massive tax increases and benefit cuts in the future," they wrote.

Medicaid expansion covers adults who make up to 138% of the federal poverty rate, or about $21,600 in 2025. States that opt into the program contribute a small percentage of the costs, with the federal government picking up the rest.

Roy's push to return Medicaid to a program solely for low-income children, pregnant women, seniors and people with disabilities comes as Republicans are searching for ways [to slash $1.5 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/52008aa2-3fd8-4fd3-b810-aecfa6e19a66?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the federal budget.

House GOP members are divided over Medicaid expansion and other benefits for low-income families. U.S. Rep. **Tony Gonzales** , the San Antonio Republican whose district includes much of the border, who leads the GOP's Congressional Hispanic Conference, [has warned members](https://substack.com/redirect/872c1670-2fdc-411d-a3a6-9a34199067cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that they face political fallout in cutting programs that are "helping hard working Americans."

Gonzales did not respond to a request for comment Friday.

The House Energy and Commerce Committee, which oversees Medicaid, has already delayed a hearing on the $780 billion in spending cuts they have been directed to come up with**** by House Leadership.

Ending the federal match for Medicaid expansion would save the government $626 billion over the next decade, according to [analysis by the health research non-profit KFF](https://substack.com/redirect/24eecc81-7f38-42a8-bb20-c44f0c9310f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And unless states decided to make up the loss, an estimated 20 millions Americans would lose their healthcare coverage.

Texas is one of ten states that [chose not to expand Medicaid ](https://substack.com/redirect/ae57580a-b5e8-4fcd-9b39-9875308667ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)to low-income Americans. But for Republicans representing states that did expand coverage, cutting federal funding has so far proven a difficult sell.

"I will not support any cuts to eligible legal individuals or facilities," U.S. Rep.**Jeff Van Drew** , R-NJ, told CNN Thursday. "With those types of issues, we're affecting real people and real lives, and that's where we have to be careful."

Roy, who has long warned of the dangers of continuing to run up the national debt, cautioned Republicans that if they wanted to continue with President**Donald Trump's** plans to extend income tax cuts they would need to address Medicaid sooner or later. In the letter on Thursday,**** he pointed to [the Congressional Budget Office](https://substack.com/redirect/6ed759ab-8872-4669-9e47-885a9ee32713?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s estimate that, without changes, Medicaid spending would increase by $1.2 trillion over the next decade.

"The political abuse of this program is helping to bankrupt the federal government," the letter read. "For once, Congress should stop procrastinating, using excuses, and finally fulfill the Republican agenda."" [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/dc79a0a2-0752-4cb7-9c90-a431f9f40f5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **BUSINESS NEWS**

**" Elon Musk adviser launches three Mars-themed companies in Texas**," San Antonio Express-News' **Brandon Lingle** -- "Three ventures with a Mars theme are the latest offshoots of **Elon Musk's** ever-growing Texas empire.

The additions to the billionaire's portfolio come amid a flurry of expansions and organizational changes in his other companies and talk of him stepping back from his role leading the Trump administration's [Department of Government Efficiency](https://substack.com/redirect/86b491b2-af36-4ed1-b322-84fc1d7c8c05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to focus on Tesla Inc., [the troubled EV maker](https://substack.com/redirect/f43d2868-28a0-425d-b1d9-78950ad8cb58?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) he leads, and his other companies.

The new entitites -- Red Planet Ventures I, II and III -- were formed in late February by **Jared Birchall** , according to filings with the Texas Secretary of State. Birchall, who is CEO of Musk's family office and holds leadership roles in several other of his companies, registered an Austin address and listed himself as manager of the new limited liability companies.

They join Musk's other firms based in Texas, which include Tesla and xAI in Austin, [SpaceX](https://substack.com/redirect/43f45e33-314d-4610-b8b9-a757c3d590ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) outside Brownsville, X and the Boring Co. in Bastrop. Neuralink, Musk's brain implant company, has labs and offices in Del Valle, east of Austin, but its headquarters is in Fremont, Calif.

Little is known about the Red Planet trio other than their name is in step with Musk's dreams of Mars colonization. Their formation mirrors that of other holding companies he's created when making business moves, most notably the purchase of social media platform Twitter in 2022.

At that time, he formed X Holdings I, II and III to facilitate the purchase of the San Francisco-based company, which he quickly renamed X and [relocated to Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/1b0ad44e-40e9-4b5d-9e16-fe1817644de1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

In March, xAI, Musk's artificial intelligence startup, bought X in an all-stock deal. He said the deal values xAI at $80 billion and X at $33 billion. Saying the companies' futures are "intertwined" Musk said the merger was a "step to combine the data, models, compute, distribution and talent."

There's much overlap across Musk's enterprises and some analysts see them as all contributing to his vision of putting a human civilization on Mars.

Musk wants to send uncrewed Starships, currently under development at SpaceX's South Texas Starbase, to the Red Planet [as soon as late next year](https://substack.com/redirect/efe25a31-f919-4ef1-90f4-17705bdde7d8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

"If those landings go well, then human landings may start as soon as 2029, although 2031 is more likely," he said in a post on X. They'll carry the humanoid [Optimus robots](https://substack.com/redirect/d98f7f66-a0f4-4786-8bcf-2341fced53fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Tesla is developing to provide automated labor.

Musk's tunneling firm, the [Boring Co.](https://substack.com/redirect/e63662d8-33ce-48de-8a0d-4522a9c94719?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), has Mars applications, too, with technologies it says could help create underground habitats. And xAI's quick-growing AI models are likely to help all his projects, including space exploration.

The mystery of the Red Planet Ventures LLCs follows Musk's habit of using webs of shell companies when he's got something new cooking.

SpaceX gobbled up swaths of land in South Texas under affiliates [Dogleg Park LLC](https://substack.com/redirect/e894c4f1-1300-4222-9b43-2145a807b0f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and Flats at Mars Crossing. The Boring Co. operates under Gapped Bass LLC in Bastrop and Tesla developed its Gigafactory outside Austin as the Colorado River Project LLC.

Birchall is listed as CEO of Neuralink, and he holds titles at the Boring Co., xAI and the Musk Foundation. He's helped Musk create various entities including the family office, which is known as Excession, xAI, the Ad Astra school and Horse Ranch LLC.

He also was an advisor to the Trump transition team, according to the New York Times." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/1974518e-8834-4c8e-8096-292d4a9f430a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS "Texas governor signs $1 billion voucher bill in milestone for school choice supporters nationally ," AP's Nadia Lathan \-- "Texas will implement program, one of the largest in the country, that uses public dollars to fund private school tuition under a bill Gov. Greg Abbott signed Saturday, capping off a yearslong effort by Republicans. School voucher supporters have long targeted the state, where past efforts buckled for decades against resistance from Democrats and rural Republicans. Last month President Donald Trump called lawmakers before a key vote needed to finally get the bill to Abbott's desk. Texas that have implemented a similar program, of which about a dozen have launched or expanded their programs in recent years to make most students eligible. School vouchers have been Abbott's primary focus this year, coming off a 2024 election cycle in which he led a campaign to oust GOP lawmakers who voted against a similar bill last session. Republican lawmakers and bill supporters say it will give parents more choice by letting them pull their children out of poor-performing public schools. "Gone are the days that families are limited to only the schools assigned by government," Abbott said before signing the bill at the governor's mansion. "The day has arrived that empowers parents to choose the school that is best for their child." Democrats and Republicans in rural districts have criticized the program, saying it will drain financial resources from Texas' more than 5 million public school students and subsidize the private education of wealthy families. Beginning next school year, Texas families can receive $10,000 per year to help pay for students' private school tuition. Children with disabilities can qualify for as much as $30,000 a year. The program will be capped at $1 billion for the first year and cover up to 90,000 students but could cost up to $4.5 billion a year by 2030."
