"**University of Texas chancellor is named president of the University of California** ,” AP's **Nadia Lathan** \-- "**James B. Milliken** , who has led major public university systems in New York and Texas, was named president of the University of California on Friday.

Milliken comes to the role from the University of Texas, where he has served as chancellor since 2018. He has also held leadership roles at The City University of New York, the University of Nebraska and the University of North Carolina.

The UC system has 10 campuses, including Berkeley, Los Angeles and Davis. Milliken starts on Aug. 1. He will be paid nearly $1.5 million a year.

“The University of California is universally regarded as the preeminent public research university in the world, and I am deeply honored to have an opportunity to join the many talented faculty, staff, and campus leaders in their vital work,” Milliken said in a UC press release. “It is more important than ever that we expand the education, research, health care, and public service for which UC is so widely admired and which has benefited so many Californians.”

The announcement comes at a time when the Trump administration is [targeting federal funding](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.taRaxrEkWqxIZl3CYy7RU-8zPWZErbzsTpoOJuc75r2wY5ui32fAKaF19PRSuH9yE_bfvWDgNi1u71A8jVhlqXtZI_0ma9wJmF2rV3F9UqZOrHFuwNzrITeDVhcARxDLRu6QC6oqcpyzniNax1i5CTLPG_n4X1MpWFmiE5q0IAEMR3W1oJqR4VB0y4GF3z8ovkrNrRtqao5Pe1wNvDzF6uzZGegRuR54KAQV_NF9bchfQYcusGTgT_bgCV2fU3ENR8U5FjUEU0wnaskmjNHjT4lRlwU4AxF8LSbw7C8x3u5pUX2HSsAVIuZtCR8rt7NNn_qM9BJskJ8JvUTzsE6Bngexq1JqPcqKr43qJsAOYNUQ5AowPhbblQDkracmLxv-7MfZGt4PvhPNpCI4x9Nh7Q8YDMHgwuVaGeZwWz3CQP9uqO5fBs8d8T1zRmcW4mJ7IXDy99I5cYL6nrOkd3cRw6J881LhYshORVA2KRDU_mRfZT6-h8ZOF26RtOIHBRmuqdMIWAQ8XBlHBdaOT1CXtbxso2jqmGL7W649hph4lRacu-5EtJW7LV9yv6ZwhygRsqp4O2LyJJU_VNaTWduFBw/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h19/h001.jbH-ND_0puHp6IN-nCnDap82q7JvzTDsgAOyid-gO4s) at universities in an attempt to get them to comply with the Republican president’s political agenda.

"**Severe storms and heavy rains expected across Texas this week as omega block hovers** ,” Austin American-Statesman's **Brandi D. Addison** and**John Bacon** \-- "A pair of storm systems could fuel [heavy rain and flooding](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eLgP52KmpCiOYGubUgAoA1DIB2ZriVbQvvyRLAU0PLtGHSjE3rIcm-8XXwe_6JZSc594ensIFBEf7cY2t2SNk6J7ASsDHslX5q6jUmSdcNZwjDA-5_WoZf-TdV8fhzpiCxJGkNDEyaD6bjakgzf-XgzjwFuYpUJlSVQxmJy_s2mGi-qONFhxPi25PUdJBxcDipAHDqzSmVMVqq617cZGqTgZ_cQHDrrW6eqySUcviuQRB35LJJDDFpi0AC_R98KrMSemVutkJaYk9NsekYNqA6BfBrMGLXTJIBOJxmfIvzUSmIJWMFDuqdqjssVlUCX82QtPLLDHnUFbNG5iqJ6nyqhPoZCqKzwxCg8PCDhtdt9567ZsHKg8ONZ_cyToj2PGDsOtHxpGEKe3mkzygKFVIaVYq-0rR4O3AbAp5_uJ1l6PMobkaq4c-_RqqilzRuq4K5Ol8DmIBroP3ja2ofTxWlFo6wbqaYY4zYT_AFbeCIWiWgeZnLa7YYQRaitPFNQiaBxHpFPHefXqeUcdlNuQQ0LNX0LIpYUsPxbpf-EtzaE8A0BB-a6UeKjokSUmLoY93Q/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h25/h001.hvSPA1qhkFZW2K14d-t9wltzXLcOFvEs8Kd33FU3Jd4) across sections of the nation from the Desert Southwest to the East Coast this week, and some areas are bracing for the possibility of tornadoes or hail the size of tennis balls, forecasters said.

“A slow-moving storm over the Central United States this week will act as the catalyst for a risk of severe thunderstorms and flooding rainfall,” [AccuWeather](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eMoOFhVR5ulVo7F1e_Q9mylUIl5dtxBUnPoNXw1hfKxYV6UQsGP2A9V61MQbHzeHI5MhXmQmqR2L0YfzNX5SLnsG2bLKIu9dYvdtzgC5vjdN-WYMZMr4NSFGksUh2lzl0ZtRDoo68eIhehFFzjXmJv8AqpckY3WwwrzsxJ6--b9oH2DUR03orB7JOAnGhenfnUe4R7rXLMux4kYaPcT02Sr_VUgWHORYOpf_dEIcAaPXBjaQBP0AMXqYWNT2TMYMwV2E9mi3CJPLW83ESKY5sH5Wx1KxP7gBynhlHPMWqyWTanX9xMs1girIbjs6IT49OkGEtMZv_vrF8LeaQ14UJacVee9z65KXxxhth7xqQaDiIunJgfMkezorBzSjVY_lRdPVim8n5XUH4OVpHgFblwKiugTWhcYz6C5PpkwcGK6ar5GW32kJ58iGzA-6VnSfiRzJiCfOLSimJ7pjcDYf6tXjKYBHld8oUrg1kw2uPsS1m7Y5GjXWdv8Nt6JF5hpuUrouQD_Mino-SM_raxZRiLeoZZcEgAoZwiRYxnol35cgB_OmHC1btmztcB1jhVJpoA/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h26/h001.mnEkTZyKoAc5Jdh84n4Y2LHzVLqFeNKOL7i2OpvkiEI) Meteorologist **Brandon Buckingham** said.

A congested atmospheric pattern [known as an omega block](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eLgP52KmpCiOYGubUgAoA1DIB2ZriVbQvvyRLAU0PLtGHSjE3rIcm-8XXwe_6JZSc594ensIFBEf7cY2t2SNk6J7ASsDHslX5q6jUmSdcNZwjDA-5_WoZf-TdV8fhzpiCxJGkNDEyaD6bjakgzf-XgzjwFuYpUJlSVQxmJy_s2mGi-qONFhxPi25PUdJBxcDipAHDqzSmVMVqq617cZGqTgZ_cQHDrrW6eqySUcviuQRB35LJJDDFpi0AC_R98KrMSemVutkJaYk9NsekYNqA6BfBrMGLXTJIBOJxmfIvzUSmIJWMFDuqdqjssVlUCX82QtPLLDHnUFbNG5iqJ6nyqhPoZCqKzwxCg8PCDhtdt9567ZsHKg8ONZ_cyToj2PGDsOtHxpGEKe3mkzygKFVIaVYq-0rR4O3AbAp5_uJ1l6PMobkaq4c-_RqqilzRuq4K5Ol8DmIBroP3ja2ofTxWlFo6wbqaYY4zYT_AFbeCIWiWgeZnLa7YYQRaitPFNQiaCvRxHKmUMF144gSwx0Qvi3U3023kYhWvB8omxRvnAUWnb9C5dPgzvYD7xjGx80yyQ/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h27/h001.GhhqlH6XOut2Vi64b2VFp5xpJIWO-iHBVXK1U4SUhcs) is to blame. The pattern forms a jet stream shaped like the Greek letter omega (Ω), often trapping a high-pressure system over the northern Plains, flanked by two closed low-pressure systems. As the block shifts or breaks down, those nearby low-pressure systems can bring stormy weather to the region.

Severe storms and heavy rainfall are expected across the state early this week as the omega block looms over the United States.

Portions of West Texas and far eastern New Mexico could see large hailstones and possibly isolated tornadoes late on Monday, AccuWeather warned. Travelers along Interstate 10 and 20 in Texas could face travel delays as storms ignite across the region.

By Tuesday, a heavy rain and thunderstorm pattern will sweep across eastern Texas, Oklahoma, Arkansas and Louisiana. Intense winds, hail, isolated tornadoes and flash flooding are possible Tuesday and Tuesday night, AccuWeather said.

In Central Texas, including Austin and San Antonio, severe weather is expected to arrive midday, with a brief pause overnight before storms resume tomorrow morning and continue into the evening.

“The main threats are large hail, damaging wind gusts, and heavy rain, with an isolated tornado also possible,” according to the National Weather Service office in Austin.

Meanwhile, showers and storms are expected in the Dallas–Fort Worth area this afternoon, though the [National Weather Service](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0ePXGLzX1ClXkXWmNbV1QLaUJYAUV1D14gz2r__iUzMRD-3-vPBXwCzDiSC4Ey3DBsqjExYJBKABPucpFXWKxjbxMqkR_-KYw0Qg6s0enbNlDgkf2TWBhM3xz1nMvN9alQcVSzuZFQZFWempfgP74vy2LcyuMzR3ILK24Yzqgv6YoDhnsnScsS4nCF8ceDSlEj3n4w8LW3IJSb29yNae-j6EtcdkVV4XZjt06kWA04kzMJF5GyeiCdITGl7yAMR5gLyT3qEdwNZUuGnKyc_pEgOVHWBBky_fDZd3pk_TDcTpxrHLIup8etIEyrKS60DiVjPYHopfKCafmDDrNzVcMUiK_73HZS2yoCFiYGMSQF8UqzTete-dWBxMI6KliGVgzeNWW1i_x3ToPuCm7ckn-M9pezaE8xSVF6fv4UKT6Y_Fl4uL-D61rbBF0PhT2FpqLKi-KKnR6AMMk-cp80DY2nZQ/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h28/h001.w348xu2Tx53nXOyHvnKyxE-zjvBu4X8BTYF-QpEaN5g) notes that “severe weather is not anticipated with this activity.”

Like Central Texas, the Dallas–Fort Worth area is expected to see more storms on Tuesday, with the potential for large hail, damaging winds, a few tornadoes, and flash flooding. The greatest risk for severe weather is expected between 11 a.m. and 5 p.m. Tuesday.

Similar conditions are expected across much of the state, from West Texas in the Permian Basin to East Texas and up into the Panhandle, as the storm system gradually moves from the Rockies and Great Plains into the southeastern U.S.

Omega blocks can persist for several days to over a week, leading to prolonged periods of unusual weather, whether that means heat, dryness, or storms, depending on the location within the pattern.

Local forecasts show storms moving out of Texas by the end of this week, however." * **STATE GOVERNMENT**

**“Texas AG Ken Paxton appeals $6.7M whistleblower judgment,** ” Dallas Morning News' **Nolan D. McCaskill** — "A whistleblower who was awarded part of a nearly $6.7 million judgment last month said**Ken Paxton** is appealing the award to stall the plaintiff’s victory while the Texas attorney general runs for U.S. Senate.

A [Travis County judge in April awarded the sum](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_kt4zZ7BA9X6VHUBegwwAunj8Wx2f59oHgsVfkKF7OAMOpEiqb8pqH-oNWDTgiHzksOyamzcy7yTSU1OLw__VHgFc-iJGgS_DUNmDtepN97syjRzKaL-6dxhXTC0U-nFIPUpIHWtF0PzrMFR1XChjoopkifY7Il_rceF23DTmW-2NotLNxVRNpgs0HghR_7q_N5uzFAHg_SvQWpnadD6c4JB7XZXC_2BpgB8JP-aWSMOaaNdhnQVyZRk6SmcKxw3Ns3V8x8WxlC566y7QcMLnCX4KneSKlOqrulpLTc-SLMMSPFRC2QZgYHSS6iImX2ne0wiICNmsI8xchb7nBocqzn2-5mkxPNwSOGZjvDZq2RwQkT-V_9g1ZDEPXkSUW6WmO2CQI8tAsbBn6lPm5t5VdAGI_woUL9UcWlYnKl4DE6yawOQ-vgzps3Kf2HbzM_C_4lPwS94qjbAQPHAwd4mcrA-QNAqmaWzaS5d810bnK3DBVV1OIhzl_gYk4r2D2piJU0nmvSuuH5DYLla1QAxx7ErgyMLaDe0B3hio4dETIdVQ/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h30/h001.Aix6vvcibZOP87oeI3x05znLsP-uKqlAZt10U6tNBEo) to four whistleblowers who reported Paxton to law enforcement in 2020 for alleged criminal conduct. After the ruling, Paxton vowed to appeal.

An attorney representing Paxton’s office filed the appeal Friday in Travis County district court.

The judgment awarded compensation for lost wages, pain and suffering, attorney fees, court costs and interest for**James “Blake” Brickman** , **David Maxwell** , **Mark Penley** and **Ryan Vassar**. The former Paxton aides sued the agency in November 2020 under the Whistleblower Act after their firings. The law prohibits retaliation against public employees who report a violation of law in good faith.

The judgment is more than double the [$3.3 million Paxton’s office agreed to pay in a 2023 settlement](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_kt4zZ7BA9X6VHUBegwwAunLLGIhc65maYAZPjV0sKPalz9xNoGLNs5K6gJxhR_SPJ7v4yKZjY8ddFmR8kYgPLWW2FpO71guSpt0Jj4rlYtxcuWfvCcKV7hUmycrTTxjQ2D0bm7Bt3XQL7zo3R7ziKPGHvuSVdAR2dvSCFm0VOzfw-c_AQp2xu9e7M8oWvFGZg1PYSgMFyWLpU8laGUBhyozMSs4ck7OGYQCUk3IOc0EugEk8EOOejk0YtM29YMzeq4Pcr8-F-cmsIle1p-6Sc9KcUf2qVLFdEvSWpXTp3p6nRarKHf25wS3vIiP-2a4aSH2_0FUqG2xfTqEmpJtAzZHDOpyic9WV6PDClt29yoXz62nA5eyZ7T8tSUOFoiP5fnccWxF83llVveMjapvptglUvpLiKNNs_8ElQvFroJ-PMfaDsdFc_oIlNh97yylyeJafZjtXJK2bb7idHyfp4mIeGRVZQXU2DttHM7zu1bx9p5ydPaMe8KHR9isv2xPalf8N_5U07UE0vgm7Rsw3-G8IAA56BtEeiGdFF0L9xYXNlDTUKevcLd1HGuHi7_0PUtUS_cE5e9zsjAo02O38Dh/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h31/h001.4USShqYbdDpp5lnOA4tGFs2p8rcQ-Kw7X0BUGyxbKTc) the Legislature declined to fund. State lawmakers would need to approve funding for the state to satisfy the latest judgment.

Brickman, former policy director at the attorney general’s office, said the appeal is another example of Paxton’s “brazen dishonesty and shameless abuse of taxpayer funds” for personal gain.

A spokesperson for Paxton’s office did not immediately respond to an emailed request for comment.

The whistleblowers accused Paxton of using his office to benefit **Nate Paul** , a real estate investor and campaign contributor. **“AG Paxton launches investigation into fluoride toothpaste marketing,** ” KVUE's **Johann Castro** — "Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** is launching an investigation into the makers of children's fluoride toothpaste over their marketing.

Paxton sent Civil Investigative Demands, or CIV's, to Procter & Gamble - who sells Crest - and Colgate-Palmolive - who sells Colgate, claiming that they illegally market their products to children.

The American Dental Association recommends no more than a pea-sized amount of fluoride for children between the ages of 3 and 6-years-old, but Paxton is claiming that the companies mislead parents to use far more than the recommended amount.

Paxton also said that the toothpaste manufacturers encourage kids to ingest toothpaste.

“I will use every tool available to protect our kids from dangerous levels of fluoride exposure and deceptive advertising,” Paxton said in a Thursday news release. “Toothpaste manufacturers must follow state law to ensure that they aren’t putting Texas families in peril through their false, misleading, and deceptive marketing, and these CIDs will help my office discover any potential wrongdoing."" * **2025**

**“San Antonio mayoral race heads to runoff between Ortiz Jones and Pablos** ,” via News 4 San Antonio — "The race for San Antonio's mayoral seat is heading to a runoff as former Under Secretary of the Air Force **Gina Ortiz Jones** and former Texas Secretary of State **Rolando Pablos** vie for the position.

This election marks the first time a mayor will serve a four-year term after voters approved longer terms last November. The overall turnout for the election was just 9.26%, the lowest since 2013.

Ortiz Jones captured nearly 27% of the votes, maintaining her lead from early voting numbers. Pablos received 17%.

Because no candidates collected 50% of the vote, Ortiz Jones and Pablos will meet in a runoff election on June 7.

Ortiz Jones, who chose to spend election night with her team rather than holding a watch party, expressed her determination to continue her campaign efforts.

"We'll continue to work hard. We run through the tape again. I've got the best team. We're very excited about our message, how it's resonating all across this city, and we'll continue knocking on those doors, making those phone calls and telling people what's possible, right with the right leader leading the city," she said.

“The fact that no sitting city council member finished in the top three positions tonight is a clear sign that San Antonio voters are sick of business as usual, and are ready to get down to business to secure a more prosperous future for our children and grandchildren," Pablos said in a statement. “San Antonio now has a clear choice, and I am confident they will reject Gina Jones for what she represents: more of the same, failed leadership from the San Antonio political machine that has left poverty rates in stagnation and caused businesses to pass over the Alamo city due to the radical, misplaced policy priorities that are completely out of line with the values of San Antonians.”

The winner of this year's election will also receive a salary increase to $87,800 per year, up from the nearly $62,000 salary previously paid to Mayor **Ron Nirenberg**." **“SpaceX workers in South Texas vote yes to create City of Starbase** ,” Texas Tribune's **Berenice Garcia** and Texas Newsroom's**Lauren McGaughy** — "**Elon Musk’s** wish to create his own city just came true.

On Saturday, voters living around SpaceX’s rocket testing and launch facility in South Texas approved a measure to incorporate the area as a new city. Soon after polls closed at 7 p.m., Cameron County election officials confirmed that overwhelming support among early voters clinched the election in favor of creating the City of Starbase.

Unofficial results later in the night showed the election was a landslide: 212 voted in favor; 6 opposed.

After the county certifies the results, the new city will be official.

Musk celebrated the vote Saturday night with a single word posted to X: “[S T A R B A S E](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.Tb7ejk-5QfVnOrZFvpt2rtYOAaA_94_ZOssrl7HzkU3GW5ubZ5ktmvyfu9648-4SBoo2uIBiGe6ZxfmvDrhzunzGTzCmKzx_YoTDbIjqP20_vcoqIGwLjIXMusmM-Va-UzYTwJkX_L5j4a7m5htI1eB1fUz_RvoLMd9L14MzJL4dCPVKSaxxv9KLUKtBrkRrncSqleNG6B7kUbO2tOzFlUYDuIEf7GoCNLbRGr9_ESTJtCh7mbVJSVGllZ7ZRHAgsTyI28OhYQwwx56_dBwQiaJ5q6b3UUZLC5TWVw3rLPaPzyHPXiN8FjtggowFCBPtKRnYIQSkFk0E3z0MoNEDKtdeuJwZBW1BsTQebkMj2x9k9w1RD1JXms4-ld8WGm4pQhQN8KUM6qgDGkvNeNLUanbTX-aoxU8BwYyVR3jU8FvGLwRosvw5Uj5aSpi56noMrwnujrjTy4xadyqsrKPMBgnMsreDUNEpfka9EGeUQhtzBZpFYU9hRwQuJ-KddSScC2216EYU8Jx642nsTpjbVQ/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h43/h001.nSTvNZ44pJVO31OxeWHho0utQLSJ8vL1e2eALLtXnGY).”

But there were no signs of a celebration at Starbase itself. After a gray, rainy Election Day, the soon-to-be-city had a quiet night. No fireworks. No street parties. A few folks gathered at the Astropub, a bar for SpaceX employees, late into the evening.

Only 283 people, those who live within the boundaries of the proposed city, were eligible to vote in the election. A Texas Newsroom analysis of the voter rolls showed two-thirds of them either [work for SpaceX](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eGNY5-4woGKqHvA-kxLUX-x7WKeMKPIkiIR0X1hNSwRatmoo2Q49EVynJqF_jUJIum_O6E9HMGhYSENsHeixyG5NOxj2OXOx2IVcQvlTOzttPQArk7LMDipHYVp-XX78syDObBzdfOBLTU35ZZkRe2-ny2rWOV0dv8MotOos-3rfO3SB-NL8SwbBaT0jiDbeeXvs7YC-5Z58vHYKne749t4tUlHUj7JUh_w4PvzSCgFwMlQQIRfcZxfWJ_XbqahudHbxZhDoEBt5yPzWL9HtWagPD1_LI3xsna9zf1NSYFkIKuPWjJC6klV2m3w6c0NVWsQTvbHgBFu_yy9CIYpSF4BWC34enww3ofE-3xXCXek1Gjad6PRtv-hOhOQ6nwL-mHM71MspiXicK3p6c-SjBi_ShjQaxPBPTkYTqw3JN4efszvm1iDl5Ib5aB488Zi59qU2xYhjwdxNtaLEOpEcDoqlqevDvNh9jeBJJXjWNyl4EdrE2DBkYyqFK6kNqRH4yLckViOe52m0SWbXx8C-VapWoDYGvePjgQPWVbv9tMkTK-OvRAMFZIbrxLzww_k48Q/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h44/h001.PVY9K3-lYXV1ldP8i5fgtA1ckCX4J7HlDuevaqQjHtw) or had already indicated their support.

**“Elon Musk’s attorney John Bash drops out of Texas Attorney General race** ,” KXAN's **Adam Schwager** — "**John Bash’s** campaign to replace **Ken Paxton** as Texas Attorney General lasted for just 21 days.

* **TEXANS IN DC**

**“Chip Roy steps up pressure on Congress to cut Medicaid expansion,** ” San Antonio Express-News' **James Osborne** — "U.S. Rep. **Chip Roy** is ramping up [pressure on House Republicans](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.Tb7ejk-5QfVnOrZFvpt2rrTJcCxzOSoBqziLInfPA1jnh7GxepuHk-4YBXtJ9B-dhz-dnpKM95gOA2GOXar_65i2utGt90i3dVg-uhyDVBuqTMj_0aDi073yxWQ07i7J5jbWhCF1415-JmqOLxLKFQZlBkCREG1MZBFFiBqQMqiUZse0yUxUqS7DkyG_HTdKukSPNrL04gmDUpKHvYjh-2zGKRyBQVXHFsJvX-jU_tcsXrH34s_oj2iy6zijbT4JC3iXho0gtMckWj4PbjDtIbZgJlW6SuGtqDsRrrJxOULgzgbYnZUBQW6FfrW9QPAOiOycBGbj4BZ8KntWQl7oHhvbtjHSCEA3M4SQMg8MdimBptV5fwXTCmd908wWorXWFTPj-gR6TWYd_gajY6pc5y_4jlunZghrqhyJNjZoqpJHYmpR06_nTOPrbTT1-5koyuGNpF4dOwuA2ZdwMkKn5443n0X07zY6D43Ob9bGdQ2pfOWeU2iGRdfNShgiHAox8OnrVdcHwM4QIItl5tfQiQ/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h54/h001.RalwMwulU-4AnhuP-pMTSL51EXHIgednYetat1lWGUU) to make the larger cuts to Medicaid that many within his party oppose.

In a letter to members on Thursday, Roy and 19 other House Republicans, including U.S. Reps. **Keith Self** and **Michael Cloud** of Texas, called for pulling back on the expansion of Medicaid benefits to low-income working adults that Congress passed during the Obama administration and that have been adopted in nearly every state.

"If the House budget reconciliation package does not include structural Medicaid reform... we will be setting up massive tax increases and benefit cuts in the future," they wrote.

Medicaid expansion covers adults who make up to 138% of the federal poverty rate, or about $21,600 in 2025. States that opt into the program contribute a small percentage of the costs, with the federal government picking up the rest.

Roy's push to return Medicaid to a program solely for low-income children, pregnant women, seniors and people with disabilities comes as Republicans are searching for ways [to slash $1.5 trillion](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI51J14spDzdVoLQq-qj1UyJn1MWLuBfFnnUcQrHTZW_x6T2zc4t-9ymltRMr8Cw95FcVi5nWOJ3v_TvXWLeVfwbmpq6avVEpdYuo5p60QXRHkWoSclcUtQD_lZ4WnHHc3ii16na3OTH4NkwPsvbRWJWGbqPZdzd64KiU8zAuYzyyG_kNVPq8ianoP6ojDAkT_oLIFXqBI0LU8_gWCDKupr1PZCiF1DXDUVu9NDCR8mHGieDPehFa5xru9GuS7qloBH6nErmi_0HOK8EUMOBFGP0MO8lYQc2gDOx6dRcK2XquTOzJuyqAlkWNAB8kCnXd4aPPdD8m5SnNe4NzHM64R9ZHD3XzU3tc7rnKAGmfsqyIroCTAH36GDVFhgHp_epTzs8TAfiLchKjvETLflC2aSsLtyU6A_QRPgKICco3auVHJJduA3F7ADrQCCIQWlaBZrFMexXoM9i8KDlnOzXKszEO9eee307IKnYrCLdxXuCNnuA0V8wldaYKGmZbO3tFrim9LoFh7mgMa_E45ygTxVK/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h55/h001.N_3V_yYAyynzybJ2Uu9VCh7PQuFgNg4_yaGTAUL53Mg) from the federal budget.

House GOP members are divided over Medicaid expansion and other benefits for low-income families. U.S. Rep. **Tony Gonzales** , the San Antonio Republican whose district includes much of the border, who leads the GOP's Congressional Hispanic Conference, [has warned members](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eLA4v3xJ7EFyLSWgpDk4sY8mqtnrTBU_XFEIkrjM9FVdX1GxPi0Bx_QHk7w75e2Al_1XBWx9LSNCNEl67zOcDwG56arO347h3obgh7H8aeEQd5h9b0l9yeC87sIfIm-ct62Iz-B74JIClPxqGU7DQbCT-biei6UMPvzcoQ8EFv6y7VbPuYxutReNabRvmU96XFTtGmf1vmTxeqttpmWK91v0zma-FY3KWpm6dLPFUyy01zvtQpzb0hc45-q_PtZUqXIfeYQly5OB4k59gv6Vw9BGMA7wcu2a9SMfjPoIpM2108iQ0gw00Z7z6L0xcDiGMgAyABfZnAW7jwI9mY8DPv2S3bdlWDkwdJppmp087OrQUIEzVgThu-v48U5Zvuf3Wg4L_fHkMQp0_4MhjCLKSq7EjIT02j0Pw7qUSo3QX9Z5VmlDgj4i6IAKvtDcuN95sNh53MOU5oFnNFzQsCdzZxs7oQu74CUSucVxaa_Z1x0nivIv-m37wPNTE_FlVAApOzsaIga8tDatC7qS0ZcP4LCq-VDKVyUXbO1N4SBLvbi-/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h56/h001.JT1Udy-FIRDWTwU-5HMTwPMrUd-toktfe0IGYzp3Vrw) that they face political fallout in cutting programs that are "helping hard working Americans."

Gonzales did not respond to a request for comment Friday.

The House Energy and Commerce Committee, which oversees Medicaid, has already delayed a hearing on the $780 billion in spending cuts they have been directed to come up with**** by House Leadership.

Ending the federal match for Medicaid expansion would save the government $626 billion over the next decade, according to [analysis by the health research non-profit KFF](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eA8av93YlDSqW_CuGfBYDkb7xfNCmhUsiTat9R4cRs0ca5IYvEMYls9O5C84zxF8522-6fX7WXaIJHUGAhhlkfgI5I4SngNX6jHPi6CzjU4_lZfFc5uhbvLia1r-BD6dOC0ZE5qUkEdaXUqxB6JT9Dc42RbhDi3mW3X64MwTksrpXBcSDFAKff3WyoH-qm11D-7ORovtvL9n4XEyY2RZ4plbhcIrygsC99oEyQp4wyBpOXxkt4hWn2ukHQpews00HCo8g2roYXYlVKROtQsn7AWF4Noq0UydNGOj8gUUyVBd6qnBD0OXUtF-2cuUSdJ7NPLlkhQ-c0H4dUM1KQ5ZGNzLbx--SM8mlOFNEB0nZPuO9QExm3_y-Q-DluoBqJ420kssXNcv8Y-kwRBBDMiX4z3gfk1o2C5qYYAKOjWBsOK9PbQ55a8TXUi7MvNet3INl3JCJNfp0-8XWCjtO9j2f5sAj23ABhQ0yAGdStCdqDxpuFoSFrGFDQVdFieX129-XyZgK0M7LIgOzr05eFFsO33cy109ozUbeVckQAxnEtZ2WIyYkOkm25O6T3Lpawd72TNG2GD1c7-lHRifUHaobU09boOYaPGvP-Y31Qs1Avujx83lsmETq_GRBgwG1y5bZQnsmNeEE2SVXekcoGXd0ebSySOl56EFP_2o-_TmsrCprbAJJadurLjmhmzVURhIlymOYvt34F237D0teHYUQtE/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h57/h001.rJnD0lcwZi0rg73dETGslc_7JYs6tXOJJQQA-A1wVEI). And unless states decided to make up the loss, an estimated 20 millions Americans would lose their healthcare coverage.

Texas is one of ten states that [chose not to expand Medicaid ](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eK32fBXtX6uRCvVTzZ89TSt52QHvWyldx11BkklckbprD2x0WszVy9hxW4U1y0cP-UaV1I3jjD_jOKc9vJ7H4UNwZyGddRLdwTuBFyNzTK6HY53XeQuwPFAaIGddBNeTigPLIr-kyOSMltLUqE13OkNNxIs-atmVJmXQ9w4hkVbC31koEyZLQROnMINaVBxbQRZCHLXy4MeiaMgk9XQ3e660N0froTumWtKe2fxHa6KxvouUmBlpkcBK2JnU0JVckwK5jBywyCtpW623q3zjV1_KvZ8yPXRJeLTYna6I-P-P1apmwzeVpa17kbr5PYooCt4EgJHp1FFFJDRnJxLTjDOp8ZGdNMhLCxfsC4x1Mr6GXT8TM1hTThg72HHUWTX_RLbpVNa5Fuv0YA2WtXrjCbgsoDA7k6JQTXZ_Kd-QfrK-0zlOsNHh1dBnXSMM8GwFlsesALhIEaeswtsNqWthD0v7rSiF_dg3yjdZKUM6a9Saoi86lapUA_QfEq9P8lowXXMBAvQTE_pbXUUfqFQBIyfSIeWdrArDIvqI8bfXK88psK3GVdJxLGBDpUEcDy-B8LKzd5ov28aDllylMw1Wqi6zHsPknBSXYEVuWG4V1oi_c3xKYWl-X7M76vyuOZ_GB2oi488UDegC7jMOmkso6mXpmQxIzXgKCA-gLnI8ErltOwSvVh4opAMzbZmjC5GUz7FyXH_mBrvaxIVPtlIC0q9Mi7ic0i_gH4pCjGvMnqh-9U1TFfcoRCktu64-PnkPAg/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h58/h001.uBq-0d2MnoGV28PoZ9fuhgTyiiNQ_GiHD_wRkUFl1ms)to low-income Americans. But for Republicans representing states that did expand coverage, cutting federal funding has so far proven a difficult sell.

"I will not support any cuts to eligible legal individuals or facilities," U.S. Rep.**Jeff Van Drew** , R-NJ, told CNN Thursday. "With those types of issues, we're affecting real people and real lives, and that's where we have to be careful."

Roy, who has long warned of the dangers of continuing to run up the national debt, cautioned Republicans that if they wanted to continue with President**Donald Trump 's** plans to extend income tax cuts they would need to address Medicaid sooner or later. In the letter on Thursday,**** he pointed to [the Congressional Budget Office](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eGtegXotf0yEVw7zmyjmKTTCNWPL9SHNRMBL4SCTr_8wa43x6V6Ec_EJU7grQvTHnQDjl7IGyLLJ-6G8TWu3hxv9unymOpiA08CStPj0bC0NfefLZbCmm5JEWBJ7CfkJTeWF9RWkNhyrKq-mKhdt1OTTNHWccD8jfqXbW1vU31HZZ8D75c4qLjtlRRzJmLDZI-6e7f94khmkHa0zvrlyT1K5DQ4FIZHrYs7yoFC6_SgdXXn19CyOg1yOfHR7W6Dk7eRgZTSUwp9NSzvfWc4n_dCK-Ke2TWFgrh-vY6hkdjOhfeNYN8Wu8D6CmmGsJ1Vygv0up4WWXkhMKRIc3QFKZ-vvBZaARDdfJ18dcnB0ihuLVTNxklkPYeBGbe40KPtJUhoxNBWkgbEwXBLis2TaMkpATAnnkQ-57Cv4oUaT0I_1yHwGd9nX9TI3R_AqNyfREspCc1ZzoX2-0C7f_jacSOX2WsQWrKrt_YJVEJOFCY6G7mhAT7GVVVjbeuhLT-2z6Y8I09NIKzML-_gkIEpkSbs/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h59/h001.uIOB49T2WFytX6wrQqkijQs7djApRIP85ztosCs5ghA)'s estimate that, without changes, Medicaid spending would increase by $1.2 trillion over the next decade.

* **BUSINESS NEWS**

**“Elon Musk adviser launches three Mars-themed companies in Texas** ,” San Antonio Express-News' **Brandon Lingle** — "Three ventures with a Mars theme are the latest offshoots of **Elon Musk 's** ever-growing Texas empire.

The new entitites — Red Planet Ventures I, II and III — were formed in late February by **Jared Birchall** , according to filings with the Texas Secretary of State. Birchall, who is CEO of Musk’s family office and holds leadership roles in several other of his companies, registered an Austin address and listed himself as manager of the new limited liability companies.

They join Musk’s other firms based in Texas, which include Tesla and xAI in Austin, [SpaceX](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eLA4v3xJ7EFyLSWgpDk4sY8ZxH9AT_caGIyjyacIkJhSoZiGY-x9j6s0glkDOg_PJVI6p0pNMKyP8rEKjkOGGh6r8-BU-H57mXzhKDjD3AdoAiUVAkIgD0sWfa-z6XtyaoCb47iKXoS-il9_0unlCvE1Tsu_S9RCRfQv6mRkZT2Npf9HTuT839k9fptcYtQNwTYwziewRMQnFYw4IrXYVgXP8Il2ycLQ8dX9Lo9N-JQnSJQjANt_JZxLoIzXQfC9zsDalLWtcNBdzV1T04lapWgf-8mQA4wdDRi_886wTPkmTD4Xtc6zf5WYILblR4Ato67U5tRHxm3mYM2buuIo_kpp1PiHZ_aPku3hLifaOvdE-husPc8p4d-6spIGchaYfWKB64Yto7yYQgAIRTmW5cJzLt9m_krM4pN5ifWToAhILWAlE_E8qIc5J1vaVIVgntH5b2PHwsN0pEb6GVISePuVg1tQA8xZVX3RFgUDp9wBxFud-gNenZmApAX8X6OhtFE8pB9itJRk1YHc5lfXiVRC9y3d96EN3OW20AiR7veFU6svevUyy9sqcAt8DpMxmw/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h63/h001.9aJauPMJhe5lWECwBbDRZB7hAvEzhhyGURVslT6HlOw) outside Brownsville, X and the Boring Co. in Bastrop. Neuralink, Musk’s brain implant company, has labs and offices in Del Valle, east of Austin, but its headquarters is in Fremont, Calif.

Little is known about the Red Planet trio other than their name is in step with Musk’s dreams of Mars colonization. Their formation mirrors that of other holding companies he’s created when making business moves, most notably the purchase of social media platform Twitter in 2022.

At that time, he formed X Holdings I, II and III to facilitate the purchase of the San Francisco-based company, which he quickly renamed X and [relocated to Texas](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eLA4v3xJ7EFyLSWgpDk4sY8ZxH9AT_caGIyjyacIkJhSPtKuQSl96mf4r_1P3Kv6llgzkbs3FVfp7WFjiwNb7-FDLlDPR_dmIq7mIvHiOA-DmBp7Wmee9uCfS77YL4zOEOrrMIsQAFYpyYsdVD5yqmCP_5dLF8HeY6sAr0o8bJwnvAKzCKMVVA7Obt_U0I8DIItDdb6cVVBRpLpXIUIgOmfMp-MWFcG04J7zIaLA1AyoAwH-uTCMc4nHPF3eiqDMrKPBpsf3PPXigZAlkMl06OSOJe9lx15Fxz6vxk98zJDa9X1KXp2HCBofML-a9jTIUumFQ2wsYhhnK0_XaNl-NdnI6SmTSkI_7wvy1J21SOVpRx-PHeBhs0MeIQGEdGoNtQh2KQYD42Io7Zb3gc3tcIrcXKJOoK90TGc2d3Xoy7d5lyeS9cWFpgm0h8C5vTm6qNCus7UivGuFpmHb9yYdeXulqmpOiV1PqYEzmFDpIHm_dZUrRmKpI2PbolsnUVttZismXLUViL2QjOj2vs4u4tuEmnqWum6ilWFPQBDGLrauKATmCM81Q-I7NCSciUM25Q/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h64/h001.NtA4IXlMcRpvxEIHttkwGepV2qSiC8Mh4miwneRsrgk).

In March, xAI, Musk’s artificial intelligence startup, bought X in an all-stock deal. He said the deal values xAI at $80 billion and X at $33 billion. Saying the companies’ futures are “intertwined” Musk said the merger was a “step to combine the data, models, compute, distribution and talent.”

There’s much overlap across Musk’s enterprises and some analysts see them as all contributing to his vision of putting a human civilization on Mars.

Musk wants to send uncrewed Starships, currently under development at SpaceX’s South Texas Starbase, to the Red Planet [as soon as late next year](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.Tb7ejk-5QfVnOrZFvpt2rvLK6Wx0xurO5BDFiEA0r9lY3s2FUL6db5lgNsWra5edLhvKdp2Op-hmlo5RpmqpIzE5HNdfDnHyECMxz4SaNhBWCYWcrd0TOEt2nDBRtFLP7rncS9bLp7OWL1ckzhtsM6fFzHxQG9U0mqJM8jydlnp8QjL5Jc4YyExIZMInnbYxgeN7N6GwIDvHKWevX1pYfEMvpu37rJk9B3fIZfti_wbUffnuqgsWQIMS4-1UxOfhQrk8ciToxY5bnPC34s_J9fMW3OAPZ_XAJHxA8Vvs-gmvBkeJcY08l7-x0pbPUExXpdVqnhPendaSdYC6vF3H9Vha3iUhATmSBaaayb5OLRL-bi1ruOzU3OfDnyvUZRWJrAkr7lr5uT92yqNR6G96m3ygy4EUXibrLms0ViDnkc4YMWbHIOa3yTW_s8e2NhjOsfHQG67VjmfE448-Oo2u6N-aHiY_-jFLAVYqseQLH0KpHtpno3AVfUlqEP61TfSnUU0nwHI2oeCOBNLb6Qt6Tw/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h65/h001.DfjjH7IECAGzOAjLjWfE-XyKhw6UXs4Y2X318z4Tiw0).

“If those landings go well, then human landings may start as soon as 2029, although 2031 is more likely,” he said in a post on X. And xAI’s quick-growing AI models are likely to help all his projects, including space exploration.

The mystery of the Red Planet Ventures LLCs follows Musk’s habit of using webs of shell companies when he’s got something new cooking.

SpaceX gobbled up swaths of land in South Texas under affiliates [Dogleg Park LLC](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eLA4v3xJ7EFyLSWgpDk4sY8ZxH9AT_caGIyjyacIkJhS1EmMjeKdZxcDZLCBRzQTK1O8-slx0e37f_ChCmhU2o7DrzWaDxa7cv1PqHCRn4y_Lmh17FBSCRCMBodBQ2b4tAWTTtFViKJGv0RyJZICLulqBISqnY4RRywbnbWtnHt75fmsxStt1x-3xxcBvK0zAC-57Gx89hXQ6KCsqjZTF5-ltTKF8pqVIs9fZm_3IqFKstbFzhFrCnFgcI9qUf_P-MONeJyIu3QCzVVWTvDFQz_dNk_qA42aJ-S-Z089eb865NODw5RuI8MNHxp7el0y1UjI9_rCBWyto5pe4AMBh_FGD_VtuW4srof1A24awfQXk0YCPGJR5Imv8zBTeT6xWQfNcnUR_t01Ys77B02Fg6AMzSE7WfR7Xg0naZOiOS0lZfZKcEP9pUSpaVECAVHYM0mcm4Bkd5tFuKdBXFH9eOoqnkc4M4llNThdPwsg35I7nyp1ce1Wk3Uv64zE_5hpyCaTLzRaMSYMYMGjFECZCXpHCljpmHBKJKC2ryNqrTdLLrJuAPonQZ7CC4FGYLaFUw/4g8/8v_gybbxTg2rbZvXlvZnHg/h68/h001.S1_Q0rseoBfXn-yldYBpq944wHZwu98c2r3tsEmN7YY) and Flats at Mars Crossing. The Boring Co. operates under Gapped Bass LLC in Bastrop and Tesla developed its Gigafactory outside Austin as the Colorado River Project LLC.

Birchall is listed as CEO of Neuralink, and he holds titles at the Boring Co., xAI and the Musk Foundation. He’s helped Musk create various entities including the family office, which is known as Excession, xAI, the Ad Astra school and Horse Ranch LLC.

He also was an advisor to the Trump transition team, according to the New York Times." Each day, the newsletter unpacks one important news story, examining it from all sides of the political spectrum. | TOP NEWS "Texas governor signs $1 billion voucher bill in milestone for school choice supporters nationally ,” AP's Nadia Lathan \-- "Texas will implement program, one of the largest in the country, that uses public dollars to fund private school tuition under a bill Gov. Greg Abbott signed Saturday, capping off a yearslong effort by Republicans. School voucher supporters have long targeted the state, where past efforts buckled for decades against resistance from Democrats and rural Republicans. Last month President Donald Trump called lawmakers before a key vote needed to finally get the bill to Abbott’s desk. Texas that have implemented a similar program, of which about a dozen have launched or expanded their programs in recent years to make most students eligible. School vouchers have been Abbott’s primary focus this year, coming off a 2024 election cycle in which he led a campaign to oust GOP lawmakers who voted against a similar bill last session. Republican lawmakers and bill supporters say it will give parents more choice by letting them pull their children out of poor-performing public schools. “Gone are the days that families are limited to only the schools assigned by government,” Abbott said before signing the bill at the governor’s mansion. “The day has arrived that empowers parents to choose the school that is best for their child.” Democrats and Republicans in rural districts have criticized the program, saying it will drain financial resources from Texas’ more than 5 million public school students and subsidize the private education of wealthy families. Beginning next school year, Texas families can receive $10,000 per year to help pay for students’ private school tuition. Children with disabilities can qualify for as much as $30,000 a year. The program will be capped at $1 billion for the first year and cover up to 90,000 students but could cost up to $4.5 billion a year by 2030." | PLEASE NOTE : You are receiving the PAID subscriber version of Must Read Texas — FOR TODAY ONLY — so you can SEE what you’re missing as a FREE subscriber. » Become a PAID subscriber . | "University of Texas chancellor is named president of the University of California ,” AP's Nadia Lathan \-- "James B. Milliken , who has led major public university systems in New York and Texas, was named president of the University of California on Friday. Milliken comes to the role from the University of Texas, where he has served as chancellor since 2018. He has also held leadership roles at The City University of New York, the University of Nebraska and the University of North Carolina. The UC system has 10 campuses, including Berkeley, Los Angeles and Davis. Milliken starts on Aug. 1. He will be paid nearly $1.5 million a year. “The University of California is universally regarded as the preeminent public research university in the world, and I am deeply honored to have an opportunity to join the many talented faculty, staff, and campus leaders in their vital work,” Milliken said in a UC press release. “It is more important than ever that we expand the education, research, health care, and public service for which UC is so widely admired and which has benefited so many Californians.” The announcement comes at a time when the Trump administration is at universities in an attempt to get them to comply with the Republican president’s political agenda. The University of California, Berkeley, is among by the U.S. Education Department over its ties to The PhD Project, a group aimed at diversifying the business world and higher education faculty. The Trump administration is also investigating Berkeley linked to pro-Palestinian protests against the war in Gaza that broke out last year across campuses nationwide. In response, Berkeley said it has “an unwavering commitment” to fighting antisemitism. The Trump administration also into three University of California campuses over their admissions policies to determine whether they comply with the U.S. Supreme Court’s in college admissions. Milliken succeeds Michael V. Drake as UC president. Drake was the first Black person to serve in the role in the system’s more than 150-year history. Drake announced last year that he would step down after serving as president since 2020, leading the system through the coronavirus pandemic, a graduate student strike and various campus protests." "Severe storms and heavy rains expected across Texas this week as omega block hovers ,” Austin American-Statesman's Brandi D. Addison andJohn Bacon \-- "A pair of storm systems could fuel across sections of the nation from the Desert Southwest to the East Coast this week, and some areas are bracing for the possibility of tornadoes or hail the size of tennis balls, forecasters said. “A slow-moving storm over the Central United States this week will act as the catalyst for a risk of severe thunderstorms and flooding rainfall,” Meteorologist Brandon Buckingham said. A congested atmospheric pattern is to blame. The pattern forms a jet stream shaped like the Greek letter omega (Ω), often trapping a high-pressure system over the northern Plains, flanked by two closed low-pressure systems. As the block shifts or breaks down, those nearby low-pressure systems can bring stormy weather to the region. Severe storms and heavy rainfall are expected across the state early this week as the omega block looms over the United States. Portions of West Texas and far eastern New Mexico could see large hailstones and possibly isolated tornadoes late on Monday, AccuWeather warned. Travelers along Interstate 10 and 20 in Texas could face travel delays as storms ignite across the region. By Tuesday, a heavy rain and thunderstorm pattern will sweep across eastern Texas, Oklahoma, Arkansas and Louisiana. Intense winds, hail, isolated tornadoes and flash flooding are possible Tuesday and Tuesday night, AccuWeather said. In Central Texas, including Austin and San Antonio, severe weather is expected to arrive midday, with a brief pause overnight before storms resume tomorrow morning and continue into the evening. “The main threats are large hail, damaging wind gusts, and heavy rain, with an isolated tornado also possible,” according to the National Weather Service office in Austin. Meanwhile, showers and storms are expected in the Dallas–Fort Worth area this afternoon, though the notes that “severe weather is not anticipated with this activity.” Like Central Texas, the Dallas–Fort Worth area is expected to see more storms on Tuesday, with the potential for large hail, damaging winds, a few tornadoes, and flash flooding. The greatest risk for severe weather is expected between 11 a.m. and 5 p.m. Tuesday. Similar conditions are expected across much of the state, from West Texas in the Permian Basin to East Texas and up into the Panhandle, as the storm system gradually moves from the Rockies and Great Plains into the southeastern U.S. Omega blocks can persist for several days to over a week, leading to prolonged periods of unusual weather, whether that means heat, dryness, or storms, depending on the location within the pattern. Local forecasts show storms moving out of Texas by the end of this week, however." ($) | STATE GOVERNMENT “Texas AG Ken Paxton appeals $6.7M whistleblower judgment, ” Dallas Morning News' Nolan D. McCaskill — "A whistleblower who was awarded part of a nearly $6.7 million judgment last month saidKen Paxton is appealing the award to stall the plaintiff’s victory while the Texas attorney general runs for U.S. Senate. A to four whistleblowers who reported Paxton to law enforcement in 2020 for alleged criminal conduct. After the ruling, Paxton vowed to appeal. An attorney representing Paxton’s office filed the appeal Friday in Travis County district court. The judgment awarded compensation for lost wages, pain and suffering, attorney fees, court costs and interest forJames “Blake” Brickman , David Maxwell , Mark Penley and Ryan Vassar. The former Paxton aides sued the agency in November 2020 under the Whistleblower Act after their firings. The law prohibits retaliation against public employees who report a violation of law in good faith. The judgment is more than double the the Legislature declined to fund. State lawmakers would need to approve funding for the state to satisfy the latest judgment. Brickman, former policy director at the attorney general’s office, said the appeal is another example of Paxton’s “brazen dishonesty and shameless abuse of taxpayer funds” for personal gain. A spokesperson for Paxton’s office did not immediately respond to an emailed request for comment. The whistleblowers accused Paxton of using his office to benefit Nate Paul , a real estate investor and campaign contributor. Paul was after to a federal charge of making false statements to a financial institution. In return, the whistleblowers alleged, Paul renovated Paxton’s Austin home and employed a woman Paxton was reportedly seeing. by the Texas House in 2023 but after a two-week trial. The impeachment process cost Texas taxpayers $5 million, . In a statement last month, Paxton referenced his acquittal and the cost of impeachment. He said a “liberal judge” wants taxpayers to waste more money on “a ridiculous judgment that is not based on the facts or the law.” In November, in the whistleblower case after agreeing in January 2024 to end the lawsuit and . Paxton against U.S. Sen. John Cornyn last month. “Despite saying he would not appeal, Paxton is now doing exactly that,” Brickman said. “This appeal has nothing to do with the facts of the whistleblowers’ claims or how the law applies — it is only to delay the finality of our victory while Paxton runs for higher office.”" ($) “AG Paxton launches investigation into fluoride toothpaste marketing, ” KVUE's Johann Castro — "Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is launching an investigation into the makers of children's fluoride toothpaste over their marketing. Paxton sent Civil Investigative Demands, or CIV's, to Procter & Gamble - who sells Crest - and Colgate-Palmolive - who sells Colgate, claiming that they illegally market their products to children. The American Dental Association recommends no more than a pea-sized amount of fluoride for children between the ages of 3 and 6-years-old, but Paxton is claiming that the companies mislead parents to use far more than the recommended amount. Paxton also said that the toothpaste manufacturers encourage kids to ingest toothpaste. “I will use every tool available to protect our kids from dangerous levels of fluoride exposure and deceptive advertising,” Paxton said in a Thursday news release. “Toothpaste manufacturers must follow state law to ensure that they aren’t putting Texas families in peril through their false, misleading, and deceptive marketing, and these CIDs will help my office discover any potential wrongdoing."" | 2025 “San Antonio mayoral race heads to runoff between Ortiz Jones and Pablos ,” via News 4 San Antonio — "The race for San Antonio's mayoral seat is heading to a runoff as former Under Secretary of the Air Force Gina Ortiz Jones and former Texas Secretary of State Rolando Pablos vie for the position. This election marks the first time a mayor will serve a four-year term after voters approved longer terms last November. The overall turnout for the election was just 9.26%, the lowest since 2013. Ortiz Jones captured nearly 27% of the votes, maintaining her lead from early voting numbers. Pablos received 17%. Because no candidates collected 50% of the vote, Ortiz Jones and Pablos will meet in a runoff election on June 7. Ortiz Jones, who chose to spend election night with her team rather than holding a watch party, expressed her determination to continue her campaign efforts. "We'll continue to work hard. We run through the tape again. I've got the best team. We're very excited about our message, how it's resonating all across this city, and we'll continue knocking on those doors, making those phone calls and telling people what's possible, right with the right leader leading the city," she said. “The fact that no sitting city council member finished in the top three positions tonight is a clear sign that San Antonio voters are sick of business as usual, and are ready to get down to business to secure a more prosperous future for our children and grandchildren," Pablos said in a statement. “San Antonio now has a clear choice, and I am confident they will reject Gina Jones for what she represents: more of the same, failed leadership from the San Antonio political machine that has left poverty rates in stagnation and caused businesses to pass over the Alamo city due to the radical, misplaced policy priorities that are completely out of line with the values of San Antonians.” The winner of this year's election will also receive a salary increase to $87,800 per year, up from the nearly $62,000 salary previously paid to Mayor Ron Nirenberg." “SpaceX workers in South Texas vote yes to create City of Starbase ,” Texas Tribune's Berenice Garcia and Texas Newsroom'sLauren McGaughy — "Elon Musk’s wish to create his own city just came true. On Saturday, voters living around SpaceX’s rocket testing and launch facility in South Texas approved a measure to incorporate the area as a new city. Soon after polls closed at 7 p.m., Cameron County election officials confirmed that overwhelming support among early voters clinched the election in favor of creating the City of Starbase. Unofficial results later in the night showed the election was a landslide: 212 voted in favor; 6 opposed. After the county certifies the results, the new city will be official. Musk celebrated the vote Saturday night with a single word posted to X: “.” But there were no signs of a celebration at Starbase itself. After a gray, rainy Election Day, the soon-to-be-city had a quiet night. No fireworks. No street parties. A few folks gathered at the Astropub, a bar for SpaceX employees, late into the evening. Only 283 people, those who live within the boundaries of the proposed city, were eligible to vote in the election. A Texas Newsroom analysis of the voter rolls showed two-thirds of them either or had already indicated their support. The three unopposed people who ran to lead the city also . It’s not clear if Musk, whose primary residence is here, cast a ballot. The vote clears the way for Musk to try to capture more , which must be closed for launches. On Saturday afternoon, a few dozen activists who say the company has harmed sensitive habitats and ignored the cultural importance of the land rallied on the beach — with an Elon Musk piñata in tow." | 2026 “Elon Musk’s attorney John Bash drops out of Texas Attorney General race ,” KXAN's Adam Schwager — "John Bash’s campaign to replace Ken Paxton as Texas Attorney General lasted for just 21 days. “Today, I’ve made the difficult decision to leave the race for Texas AG,” Wednesday evening. “On Friday, our family had a health scare that threw into sharp focus how I should prioritize my time right now, and that is not running for office.” Bash announced his campaign on April 9, drawing headlines not only for being the first major candidate to enter the race, but for his ties to billionaire Elon Musk. Bash currently Musk in a defamation case after Musk amplified social media posts falsely placing a California man at a clash involving far-right protesters near an Oregon Pride festival. Bash spoke with two weeks ago, highlighting his record as the former U.S. attorney for the Western District of Texas — contrasting it with the record of State Senator and oil and gas businessman Mayes Middleton , R – Galveston. “I’m not a career politician. I’ve never run for office before,” he said. “(Attorney General is) not a position you use to advance your political career. It’s a real working position that needs to come up with legal strategies to keep our citizens safe, to keep them prosperous.” In his post announcing the end of his campaign, Bash slightly changed his tune about Middleton. “The other candidate in the race, Senator , has served Texas honorably in the Legislature, and I know him to be a good man,” Bash wrote. Despite the compliments, he failed to endorse any candidates for the job. “I will be rooting for the next AG to represent our great State with distinction and to continue the impactful work of General .” With Bash out of the race, Middleton is the only person publicly running to replace Paxton. The election is in November 2026." | TEXANS IN DC “Chip Roy steps up pressure on Congress to cut Medicaid expansion, ” San Antonio Express-News' James Osborne — "U.S. Rep. Chip Roy is ramping up to make the larger cuts to Medicaid that many within his party oppose. In a letter to members on Thursday, Roy and 19 other House Republicans, including U.S. Reps. Keith Self and Michael Cloud of Texas, called for pulling back on the expansion of Medicaid benefits to low-income working adults that Congress passed during the Obama administration and that have been adopted in nearly every state. "If the House budget reconciliation package does not include structural Medicaid reform... we will be setting up massive tax increases and benefit cuts in the future," they wrote. Medicaid expansion covers adults who make up to 138% of the federal poverty rate, or about $21,600 in 2025. States that opt into the program contribute a small percentage of the costs, with the federal government picking up the rest. Roy's push to return Medicaid to a program solely for low-income children, pregnant women, seniors and people with disabilities comes as Republicans are searching for ways from the federal budget. House GOP members are divided over Medicaid expansion and other benefits for low-income families. U.S. Rep. Tony Gonzales , the San Antonio Republican whose district includes much of the border, who leads the GOP's Congressional Hispanic Conference, that they face political fallout in cutting programs that are "helping hard working Americans." Gonzales did not respond to a request for comment Friday. The House Energy and Commerce Committee, which oversees Medicaid, has already delayed a hearing on the $780 billion in spending cuts they have been directed to come up with** by House Leadership. Ending the federal match for Medicaid expansion would save the government $626 billion over the next decade, according to . And unless states decided to make up the loss, an estimated 20 millions Americans would lose their healthcare coverage. Texas is one of ten states that low-income Americans. But for Republicans representing states that did expand coverage, cutting federal funding has so far proven a difficult sell. "I will not support any cuts to eligible legal individuals or facilities," U.S. Rep.Jeff Van Drew , R-NJ, told CNN Thursday. "With those types of issues, we're affecting real people and real lives, and that's where we have to be careful." Roy, who has long warned of the dangers of continuing to run up the national debt, cautioned Republicans that if they wanted to continue with PresidentDonald Trump 's plans to extend income tax cuts they would need to address Medicaid sooner or later. In the letter on Thursday, he pointed to estimate that, without changes, Medicaid spending would increase by $1.2 trillion over the next decade. "The political abuse of this program is helping to bankrupt the federal government," the letter read. "For once, Congress should stop procrastinating, using excuses, and finally fulfill the Republican agenda."" ($) | BUSINESS NEWS “Elon Musk adviser launches three Mars-themed companies in Texas ,” San Antonio Express-News' Brandon Lingle — "Three ventures with a Mars theme are the latest offshoots of Elon Musk 's ever-growing Texas empire. The additions to the billionaire’s portfolio come amid a flurry of expansions and organizational changes in his other companies and talk of him stepping back from his role leading the Trump administration’s to focus on Tesla Inc., he leads, and his other companies. The new entitites — Red Planet Ventures I, II and III — were formed in late February by Jared Birchall , according to filings with the Texas Secretary of State. Birchall, who is CEO of Musk’s family office and holds leadership roles in several other of his companies, registered an Austin address and listed himself as manager of the new limited liability companies. They join Musk’s other firms based in Texas, which include Tesla and xAI in Austin, outside Brownsville, X and the Boring Co. in Bastrop. Neuralink, Musk’s brain implant company, has labs and offices in Del Valle, east of Austin, but its headquarters is in Fremont, Calif. Little is known about the Red Planet trio other than their name is in step with Musk’s dreams of Mars colonization. Their formation mirrors that of other holding companies he’s created when making business moves, most notably the purchase of social media platform Twitter in 2022. At that time, he formed X Holdings I, II and III to facilitate the purchase of the San Francisco-based company, which he quickly renamed X and . In March, xAI, Musk’s artificial intelligence startup, bought X in an all-stock deal. He said the deal values xAI at $80 billion and X at $33 billion. Saying the companies’ futures are “intertwined” Musk said the merger was a “step to combine the data, models, compute, distribution and talent.” There’s much overlap across Musk’s enterprises and some analysts see them as all contributing to his vision of putting a human civilization on Mars. Musk wants to send uncrewed Starships, currently under development at SpaceX’s South Texas Starbase, to the Red Planet . “If those landings go well, then human landings may start as soon as 2029, although 2031 is more likely,” he said in a post on X. They’ll carry the humanoid Tesla is developing to provide automated labor. Musk’s tunneling firm, the , has Mars applications, too, with technologies it says could help create underground habitats. And xAI’s quick-growing AI models are likely to help all his projects, including space exploration. The mystery of the Red Planet Ventures LLCs follows Musk’s habit of using webs of shell companies when he’s got something new cooking. SpaceX gobbled up swaths of land in South Texas under affiliates and Flats at Mars Crossing. The Boring Co. operates under Gapped Bass LLC in Bastrop and Tesla developed its Gigafactory outside Austin as the Colorado River Project LLC. Birchall is listed as CEO of Neuralink, and he holds titles at the Boring Co., xAI and the Musk Foundation. He’s helped Musk create various entities including the family office, which is known as Excession, xAI, the Ad Astra school and Horse Ranch LLC. He also was an advisor to the Trump transition team, according to the New York Times." Steve Daines

[**PITCH IN >> **](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=238d8a9d2fe74ed88e6ab3483b4f74cc&_e=uafGz1RBbJFQPCG9Gvie95Of2qIS8ARyKYEz1SuZoCcTxFOpQNj9b5SM1rBSGS6vieu8bpNrujo4pJItxDQNv-Sb-hGRNdfe4RVvXrfnTorO7vUpzaLMfICZ7V0pxnW_5-zjDDfGMJ0JbyqqflP3btVV6kHdh6RCS0zS5tDHYgzrfEFNWqyTBzvNtoJTv33fZHRc3mBa_Djp2VeNQnhrZFsD6woWVWmz01VKKbsagb4BmVKER6-pvKAL1WXa0ar0nB80fnRzNZ_EVohnn2dHZFwf0UV31szV5-M8e4oPl-wISzYKajq2ibtTuI56dy6TlVc1C-_0F2aTY8YS-aLDNydBfMJRUwJ0JX5Gjg5l3zgIby0XOLCxLH3rshQcOhPFyNBb25jeQSC9ngoA7HpsAw%3D%3D)

Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. clean_body: I’ll sum up my re-election fight for Montana’s battleground Senate seat in one sentence: | Fellow American, I’ll sum up my re-election fight for Montana’s battleground Senate seat in one sentence: If we lose this seat, we lose the Republican Senate Majority. This one simple reason is why Democrats poured millions into Montana’s 2024 election, and it’s the reason they’re getting ready to spend millions to defeat me in 2026. If we lose the Senate Majority, President Trump’s term will be cut in half! It doesn’t get much easier than this. For America, Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Senator Steve Daines | | Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

I’m proud that I don’t do a second of “call time.” **That’s because I want to be supported by those I’m representing in Congress.**

**I’m not a typical member of Congress, and my lived experience shapes the policy I make today.** But none of this is possible without supporters like you, chipping in one dollar at a time.

Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.

name: Sen. Steve Daines
subject: As clear as the nose on your face
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana
body:
I’ll sum up my re-election fight for Montana’s battleground Senate seat in one sentence: |
---
[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=72b2f1c7b4f44abd96ffac4f0e1bfdaa&_e=HXtmx73yL4OO4y_lnpk_9Yjr-3uZTnjkU0GvNH8Rpf3Greodg6Y7BxSy4tGJsaLU6G9qLx71qPvvuGs3dCaA4cuja6vGRLv6Ch3J7kYeKOgxhvKGzbuvOp4V0HTqjy-SwOA3Q92fj6JcJDM_SXEFDGinHdaNqqSHv7rJHR3IrMtuxv6cy_kUZ6Rxmqd6eybs4jQmKeU_ll7uJ_eDcNamyb74lQPo8Ao_Cg-UlBguPi3aZtngQ8pHjshVizrr49mO-KeK_yjbOphf-215EuBSeqIOxQ9M9_AyeHBCXO9AlSNrvAXjvASR9XUFprCw0mEOhVb7ZNoWV_ZWvTBasjYyriLZSjtK3a5r9pjoyEDpkT9IcNC_lb_Z_CHKn6RA9wC_AT_KDHJE089Fn2R8RZeICA%3D%3D) Fellow American,

I’ll sum up my re-election fight for Montana’s battleground Senate seat in one sentence:

_**If we lose this seat, we lose the Republican Senate Majority.**_
This one simple reason is why Democrats poured millions into Montana’s 2024 election, and it’s the reason they’re getting ready to spend millions to defeat me in 2026. _If we lose the Senate Majority, President Trump’s term will be cut in half!_

[**Donations like yours are the lifeblood of our campaign. clean_body: I’ll sum up my re-election fight for Montana’s battleground Senate seat in one sentence: | Fellow American, I’ll sum up my re-election fight for Montana’s battleground Senate seat in one sentence: If we lose this seat, we lose the Republican Senate Majority. This one simple reason is why Democrats poured millions into Montana’s 2024 election, and it’s the reason they’re getting ready to spend millions to defeat me in 2026. If we lose the Senate Majority, President Trump’s term will be cut in half! It doesn’t get much easier than this. For America, Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Senator Steve Daines | | Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

subject: Trump and Musk want to sell off the USPS!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund
body:
Postal workers and unions are sounding the alarm

| **USPS workers and unions sound the alarm:** They need _everyone_ to pay attention as Trump and Musk attempt to dismantle and privatize the USPS! [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107141082/543723637/1878703426?refcode=NDCAF_20250505_EMPET_E2_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3M2Y4MzYyOS1lMzI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_G9n5BgIT6uQYduMzYBi7VfG1phy7voZ9BBSL582KYE=&emci=c57fc843-c429-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=73f83629-e329-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=48524772) | [STAND WITH POSTAL WORKERS: DEMAND CONGRESS REJECT TRUMP’S PLAN TO PRIVATIZE THE USPS »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107141084/543723638/1878703426?refcode=NDCAF_20250505_EMPET_E2_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3M2Y4MzYyOS1lMzI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_G9n5BgIT6uQYduMzYBi7VfG1phy7voZ9BBSL582KYE=&emci=c57fc843-c429-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=73f83629-e329-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=48524772)
---
In March**, DOGE cut billions of dollars from the USPS budget and cut nearly 10,000 employees!**

Now, **Trump and Musk are quietly shopping the USPS to private investors and laying the groundwork to sell it off!**

**If privatized, essential USPS services could vanish or become outrageously expensive** — cutting off rural communities, seniors, veterans, and millions of Americans who depend on affordable, reliable mail.

We're expecting this to be one of the most expensive gubernatorial races in the country, that's why it's mission-critical for everyone who supports him to go on record. for Governor| | | | | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO 350 E 1st Ave Ste 100 Columbus, OH 43201 To unsubscribe from Vivek Ramaswamy for Ohio,

name: Lizzie Fletcher
subject: Thank you for standing up
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
A lot happened in the last month, in the last 100 days, and in the last year. And I want to thank you for what you have done in the last few months to respond.
We started April with President Trump imposing sweeping new tariffs—throwing markets into turmoil and making things more expensive and stressful for American families already struggling with rising costs. And we ended the month marking his first 100 days back in office—arguably the most damaging 100 days of any president in our history.
In just the last month, the Trump administration has:
* Fired thousands of federal health workers and scientists
* Created chaos at the Social Security Administration
* Deported people to another country without due process of law—including American citizens
* Attacked law firms, lawyers, and judges for following the law and supporting the rule of law
And through it all, House Republicans have rubber-stamped these unprecedented and unconstitutional actions, abandoning their own power and responsibility to their constituents.
Amid the chaos, though, we’ve seen powerful signs of hope and resistance:
* Democrats won a critical State Supreme Court race in Wisconsin.
* “Hands Off” demonstrations across the country—including the one I attended in Houston—became the largest day of protest yet against this administration’s policies and actions.
* And on National Law Day, I stood with lawyers from across the country to retake our oath—to support the Constitution, the rule of law, and an independent judiciary.
We have some big fights in the days and months ahead—like stopping the GOP’s devastating proposed budget cuts to Medicaid and other vital programs that people in our community and across the country rely on. But I know that, together, we can meet these challenges. And, as long as we remain engaged, focused, and strategic, we can not only win elections but win back the future for our country.
None of that happens without you. So thank you—for reading and sharing these emails, for contributing your time and money to these efforts, and for sharing your thoughts and priorities with me through the monthly survey.
Your engagement matters. Our democracy depends on it. And I’m so grateful to be in this fight with you.
Together, we can do anything,
Lizzie Signature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250505?refcode=lpfe250505]

VOLUNTEER [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Ln8e8kjn2Euuh3daVlmozQ2]
3262 Westheimer, PMB 636, Houston, TX 77098
PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
Don't want to receive these emails anymore? unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/yiT4vmtXrUCBvy-D5k60rg2?unsubscribedata=tdQfpVPrG3d%2FBZqzlLj4Rarm1ozwL2W1O09yxqw0LIJQfGz4J2B%2Fxa5xo2JAtaIlT02pzC58UP8UdrHeAxuDFyzIKjBjHCFtO5owpiyB3%2FT%2FmOkMbCzTc%2BybwOfctpMw8kafTKqqEa1xLPQJucivnhi6KR0q1RsJakJ8WZrWaC2dDwILM0ye3%2F%2B%2BvRRQXej0uK95oU%2F5R2zI%2BCgiuYbHVmMR7j2tg5GChSHuTJTVD3xTV%2BmiFFJodDCOmFBJICUuaGR9nkYvt9VaBLK1FbhFBlAAdipo7blMf11amwxLdl2ARD9rBlKwPksx8tzgXXvaBlhs%2BFTCnsOjbr76tv%2FwYT1358TH5Tgt5Qh0%2FdYVLeCdP4saJta3mQFqmhBNGjsRN4l89635SpsxeHfSMtEXRlGKzyrsdJtMmxloDZ3T3GQA%2FTYyArItqYCEht%2Bi8HFd www.lizziefletcher.com [https://lizziefletcher.com/]

name: NPR + PBS Protection Alert via Our Revolution
subject: PBS CEO: "There is no Plan B"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

Peter Willis,

🚨🚨Trump’s war on our public media went nuclear as he signed a terrifying
Executive Order demanding the Corporation for Public Broadcasting (CPB)
"cease federal funding for NPR and PBS." 🚨🚨

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26539?source=email-20250505&t=1011&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Headline: Trump signs executive order to stop federal funding for NPR
and PBS.

PBS’s CEO is sounding the alarm. She recently warned that "There is no
Plan B" if Trump succeeds in his dangerous demand for Congress to zero out
funding for public broadcasting.

Make no mistake: Defunding PBS and NPR will force countless local stations
to close. And many of the most vulnerable stations are in rural and
underserved areas, with populations that don’t have access to or can’t
afford cable or satellite.

Congress is feeling the heat and even a few Republicans recently expressed
concern. NOW is the moment to keep up the pressure and show Trump that his
ruthless attempt to kill public media won’t work.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26540?source=email-20250505&t=1012&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Will you urgently stand with PBS and NPR and tell Congress to step in
and tell Trump to keep his hands off our public media?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26540?source=email-20250505&t=1013&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]ADD YOUR NAME

For years, Trump has been hell bent on destroying NPR and PBS. He detests
the fact he can’t control the stories they publish – and without
independent media he’s free to spread whatever lies and disinformation he
likes.

He’s on a warpath to defund them and is using his latest Executive Order
to try to circumvent Congress. But he’s not stopping there. Last week, he
ALSO removed three of the five CPB board members. CPB is fighting back,
filing a lawsuit to stop this unprecedented attack on its editorial
independence. And we’re going to stand with them.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26539?source=email-20250505&t=1014&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Headline: Corporation for Public Broadcasting sues to block Trump from
firing 3 board members.

We WILL NOT allow Trump’s reckless political interference to undermine our
democracy's most trusted independent media sources. Congress works for
us—not Donald Trump, not special interests—and they need to know clearly
where we stand.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26540?source=email-20250505&t=1015&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Will you urgently add your name to show Congress that the public stands
with NPR and PBS and WILL NOT SUPPORT attempts to silence and defund our
public media?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26540?source=email-20250505&t=1016&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]ADD YOUR NAME

This fight isn't simply about television and radio – it's about protecting
our democracy's cornerstone institutions from partisan attacks. It's about
standing with independent journalism that prioritizes truth over profits,
children's development over corporate advertisers, and transparency over
political agendas.

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

Citizen Action Team

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Facebook | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10?t=1002&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Twitter | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/11?t=1003&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Instagram | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2355?t=1004&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Youtube | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2356?t=1005&akid=8404%2E5885482%2ERKee7A ]Website |

Friend, something has gone terribly wrong with our food assistance program in America. According to a recent study, more food-stamp money is being spent on soda and sweets than on healthy, nutritious foods! **If food stamps are being used to make Americans unhealthy, then the system isn't working.** Glenn wants to hear your thoughts - **[Should we limit the food and beverages that food stamps can buy?](http://links.arenawins.com/u/click?_t=f9ef163c38f94024905cc6b5e55f222e&_m=4fc7052e37c6433c8aa0f0c2a7c9abca&_e=TGab04m6dC0whL-J_HYh-RiqGTE0PjRcy5eYO2p-fEm713ko5qtlwTGlV_Oo8dYDYaiQRMFWLJ7Ns2_o-U-ugAEKNhcSYrNlKt1hbKiB-u7jW7j0Nol0HhD-CJhmGMVeXkrXyNbSvOLOHI_2oi-WiAfoFpTsbj8csdcP-B6lfwlkzOIYIDg-pyR5ZDdFI9UyGLF5v1VTKdos0NE0yOyTy6FzawnPXg0SScR06XWqWxPjQDqJE-wryOQ_1gJnmM6KhrpxCH-trDPCFujebpj6NPp_tI4YnGYF5ddZw13vsBzbGlmaztIRW9IU2u_OWtCkoPaRHtfheM6cw1xlUHucKA%3D%3D)** Thanks for your input, **Team Grothman**

subject: Judicial Watch Sues Defense Department for Records on 2021 Meeting Between Garland and Milley about President Trump
body:
Judicial Watch announced today it filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit against the U.S. Department of Defense for records regarding a reported 2021 meeting between then-Attorney General Merrick Garland and then-Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Mark Milley during which they discussed President Trump.

---
| | | Because no one is above the law! | May 5, 2025

### [Trump Justice Dept. to settle Babbitt family lawsuit in fatal Jan. 6 shooting ](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73784&L=35115&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvMTQ5NzM4LTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXRpcHNoZWV0JnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=c28111ef313f29f085bd58fef3f0c047658b58df0f6067cbb7d659a5fa22561e) The Washington Post The U.S. Justice Department has reached an agreement to settle the wrongful death case brought by the family of Ashli Babbitt, who was fatally shot by police in the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, lawyers for both sides told a federal judge Friday.

“We have reached a settlement in principle,” Justice Department civil attorney Joseph Gonzalez told U.S. District Judge Ana C. Reyes of Washington, D.C., who is hearing the family’s $30 million lawsuit against the government.

No final deal has been signed and terms have not been disclosed, said Robert Sticht, a lawyer for the conservative group Judicial Watch, which is representing Babbitt’s family. [READ MORE](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73784&L=35115&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvMTQ5NzM4LTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXRpcHNoZWV0JnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=c28111ef313f29f085bd58fef3f0c047658b58df0f6067cbb7d659a5fa22561e)

### [Persecuted Ex-Minister Romanowski to Lead Hungarian–Polish Institute of Freedom ](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73784&L=35116&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcGVyc2VjdXRlZC1leC1taW5pc3Rlci1yb21hbm93c2tpLXRvLWxlYWQtaHVuZ2FyaWFuLXBvbGlzaC1pbnN0aXR1dGUtb2YtZnJlZWRvbS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249dGlwc2hlZXQmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6a2b5f44f8810f12ed3ff0d79853b0ef48c700e6f6277d0f4e1bc7321f898717) The Hungarian Conservative Former Polish Deputy Justice Minister Marcin Romanowski has been appointed head of the newly established Hungarian–Polish Institute of Freedom. The announcement was made at a joint press conference in Budapest, featuring Romanowski; Miklós Szánthó, Director General of the Budapest-based conservative think tank, the Center for Fundamental Rights, which founded the new institute; and Chris Farrell, Director of Investigations and Research at the American conservative rights advocacy organization Judicial Watch. clean_body: Judicial Watch announced today it filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit against the U.S. Department of Defense for records regarding a reported 2021 meeting between then-Attorney General Merrick Garland and then-Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Mark Milley during which they discussed President Trump. | | | Because no one is above the law! | May 5, 2025 ---|---|--- | | | | RIGHTS AND FREEDOMS IN PERIL "When it comes to fighting for the American people’s ‘right to know,’ no one holds a candle to Tom Fitton and his team at Judicial Watch" \- Sean Hannity Tom Fitton returns with an exhaustive investigation into the progressive movement’s efforts to dismantle the venerable institutions of American rights and freedoms. Today's Top Stories | | | | | Judicial Watch announced it filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit against the U.S. Department of Defense for records regarding a reported 2021 meeting between then-Attorney General Merrick Garland and then-Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Mark Milley during which they discussed President Trump. ---|--- | | | | | “It should go without saying that Evanston’s reparations program is clearly discriminatory and unconstitutional. Judicial Watch’s class action lawsuit should proceed,” said Judicial Watch President Tom Fitton. ---|--- | | | | | Judicial Watch announced it received 14 pages of records from U.S. Customs and Border Protection in a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request that show border agents seized drones transporting methamphetamine, heroin, and fentanyl across the U.S. southern and northern borders. ---|--- | | | | | While liberals and their mainstream media allies express outrage that the federal agency that funds most of the nation’s scientific research is cancelling grants focused on diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI), it is worth taking a look at a recent U.S. Senate report illustrating how the Biden-Harris administration diverted billions of dollars from scientific research to DEI activities. The extensive document, published by the Senate Commerce Committee, uses a multitude of examples to show that, under Biden, the National Science Foundation (NSF) increasingly politicized the use of taxpayer dollars meant for scientific research. ---|--- | | | The Washington Post The U.S. Justice Department has reached an agreement to settle the wrongful death case brought by the family of Ashli Babbitt, who was fatally shot by police in the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, lawyers for both sides told a federal judge Friday. “We have reached a settlement in principle,” Justice Department civil attorney Joseph Gonzalez told U.S. District Judge Ana C. Reyes of Washington, D.C., who is hearing the family’s $30 million lawsuit against the government. No final deal has been signed and terms have not been disclosed, said Robert Sticht, a lawyer for the conservative group Judicial Watch, which is representing Babbitt’s family. The Hungarian Conservative Former Polish Deputy Justice Minister Marcin Romanowski has been appointed head of the newly established Hungarian–Polish Institute of Freedom. The announcement was made at a joint press conference in Budapest, featuring Romanowski; Miklós Szánthó, Director General of the Budapest-based conservative think tank, the Center for Fundamental Rights, which founded the new institute; and Chris Farrell, Director of Investigations and Research at the American conservative rights advocacy organization Judicial Watch. Washington Examiner Judicial Watch President Tom Fitton, nominated by Trump in his first term to sit on the seven-member Commission on Judicial Disabilities and Tenure, wrote the lone dissent and voted Edelman “unqualified” to resume his work as a judge. “The public expects judges to exercise discretion with utmost caution, particularly in cases involving obviously dangerous defendants with criminal convictions or arrests,” Fitton told the Washington Examiner. | CLIPS | | | | | | Keep our investigators on the job uncovering the truth: | | | | | ---|---|--- Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800, Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 (C)2017-2025, All Rights Reserved |

name: Chairwoman Keesling
subject: 4 BIG wins for Hoosiers!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
We have so much to be grateful for. These are just four of the reasons I’m excited about what’s to come. | | | [](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/Xr0-GNNfznYs0ipGfJSl-nYKzlOEWj_EW557AGX2R1hSg7r5eUfjHkEXU0xf2iK8gUDfDv3FCCWFmIfR9vO5cEHp50Q8ZP0a879i50OhxUUeQCInkNQr_V77JRg1tDUeG_CZ25XBXeyUJ2qgHBjUZ6ME4gCiyU9wxX0gCNpLvqknM88sSDApB11nw2V8nQlB5707m9a1GE19egB_JuCElRjyjUhfeT09-Dfj3tT2PymKhbDM7WlJex2SHgk15iH7_TOv3hH_3FFC4Y-E2flWTmXucTz5H71o9DAA1gG3PSpO1DTi_uYO4A4zDW7Biea69uqPWjZHXVXKkElQ8o921-55NlXHrYlvbFyACuiqWvivOBLllvf-8C3nc77tVXOZ/cLFC33kkRj2wu1Yy4OulYJrodH_v3Bsa/8)
---
Fellow Republican, The last couple of months have been a whirlwind of big ideas, tough debates, and hard-won victories. Thanks to conservative leaders in the General Assembly, we saw real results on the issues that matter most to everyday Hoosiers.

**Our conservative leaders:**
_ ✅ Delivered $1.5 billion in property tax relief and referendum accountability_
_ ✅ Expanded school choice and career opportunities for students_
_ ✅ Ended DEI mandates and protected women’s sports_

## Mexican president fires warning shot to Donald Trump – ‘stay in your territory!’ | World | News
Donald Trump has blasted Mexico’s president, saying she was “afraid of the cartels” in her country. The US president said he thought his Mexican counterpart Claudia Sheinbaum was a “lovely woman” but that she “can’t even think straight” because of her fears of organised gangs in the country. Speaking on Sunday evening aboard Air Force [...] [Read more](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/yXVjFrNxZITPT4ELBmFekTaHfqG9qxZsIXnDU9V7hduoKvv8gs8Jo8CGttpg02jNCbpgeCbuYvlTTZnyeIcLZwLCtnkqPL-AFjaiYJ3BGujC-jkHDjKLSALM-6qI0Jj2PPz8gwyLguzEgJry9z-2_xKZsmQdxO19tE8QMsEvUB6zMjRU9MgEoMBliggy2sl5ihufXkHF4cY16bL3EBT07UGbD2a_ZMLNX-yVo7SljUJGJrvuS8LJRjMV6rW3tnKmUWPag3DlyVmwWd8ciPfXL6wb_ZnN9H1CPJ_0FVYm1svIpWfptaS373mGoVkIikxxLrui-A6Dh9hiYxxKQEdbWB4HOMatHjECZzc7zN1S24xqaLDS0bQnWmnZisyP0m7thA8/hAu-dj_dLPFxXLuPrtwXHI2-wjEOfffx/8)

In 2008 National Review ran Mark Steyn's story on Michelle Obama with a classic cover and the headline "Mrs. Grievance." In 2020 NR's Kyle Smith followed up in "What Does Michelle Obama Have to Complain About?" In 2025 Mrs. Obama is still providing raw material: How do you raise kids in the White House? Have you ever thought about what you'd do if you won a Powerball jackpot? I suspect many if not most of us have, at one time or another, although I don't play lotteries, so my chances of winning are infinitely slim. Anyway, I'm sure you've also seen the stories [...] [Read more](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/kYHNtwz-8HSjNX1NP0A8BoBLwuVOrqVbQj8XSQUdJTPcczJQ3Q9pkEicwh32f7VisTiBxLwB2vURLg7Etxcg01uQFSsq69hyrfaYmVkgb05Ud125XwoedswN9S5Bj0GvD3iMIjzbln6QXiCEr37PhT7prIl5Sr-UTHSIwt6zMHnsZ4fxCNITXtCOjFsuEjWVFOyJ7bDB4JovXHiHLkmc5tF9iCSRNxE59ga9oEUf471epkzLYg-1HRsF7tg5nhvg_w6RvoBojj0wXeVqmaU2HWm9pQPhCsM8z4YC-J5anICxBiaeCTHDMwGnlNHs-XNb_VCYwDjLTj8e0WrPvZc9vXyjBrkUhN8v-AG_HY0dKRkAbGAywbHLjLitVG0/fjoYFgpoDO0AozOUjN98o9ZLFb1tg4wS/8)

Hamas has “a window of opportunity” to agree to a hostage deal with Israel before the end of President Donald Trump’s visit to the Middle East next week, according to a top Israeli defense official. If no deal is reached, Israel will launch an aggressive campaign inside Gaza aimed at the elimination of Hamas, according [...] [Read more](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/Rwa7B8NfyWcU8N61UKe_H_j78ynFFI05LCo8ZWtVt_uIpQr140NmmRbh9-FDUpMJCva_UpkHi6bOQBkVYb0DhZy3iA1JPncYvYlvyvK01w19FmUSAGycyf2t1tSPRZVPo0QDN4oL6P9V2gFauXP74IUFFdHbNysoADsoljgXJ941P8YTFXTurKKAJTLVXDmcUsypaMSRc4gZK4kizssH4vEH0Z64cFj6tHxRJSuTJWsjPcQoofb0MoInq8v-h0hwXgaxPfI2zYxIQmGZVsSLzj1NoCNjfqeMOuNjj1AksKkYluLM7qpZl5BJtYvif6cCUJGGgZIDtAUElEYzEE9MJg/ixBrZ4mWO2IT0O_EHHeVmhmIpvHoP0Yy/8)
