## EU blocks British access to migrant data in devastating blow to Keir Starmer in Brexit ‘reset’: ‘They’re not budging!’
The European Union has denied Britain access to crucial migrant databases in the latest humiliating blow to Sir Keir Starmer’s so-called Brexit “reset”. The bloc has informed British diplomats that Brussels cannot disclose critical data with British law and immigration bodies. Previously, Starmer insisted that “the more we can share, the better” and had outlined [...] [Read more](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/XR-SlHj0cL7VnJbZXUK9ABN75leWsu8nbDAKYWurkg7Shpa3BKZN-nvdLPIwe95ogyNJ8DENWIT16mlZi2o-GjBEUW5OOAxDDAjLBR5TkhjQbnr4IcGxe042RNyoCFvL-FsF5mbpeCDFOP-vlvxjeMK21ANBHV5DofJZ1wvoZ6YgNVIGgTO-KwdZbsd6_yMbbObENuoZ5DC27dS24ItaeFCtv6h1m3rwzL5Mc-ZBJaOWTqp5Tqt50QtMkiTLVTVImLfkr23U-7zdWineYE0KzZReK5DVpcP1VatbW-tt6D9IemIIY9lfLeXdgNZFChbVuM9yulFKvT6ajsZOQlU0T6CxHskEjr7F5X4jBIHTL7Kf5ovNwLgs4If5IaaHPX8_1AJFMLtKjKZ-YEkT_CJ7I6wyTsFmy00YFivc/5W-4_s_z2bDT7GUpIeBtMZFErR6PtQSv/8)

name: New PA Project Education Fund
subject: You're Registered to Vote, Now What?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
In our previous edition we warned about the slippery slope we were in when it came to deportations.
Since, the Trump regime has wrongly detained dozens of U.S. citizens — including a ten-year-old Texas girl [https://www.nbcnews.com/news/latino/us-citizen-child-recovering-brain-cancer-deported-mexico-undocumented-rcna196049] recovering from brain cancer, a 19-year-old U.S. citizen with learning disabilities who was [https://www.motherjones.com/politics/2025/04/us-citizen-jose-hermosillo-wrongly-detained-ice-patrol-governments-account-false/?fbclid=IwY2xjawJ2Qi9leHRuA2FlbQIxMQBicmlkETFPbU5wOTFVREhWYktoNnc5AR7Fe9oVKjamHuG3WmhfyvfnCRNedF3MORvKe81-https://www.motherjones.com/politics/2025/04/us-citizen-jose-hermosillo-wrongly-detained-ice-patrol-governments-account-false/] wrongfully incarcerated [https://popular.info/p/federal-immigration-authorities-wrongfully] for 10 days in a privately run immigration detention facility in Arizona or a 20-year-old born in Georgia [https://www.nbcnews.com/news/latino/us-born-citizen-detained-ice-immigration-florida-rcna201800] , and arrested in Florida.
All mistakes, that cause fear in the American population. The deportation of Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, who, due to an “administrative error,” was sent to a dangerous and violent mega-prison in El Salvador, does not help ease the fear.
The Fifth and Fourteenth Amendments to the Constitution [https://www.law.cornell.edu/wex/due_process] establish the right to due process — the Fifth Amendment says that “[n]o person shall ... be deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law.” The Fourteenth Amendment extends this obligation to the states.
No PERSON. This is true even for the undocumented, even if someone is here without authorization.
Due process for Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, should have included notice of deportation and the opportunity to contest it before it happened. Now even as the Supreme Court has ruled for his return, he has yet to be returned home
We remember Fannie Lou Hamer's statement: "No one is free until everyone is free," which is more relevant today than ever.
REGISTER TO VOTE [https://vote.pa/check/?org=nppef]
Social Media of the Week
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Screenshot%202025-04-24%20110558.png] "I'll tell you what freedom is to me: no fear." – Nina Simone
Nina Simone was a classically trained pianist (not common for a young Black woman in the 1950s) who fused her foundation with jazz, gospel, blues, and folk to create a sound entirely her own, defying all genres.
She didn’t fit into a box or label, nor did she try to.
Simone was denied entry to a prestigious music school, the Curtis Institute of Music in Philadelphia, because of her race. This moment fueled her lifelong work in civil rights.
Her songs like “Mississippi Goddam” became an anthem for civil rights and Black empowerment nationally.
"An artist's duty is to reflect the times." – Nina Simone
#NinaSimone [https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/ninasimone?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZViLdRd4IdJCtrYBGLEVTsPypsKpd6j0yaJmAcVXReBXdJJtwHk4ez66hT4UrL2bj7DBCdw6k-jLVGQf2hZ2Rvg2Ic85g5mN6f5_osQMI3oU5fwxrWsV4zVuFTAzsS68tQ-im0MyR10YVg0qkqSDvfYOhYedO-ZcnQeMoFAbdzmLA&__tn__=*NK-R] #BlackHistory [https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/blackhistory?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZViLdRd4IdJCtrYBGLEVTsPypsKpd6j0yaJmAcVXReBXdJJtwHk4ez66hT4UrL2bj7DBCdw6k-jLVGQf2hZ2Rvg2Ic85g5mN6f5_osQMI3oU5fwxrWsV4zVuFTAzsS68tQ-im0MyR10YVg0qkqSDvfYOhYedO-ZcnQeMoFAbdzmLA&__tn__=*NK-R] #CivilRights [https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/civilrights?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZViLdRd4IdJCtrYBGLEVTsPypsKpd6j0yaJmAcVXReBXdJJtwHk4ez66hT4UrL2bj7DBCdw6k-jLVGQf2hZ2Rvg2Ic85g5mN6f5_osQMI3oU5fwxrWsV4zVuFTAzsS68tQ-im0MyR10YVg0qkqSDvfYOhYedO-ZcnQeMoFAbdzmLA&__tn__=*NK-R]
SHARE ON FACEBOOK [https://www.instagram.com/p/DIwHhQRzQNo/]
https://www.instagram.com/p/DIwHhQRzQNo/ [https://www.instagram.com/p/DIwHhQRzQNo/]
Let's save our planet by saving our democracy.

Happy #EarthDay [https://www.instagram.com/explore/tags/earthday/] from NPPEF - remember you have the choice to voice your vote for leaders and legislations that protect our only home, planet Earth.
SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [https://www.instagram.com/p/DIwHhQRzQNo/]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/7237344C447445787878394747342B75584E47375A57552F745265427662334B544A4A36505A58657A5871474B7A5A6E53705571424758325657365969365747/editor_images/WCM%20logo.JPG] #CourtsMatter
The U.S. Justice Department withdrew its lawsuit [https://triblive.com/news/pennsylvania/justice-department-drops-lawsuit-accusing-pennsylvania-city-of-diluting-power-of-hispanic-voters/] that accused the heavily Hispanic city of Hazleton in Pennsylvania of illegally diluting the political power of its growing Hispanic population. The department’s initial lawsuit, filed in January, said the “at-large” system of electing city council members, as opposed to electing them by district, was unfair to Hispanic voters and prevented them from getting elected to city council. Hazleton’s 30,000 residents are about two-thirds Hispanic, according to U.S. Census figures. One-third is non-Hispanic white.
Closer to Philadelphia, the largest health system in Delaware County began closing two hospitals [https://6abc.com/post/crozer-health-hospital-emergency-rooms-stop-accepting-patients/16228691/] Wednesday after a federal judge in Texas approved their bankruptcy proceedings. The emergency rooms of Taylor Hospital in Ridley Park and Crozer-Chester Medical Center in Upland are expected to close today, Friday, April 25th. Emergency medical services will end next Friday, on May 2. Crozer's ambulatory centers at Brinton Lakes, Broomall, Haverford, and Media are expected to remain open.
This November, voters will have the power to retain or vacate three seats of the Pennsylvania Supreme Court.
The votes cast by powerful Pennsylvania voters in November will impact our lives for years to come, as Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court will make decisions about our election administration, immigrant rights. access to abortion, the safety of our workplaces, how bankruptcies are handled, and the right to unionize. Make sure you can voice your vote!
CHECK YOUR REGISTRATION [https://vote.pa/check/?org=nppef]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/DelCo_2.png] We are hiring!
Let's provide civic education together!
We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Erie, Lawrence, Lehigh Valley, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania.
Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote!
Positions start at $20 an hour.
Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org
EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org]
News You Can Use
State Rep. David Rowe (R-Union) wants to introduce a bill to make it easier for hunters and fishermen to get registered to vote [https://www.palegis.us/house/co-sponsorship/memo?memoID=46210] . He represents a lot of sportsmen in the rural 85th state House District, which includes parts of Union, Juniata, and Mifflin counties and all of Snyder County.
“The bill will not complicate the process of getting a license. The bill will not add another step to the hunting license process,” Rowe told Capital-Star [https://penncapital-star.com/government-politics/general-assembly/state-rep-david-rowe-wants-to-make-it-easier-for-sportsmen-to-register-to-vote/] . “Rather, it will simply make it more accessible. So, for sportsmen that are going to a state office, there will be paper registration forms available for them to simply grab.”
As a voting rights organization, we applaud all legislation that would make it easier for people to register to vote, and make our voices to be heard at the ballot box.
Meanwhile, Pennsylvania’s first two openly autistic legislators, State Reps. Jessica Behnam (D-Allegheny) and Abigail Sailsbury (D-Allegheny), condemned the comments made by the Department of Health Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., attacking autistic people [https://www.witf.org/2025/04/23/pa-lawmakers-some-on-the-autism-spectrum-denounce-rfk-jr-comments/] . They are also opposed to his plan to collect private medical records from US citizens to research autism. Remember that slippery slope regarding immigrant deportations?
And if you are waiting for a tax return but have defaulted student loans, there is a chance the government will garnish your return and even your wages. The Department of Education announced Monday it will restart collections on defaulted federal student loans on May 5 [https://thehill.com/homenews/education/5259465-education-department-defaulted-federal-student-loans-debt-collection-trump-mcmahon] , including potentially garnishing wages for millions of workers. The Treasury Offset Program allows the government to withhold federal payments such as tax returns for individuals in default. After a 30-day notice, the federal government could also begin garnishing a borrower’s wages. So make sure to check your statement.
NPPEF on the Ground
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/IMG_2941.jpg] Engaging the young
Regional Organizing Director, Daniel Levin providing civil education to students. Did you know that as long as a student is 18 years of age on or before the day of the next election - they can register to vote?
Many 17 year old students don't know this and might pass up the opportunity to register to vote, thinking they have to be 18 at time of registering.
It's our passion to bridge the gap here on civic education.

clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! In our previous edition we warned about the slippery slope we were in when it came to deportations. Since, the Trump regime has wrongly detained dozens of U.S. citizens — including a ten-year-old Texas girl [] recovering from brain cancer, a 19-year-old U.S. citizen with learning disabilities who was [] wrongfully incarcerated [] for 10 days in a privately run immigration detention facility in Arizona or a 20-year-old born in Georgia [] , and arrested in Florida. All mistakes, that cause fear in the American population. The deportation of Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, who, due to an “administrative error,” was sent to a dangerous and violent mega-prison in El Salvador, does not help ease the fear. The Fifth and Fourteenth Amendments to the Constitution [] establish the right to due process — the Fifth Amendment says that “[n]o person shall ... be deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law.” The Fourteenth Amendment extends this obligation to the states. No PERSON. This is true even for the undocumented, even if someone is here without authorization. Due process for Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, should have included notice of deportation and the opportunity to contest it before it happened. Now even as the Supreme Court has ruled for his return, he has yet to be returned home We remember Fannie Lou Hamer's statement: "No one is free until everyone is free," which is more relevant today than ever. REGISTER TO VOTE [] Social Media of the Week [] "I'll tell you what freedom is to me: no fear." – Nina Simone Nina Simone was a classically trained pianist (not common for a young Black woman in the 1950s) who fused her foundation with jazz, gospel, blues, and folk to create a sound entirely her own, defying all genres. She didn’t fit into a box or label, nor did she try to. Simone was denied entry to a prestigious music school, the Curtis Institute of Music in Philadelphia, because of her race. This moment fueled her lifelong work in civil rights. Her songs like “Mississippi Goddam” became an anthem for civil rights and Black empowerment nationally. "An artist's duty is to reflect the times." – Nina Simone #NinaSimone [] #BlackHistory [] #CivilRights [] SHARE ON FACEBOOK [] [] Let's save our planet by saving our democracy. Happy #EarthDay [] from NPPEF - remember you have the choice to voice your vote for leaders and legislations that protect our only home, planet Earth. SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [] [] #CourtsMatter The U.S. Justice Department withdrew its lawsuit [] that accused the heavily Hispanic city of Hazleton in Pennsylvania of illegally diluting the political power of its growing Hispanic population. The department’s initial lawsuit, filed in January, said the “at-large” system of electing city council members, as opposed to electing them by district, was unfair to Hispanic voters and prevented them from getting elected to city council. Hazleton’s 30,000 residents are about two-thirds Hispanic, according to U.S. Census figures. One-third is non-Hispanic white. Closer to Philadelphia, the largest health system in Delaware County began closing two hospitals [] Wednesday after a federal judge in Texas approved their bankruptcy proceedings. The emergency rooms of Taylor Hospital in Ridley Park and Crozer-Chester Medical Center in Upland are expected to close today, Friday, April 25th. Emergency medical services will end next Friday, on May 2. Crozer's ambulatory centers at Brinton Lakes, Broomall, Haverford, and Media are expected to remain open. This November, voters will have the power to retain or vacate three seats of the Pennsylvania Supreme Court. The votes cast by powerful Pennsylvania voters in November will impact our lives for years to come, as Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court will make decisions about our election administration, immigrant rights. access to abortion, the safety of our workplaces, how bankruptcies are handled, and the right to unionize. Make sure you can voice your vote! CHECK YOUR REGISTRATION [] [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Erie, Lawrence, Lehigh Valley, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use State Rep. David Rowe (R-Union) wants to introduce a bill to make it easier for hunters and fishermen to get registered to vote [] . He represents a lot of sportsmen in the rural 85th state House District, which includes parts of Union, Juniata, and Mifflin counties and all of Snyder County. “The bill will not complicate the process of getting a license. The bill will not add another step to the hunting license process,” Rowe told Capital-Star [] . “Rather, it will simply make it more accessible. So, for sportsmen that are going to a state office, there will be paper registration forms available for them to simply grab.” As a voting rights organization, we applaud all legislation that would make it easier for people to register to vote, and make our voices to be heard at the ballot box. Meanwhile, Pennsylvania’s first two openly autistic legislators, State Reps. Jessica Behnam (D-Allegheny) and Abigail Sailsbury (D-Allegheny), condemned the comments made by the Department of Health Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., attacking autistic people [] . They are also opposed to his plan to collect private medical records from US citizens to research autism. Remember that slippery slope regarding immigrant deportations? And if you are waiting for a tax return but have defaulted student loans, there is a chance the government will garnish your return and even your wages. The Department of Education announced Monday it will restart collections on defaulted federal student loans on May 5 [] , including potentially garnishing wages for millions of workers. The Treasury Offset Program allows the government to withhold federal payments such as tax returns for individuals in default. After a 30-day notice, the federal government could also begin garnishing a borrower’s wages. So make sure to check your statement. Many 17 year old students don't know this and might pass up the opportunity to register to vote, thinking they have to be 18 at time of registering. clean_body: 43 Years is long enough Marcy Kaptur is out of touch. | | | | | | | | | | Hi, After nearly 43 years in Washington, Democrat career politician Marcy Kaptur has lost touch. Not only is she the longest-serving woman in the U.S. House of Representatives, but she came within just five thousan d votes of defeat last election! There’s never been a better time to, and Alea Nadeem is an Air Force Veteran and patriot who will get the job done. | | Washington is broken because too many politicians have made a career out of power, rather than a mission to serve. While Marcy Kaptur has been busy protecting her power, prices have skyrocketed, our borders have been left wide open flooding America with drugs, and our enemies abroad have grown bolder. Simply put: Career politicians like Marcy Kaptur have failed the good and hardworking people of Ohio. America needs fighters, not politicians. | | Forty three years is long enough. It’s time for NEW leadership. It’s time for REAL service. It’s time to FIGHT and WIN! Let’s change Washington together. Sincerely,Team Alea for Congress | | | | | | Paid for by Alea for Congress | **The appearance of Alea Nadeem in military uniform does not imply or constitute endorsement by the U.S. Air Force or U.S. Department of Defense. | | | | | | | | Executive Pkwy #7107, Toledo, OH 43606 |

**Peter: These days, every headline feels like one more reminder that MAGA extremists will stop at nothing to undermine our democracy:**
“‘An existential threat to democracy’: the US judge facing a challenge to her election victory” — _The Guardian_
“How Trump’s tariffs fit the autocrat’s playbook” — _New Yorker_
“Hundreds of scholars say U.S. is swiftly heading toward authoritarianism” — _NPR_
We aren’t just grappling with bad policies — we’re in a battle over whether democracy itself will endure. The implications are massive. We must heed President Obama’s words and put our values into action.
[**Change the headlines, Peter. Fight for a stronger, more representative democracy. Thanks to supporters like you, the NDRC achieves real victories to make our democracy fairer and more representative:
* We work to elect leaders who will defend democracy.
* We fight for fair maps and push back against extreme partisan gerrymandering and other anti-democracy efforts that dilute voters’ power.
* And we mobilize voters leading up to important elections — including races this year in Virginia, Pennsylvania, and Michigan, where the outcomes could affect our work for years to come.

clean_body: Your gift is a commitment to sustaining our democracy-saving work. | | = Peter: These days, every headline feels like one more reminder that MAGA extremists will stop at nothing to undermine our democracy: “‘An existential threat to democracy’: the US judge facing a challenge to her election victory” — The Guardian “How Trump’s tariffs fit the autocrat’s playbook” — New Yorker “Hundreds of scholars say U.S. is swiftly heading toward authoritarianism” — NPR We aren’t just grappling with bad policies — we’re in a battle over whether democracy itself will endure. The implications are massive. We must heed President Obama’s words and put our values into action. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thanks to supporters like you, the NDRC achieves real victories to make our democracy fairer and more representative: We work to elect leaders who will defend democracy. We fight for fair maps and push back against extreme partisan gerrymandering and other anti-democracy efforts that dilute voters’ power. And we mobilize voters leading up to important elections — including races this year in Virginia, Pennsylvania, and Michigan, where the outcomes could affect our work for years to come. = — Team NDRC = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. name: Deb Haaland
subject: re: Medicare
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Every person in this country should be able to afford the medicine they need.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=1760%2E400544%2Eo3VUWS)
**I just want to say it like it is.**
Every person in this country should be able to afford the medicine they need, without skipping doses, going into debt, or choosing between prescriptions and groceries.
But that’s exactly what too many New Mexicans are forced to do, and Donald Trump just made it worse.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250505_1_pharma?amount=5&refcode=em20250505-1760&refcode2=1760_400544_o3VUWS&akid=1760%2E400544%2Eo3VUWS)
Recently, Donald Trump issued an executive order aimed at gutting Medicare’s ability to negotiate lower drug prices – a hard-won protection in the Inflation Reduction Act.
**Let’s be clear:** this will raise drug prices for seniors, waste taxpayer dollars, and put even more power in the hands of billion-dollar drug companies.
I know what it’s like to struggle. To count every dollar at the pharmacy and pray it’ll stretch far enough.
**That’s why I’ll always stand up to keep money in our pockets – not hand it over to Big Pharma. But I can’t do this alone.**
[**Will you chip in $5 right now to help us fight back against Trump’s billionaire agenda and elect a governor who puts people first?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250505_1_pharma?amount=5&refcode=em20250505-1760&refcode2=1760_400544_o3VUWS&akid=1760%2E400544%2Eo3VUWS)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250505_1_pharma?amount=5&refcode=em20250505-1760&refcode2=1760_400544_o3VUWS&akid=1760%2E400544%2Eo3VUWS)**
Trump is trying to rewrite the rules in Big Pharma’s favor. I’m running to protect working families and ensure no one is priced out of the care they need.
Let’s do this together.
Be fierce,
Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250505-1&t=2&refcode2=1760_400544_o3VUWS&akid=1760%2E400544%2Eo3VUWS)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=1760%2E400544%2Eo3VUWS)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=1760%2E400544%2Eo3VUWS)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)
PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO

name: Jane Fonda
subject: I refuse to stay quiet about this.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
Trump just dismissed hundreds of scientists working to protect our environment.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107143403/543731616/1328794144?refcode=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ms=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNiNDIzNTY4LWU4MjktZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6SOmjnpYdzI-tQSQgWhUkLxfymwwPzMBwOM0RqrGL6Y=&emci=4dba02f6-c129-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=4823105)
---
If the facts don’t fit the fossil fuel agenda, Trump’s solution is to erase the facts. Last week, Trump dismissed hundreds of scientists and experts working on the federal government’s flagship climate report. For over 20 years, the **National Climate Assessment** has been the nation’s most comprehensive and trusted source on how rising temperatures impact our lives. It gives us the information we need to protect our communities, our homes, and our futures from the worst effects of the climate crisis. **All of that is now gutted and buried because the truth threatens the fossil fuel industry. The facts are too inconvenient for billionaires who want to keep destroying our planet.** **[So it’s time to wake up and rise up. JanePAC is doing the critical work of building a political firewall to block Trump’s fossil fuel agenda. But we don’t have time to waste — our planet is on the line, our lives are on the line, and right now we need to elect the next generation of climate leaders. Can we count on your donation today?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107143405/543731618/2105508926?refcode=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ms=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNiNDIzNTY4LWU4MjktZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6SOmjnpYdzI-tQSQgWhUkLxfymwwPzMBwOM0RqrGL6Y=)** Let me say this plainly: Donald Trump doesn’t care how history will judge him, and that’s so deeply sad. It’s not just that he’s making everyday life so painfully difficult for the very people who voted for him, he is trying to destroy the possibility of us having a future that supports human life. This isn’t some left-wing rhetoric, just ask the climate scientists. That’s why they’re being fired. Along with decades of fossil fuel CEOs, Trump will have many millions of lives on his hands by not only denying the climate crisis but also trying to destroy humanity’s chances of doing something about it. **Does he realize what his pro-fossil-fuel policies are doing to us, and he just doesn’t care? Or does he really believe it’s a hoax? I don’t think it’s the latter.** So today, I am personally calling on you to join me. **[We must not take this sitting down. I’m in this fight because I refuse to give up on what’s worth saving. If you feel that too — if this moment has you angry and ready to do something — then join me. Chip in what you can, and let’s fight for the future that Trump and his fossil fuel pals are trying to steal.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107143407/543731620/2105508926?refcode=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ms=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNiNDIzNTY4LWU4MjktZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6SOmjnpYdzI-tQSQgWhUkLxfymwwPzMBwOM0RqrGL6Y=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $5**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107143409/543731622/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ms=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNiNDIzNTY4LWU4MjktZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6SOmjnpYdzI-tQSQgWhUkLxfymwwPzMBwOM0RqrGL6Y=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107143410/543731623/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ms=ea.cb423568-e829-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNiNDIzNTY4LWU4MjktZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6SOmjnpYdzI-tQSQgWhUkLxfymwwPzMBwOM0RqrGL6Y=)

clean_body: Trump just dismissed hundreds of scientists working to protect our environment. | If the facts don’t fit the fossil fuel agenda, Trump’s solution is to erase the facts. Last week, Trump dismissed hundreds of scientists and experts working on the federal government’s flagship climate report. For over 20 years, the National Climate Assessment has been the nation’s most comprehensive and trusted source on how rising temperatures impact our lives. It gives us the information we need to protect our communities, our homes, and our futures from the worst effects of the climate crisis. All of that is now gutted and buried because the truth threatens the fossil fuel industry. The facts are too inconvenient for billionaires who want to keep destroying our planet. = Let me say this plainly: Donald Trump doesn’t care how history will judge him, and that’s so deeply sad. It’s not just that he’s making everyday life so painfully difficult for the very people who voted for him, he is trying to destroy the possibility of us having a future that supports human life. This isn’t some left-wing rhetoric, just ask the climate scientists. That’s why they’re being fired. Along with decades of fossil fuel CEOs, Trump will have many millions of lives on his hands by not only denying the climate crisis but also trying to destroy humanity’s chances of doing something about it. Does he realize what his pro-fossil-fuel policies are doing to us, and he just doesn’t care? Or does he really believe it’s a hoax? I don’t think it’s the latter. So today, I am personally calling on you to join me. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Onward, Jane Fonda = | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. To make **_[_California Golden Again_](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c5f82fe1e2674ccb9ee89ace44164652&_e=6zSL5W06ajYngC7Pb4U4oRw2ATX_wgnJR-ak8DvRbTI3DUumAgDprGWhDz6N7mDpIlrkOMth_d5YOJm3ZLnbAoylPW9cpj9OJv5sbcsZfHMqpg7M9XzyuOnP9N3nkDAUJTts59JdBKWEK7HIhgdYLVcabX50lFTK3PXFQ3nkfKyxVm5wJoncuniZhjPABH_Z8ubAEgEIehnMWe_pJ5xIUnzbqV04EzNpP-mRmsb6UHdKxeu9odKwoUStsAl-u_D5pPkC4FtAtzVEunFRKc-NL9q--5LyV-wA565rxIZo2DV1awkxbJJrXnS7CpAfgaQiMpLK6WegizPlpqvZwATDivgdlknPAqrU7mS_Rz36gppHkSV3gTaEmn3rbqHgtCxpk7mfNsNvj5dQnOYvbn5U-OZfmuM3FQYDUerBlPVCp-KMj4psQ7SqmC7-kj3hJhOP)_.** Our grassroots movement is already gaining steam, and some of the strongest leaders in the conservative movement have already endorsed my campaign.

[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c5f82fe1e2674ccb9ee89ace44164652&_e=6zSL5W06ajYngC7Pb4U4oRw2ATX_wgnJR-ak8DvRbTI3DUumAgDprGWhDz6N7mDpIlrkOMth_d5YOJm3ZLnbAoylPW9cpj9OJv5sbcsZfHMqpg7M9XzyuOnP9N3nkDAUJTts59JdBKWEK7HIhgdYLVcabX50lFTK3PXFQ3nkfKyxVm5wJoncuniZhjPABH_Z8ubAEgEIehnMWe_pJ5xIUnzbqV04EzNpP-mRmsb6UHdKxeu9odKwoUStsAl-u_D5pPkC4FtAtzVEunFRKc-NLyypentS8qvBvwJT5U0f6rbCYyR5kwwQQhhvKh_9VugOY04OVrkKLUnBuG_cp_cituZqk9_9XNDD8G2Fe47WWIpRV6qjtF1955mjYOGmk9o9_tPRBD3HGVnIA8cKBNlQpZq0ZRlEEVdz2DMj8DWOwLgqO_K6I_4QVRaXIcUx2AuC)

There’s nowhere better than California. But everyone can see how badly things have gone off track. We were once the land of opportunity but now we have America’s highest prices for gas, power outages are a regular occurrence, and almost a million Californians don’t have access to clean drinking water. Today we’re sadly famous for dystopian scenes of crime, chaos, and squalor. Let’s make California the land of opportunity again with:
---
# GREAT JOBS
---
# **GREAT HOMES**
---
# **GREAT KIDS**
---
We need to make California an inspiration again, not a warning of what not to do. There’s only one way to do it. We need to end the Democrat one-party rule that got us into this mess. California is ready for a change. In 2024 almost every county shifted away from the Democrats. Since Gavin Newsom took office, **one million new Republican voters** have registered. Earlier this year a poll found that **48%** of Californians would consider voting for a Republican governor. I’m honored to have strong conservative leaders like Vivek Ramaswamy, Charlie Kirk, and Kirk Cameron endorsing me, but your personal endorsement matters more to me.
---

---
# Meet Steve Hilton
---
I love California so much. I moved to the Bay Area over a decade ago. I’ve raised my family here, taught at Stanford, started a business, written books, hosted a TV show ... lived the California dream. It’s my home, in the deepest sense. I’m a proud American, and an incredibly proud Californian too. But I’ve never forgotten where I came from. My parents were immigrants – they fled communism in Hungary to start a new life in England. In a free country. I appreciate exactly what that means.We didn’t have much money. My mother worked in a shoe store, my stepfather worked construction. My dad, who died when I was a kid, was the goalie for the Hungarian national ice hockey team. In 2012 we moved to California. I hosted a TV show — The Next Revolution with Steve Hilton — and podcasts: California Rebel Base, The Steve Hilton Show, Big Ideas Book Club. Through my policy organization, Golden Together, I’ve set out the changes we need to make to get this state back on track. Over the last few years, I’ve traveled the state, meeting people from all walks of life and backgrounds, in every part of California. I’ve spent time with business owners and workers in every industry, struggling with the absolute nightmare it’s become to start and grow a company here. I’ve seen the struggles so many Californians face raising a family given the unbelievable costs of living here. To make real change happen in government, you need the ability to think differently, the courage to challenge establishment groupthink and the conviction to follow through. That’s what I will bring to this job – to the urgent necessity of restoring the California Dream for everyone, and making this beautiful state, that we love so much, truly Golden Again.

clean_body: Let’s Make California Golden Again!| | | - | | Dear Fellow Conservative, I’m Steve Hilton, and I’m running to be the next Governor of California to bring back freedom, opportunity, and common sense. To make . Our grassroots movement is already gaining steam, and some of the strongest leaders in the conservative movement have already endorsed my campaign. There’s nowhere better than California. But everyone can see how badly things have gone off track. We were once the land of opportunity but now we have America’s highest prices for gas, power outages are a regular occurrence, and almost a million Californians don’t have access to clean drinking water. Today we’re sadly famous for dystopian scenes of crime, chaos, and squalor. Let’s make California the land of opportunity again with: GREAT JOBS GREAT HOMES GREAT KIDS We need to make California an inspiration again, not a warning of what not to do. There’s only one way to do it. We need to end the Democrat one-party rule that got us into this mess. California is ready for a change. In 2024 almost every county shifted away from the Democrats. Since Gavin Newsom took office, one million new Republican voters have registered. Earlier this year a poll found that 48% of Californians would consider voting for a Republican governor. I’m honored to have strong conservative leaders like Vivek Ramaswamy, Charlie Kirk, and Kirk Cameron endorsing me, but your personal endorsement matters more to me. To win, we’re building a grassroots movement. I know what I believe and what it takes to save California. Your contribution of $25 , $50 , or $100 will get our message to millions of voters. I’m the only candidate that can win and I promise I will never bend the knee to the left. I truly appreciate your help. With your support, we’ll make California Golden Again. Steve HiltonCandidate for Governor of California | | Meet Steve Hilton I love California so much. I moved to the Bay Area over a decade ago. I’ve raised my family here, taught at Stanford, started a business, written books, hosted a TV show ... lived the California dream. It’s my home, in the deepest sense. I’m a proud American, and an incredibly proud Californian too. But I’ve never forgotten where I came from. My parents were immigrants – they fled communism in Hungary to start a new life in England. In a free country. I appreciate exactly what that means.We didn’t have much money. My mother worked in a shoe store, my stepfather worked construction. My dad, who died when I was a kid, was the goalie for the Hungarian national ice hockey team. In 2012 we moved to California. I hosted a TV show — The Next Revolution with Steve Hilton — and podcasts: California Rebel Base, The Steve Hilton Show, Big Ideas Book Club. Through my policy organization, Golden Together, I’ve set out the changes we need to make to get this state back on track. Over the last few years, I’ve traveled the state, meeting people from all walks of life and backgrounds, in every part of California. I’ve spent time with business owners and workers in every industry, struggling with the absolute nightmare it’s become to start and grow a company here. I’ve seen the struggles so many Californians face raising a family given the unbelievable costs of living here. To make real change happen in government, you need the ability to think differently, the courage to challenge establishment groupthink and the conviction to follow through. That’s what I will bring to this job – to the urgent necessity of restoring the California Dream for everyone, and making this beautiful state, that we love so much, truly Golden Again. | | | PAID FOR BY STEVE HILTON FOR GOVERNOR 2026 FPPC ID#1480425 Please add mailto:info@stevehiltonforgovernor.com "info@stevehiltonforgovernor.com" to your email contacts to make sure our updates are making it into your inbox. PO Box 730,HILMAR, CA 95324

body:
| $5 donations help fuel all the work Adam’s doing in the Senate to deliver for California families and defend our democracy.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=8542%2E5257348%2EGO_NYN)
Derek — Happy May 5th!
Today marks the 5th day of the 5th month of the year, so we thought we’d give you 5 reasons to [**donate $5 to support Adam’s work.**](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1230?c0=q22025_abs_em.AkhZ-L&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250505_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=8542%2E5257348%2EGO_NYN)
1. **The Republican Senate majority is extremely slim.** But it’s an arduous path to flip the Senate that requires protecting seats in states like Georgia, New Hampshire, Minnesota, and Michigan. And it would mean picking up four seats from Republicans. We can do this, Derek. As Vice Chair of the Senate Democrats' campaign arm, Adam is working every day to help Democrats flip Senate seats blue during next year’s midterm elections.

2. **The Senate is our best line of defense to put a check on Trump’s agenda.** At every turn, Senate Republicans have refused to stand up to Trump’s extreme and corrupt agenda — which is why we need to elect Democratic Senators who will. A Democratic Senate majority will fight to hold the Trump administration accountable, stop Trump from slashing critical services like Social Security and Medicaid, and stand up to Trump’s lawlessness.

3. **While Trump attacks Adam for holding him accountable, Adam is fighting back.** Trump has attacked Adam more times than we can count, but Adam refuses to be silenced by _anyone_. He’s going to continue defending our democracy and calling out this administration for its attacks against the rule of law.

4. **But it takes a lot to stand up to the MAGA army.** Adam has been the target of GOP anger for years, and they’re showing no signs of slowing down. That’s why Adam counts on folks like you to stand with him as he works to hold this administration accountable.

5. **$5 goes a long way.** Adam refused to accept corporate PAC money while running for Senate because he wanted his work to be powered by everyday folks like you — not the wealthy and powerful he’s working to hold accountable. $5 donations help fuel all the work he’s doing in the Senate to deliver for California families and defend our democracy.

[**So Derek — with those reasons in mind, can you chip in $5 (or more!) today to support Adam’s work in the Senate?**](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1230?c0=q22025_abs_em.AkhZ-L&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250505_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=5&akid=8542%2E5257348%2EGO_NYN)

## DOJ Sues Colorado Over Sanctuary Policies as Public Safety Takes Center Stage
|
---

On Friday May 2, the U.S. Department of Justice filed a lawsuit against the State of Colorado and the City and County of Denver for policies that obstruct federal immigration enforcement. This move reinforces what SAFE Colorado has been saying all along: **sanctuary policies make our communities less safe** by creating barriers between law enforcement and immigration authorities. The DOJ lawsuit targets Colorado’s 2019 law (SB19-054) and Denver’s local ordinances that prevent cooperation with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). These policies prohibit law enforcement officers from sharing critical information with ICE or honoring detainers, even when it involves individuals with serious criminal records. **We believe public safety must come first.** SAFE Colorado is not alone in raising concerns. This issue was featured on Fox News this week, where our Co-Founder and former ICE Field Office Director **John Fabbricatore** appeared to explain how Colorado’s sanctuary policies directly undermine federal law enforcement and public safety.
➡️ [Watch the Fox News segment here](https://rpgdxp.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyOTI0MjMsXCJsXCI6MTUzNTg5NjkyMzI3MDAzNjc4LFwiclwiOjE1MzU4OTcwMzc3MjIxMDU0M30iLCJzIjoiN2ZmNDBmOWFlYTQ1ODVlOCJ9)**
****Here’s what you can do:**
* Read the full story: [DOJ sues Colorado, Denver over sanctuary policies](https://rpgdxp.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyOTI0MjMsXCJsXCI6MTUzNTg5NjkyMzMxMTk3OTgzLFwiclwiOjE1MzU4OTcwMzc3MjIxMDU0M30iLCJzIjoiMzdiNDc4NGJkNjllNWZjYSJ9)
* Contact your state legislators and demand they repeal dangerous sanctuary laws
* Support our mission: SAFE Colorado is working every day to empower law enforcement and restore common-sense public safety policies.

****

name: Wesley Bell
subject: Trump doesn't give a damn about Missouri teachers or schools.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
End of story. Peter,

It’s National Teacher Appreciation Week — **and I want to start by saying thank you to every teacher who shows up for our kids, day in and day out.**

The ones who stay late grading papers, who buy school supplies out of their own pockets, and who help shape the hearts and minds of the next generation. Missouri teachers are some of the most dedicated people I know, _and they deserve so much more than just our gratitude._

But right now, **they’re under attack.**

With Donald Trump back in the White House, his administration has made it clear: Public education is in their crosshairs.

He’s moving to dismantle the Department of Education entirely — and if that happens, Missouri schools would be some of the hardest hit.

Missouri schools would lose millions in federal support for special education, free school lunches, and programs that serve our most vulnerable students. And it doesn’t stop there...they're attacking LGBTQ+ kids, extreme groups are targeting books that challenge traditional thinking, and extreme politicians trying to dictate what can and can’t be taught in our classrooms.

Trump and his MAGA cronies are launching an all-out assault on our public schools and the people working so hard to hold them together. I’m not letting this go unanswered.

[**This National Teacher Appreciation Week, add your name to my petition if you believe our schools — and the people who run them — deserve better than what the Trump administration is trying to do.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107143998/543733746/-16148020?ms=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&refcode=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&source=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=agd3RXglfW11yiYhG4FEr8KsyULBkD5VpCirXMz7MmmbKE1Yp63pmrRv/pogE9dnuMOWY5CZu/ejOG133LfTeM3chO2jBaywp5PTnAIA4TwtJf5rfFlwV8d7xeRhcTiqMm3Tij4Zzf7WpeSKx8adsHQWCZsD8C9uxreOAHG2mio=&refcode2=b4945d43-eb29-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjQ5NDVkNDMtZWIyOS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=007bxRwVhK5o2ckTnFwX2vMQIu6l5JXujIIq2N9BVHI=&emci=a6a7671b-d329-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=b4945d43-eb29-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=1020964) | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107143999/543733747/-16148020?ms=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&refcode=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&source=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=agd3RXglfW11yiYhG4FEr8KsyULBkD5VpCirXMz7MmmbKE1Yp63pmrRv/pogE9dnuMOWY5CZu/ejOG133LfTeM3chO2jBaywp5PTnAIA4TwtJf5rfFlwV8d7xeRhcTiqMm3Tij4Zzf7WpeSKx8adsHQWCZsD8C9uxreOAHG2mio=&refcode2=b4945d43-eb29-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjQ5NDVkNDMtZWIyOS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=007bxRwVhK5o2ckTnFwX2vMQIu6l5JXujIIq2N9BVHI=&emci=a6a7671b-d329-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=b4945d43-eb29-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=1020964)
---
And let’s be real — it’s easy for someone like Donald Trump, who was born into privilege, sent to elite private schools, and never had to rely on the public school system, to treat public education as if it doesn't matter.

But deep down, he knows better. He knows that public schools are where kids — no matter their zip code — get a fair shot to learn, grow, and build a better future.

**_He just doesn’t care._**

[**I’m standing with Missouri’s teachers and kids — and I’m asking you to do the same. Sign on today. >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107144000/543733748/-16148020?ms=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&refcode=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&source=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250506_teacha_fndd_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=agd3RXglfW11yiYhG4FEr8KsyULBkD5VpCirXMz7MmmbKE1Yp63pmrRv/pogE9dnuMOWY5CZu/ejOG133LfTeM3chO2jBaywp5PTnAIA4TwtJf5rfFlwV8d7xeRhcTiqMm3Tij4Zzf7WpeSKx8adsHQWCZsD8C9uxreOAHG2mio=&refcode2=b4945d43-eb29-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjQ5NDVkNDMtZWIyOS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=007bxRwVhK5o2ckTnFwX2vMQIu6l5JXujIIq2N9BVHI=&emci=a6a7671b-d329-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=b4945d43-eb29-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=1020964)

Onward,

Wesley

clean_body: End of story. Peter, It’s National Teacher Appreciation Week — and I want to start by saying thank you to every teacher who shows up for our kids, day in and day out. The ones who stay late grading papers, who buy school supplies out of their own pockets, and who help shape the hearts and minds of the next generation. Missouri teachers are some of the most dedicated people I know, and they deserve so much more than just our gratitude. But right now, they’re under attack. With Donald Trump back in the White House, his administration has made it clear: Public education is in their crosshairs. He’s moving to dismantle the Department of Education entirely — and if that happens, Missouri schools would be some of the hardest hit. Missouri schools would lose millions in federal support for special education, free school lunches, and programs that serve our most vulnerable students. And it doesn’t stop there...they're attacking LGBTQ+ kids, extreme groups are targeting books that challenge traditional thinking, and extreme politicians trying to dictate what can and can’t be taught in our classrooms. Trump and his MAGA cronies are launching an all-out assault on our public schools and the people working so hard to hold them together. I’m not letting this go unanswered. | And let’s be real — it’s easy for someone like Donald Trump, who was born into privilege, sent to elite private schools, and never had to rely on the public school system, to treat public education as if it doesn't matter. But deep down, he knows better. He knows that public schools are where kids — no matter their zip code — get a fair shot to learn, grow, and build a better future. He just doesn’t care. Onward, Wesley = = PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI PO Box 190669, ST LOUIS, MO 63119

name: EMILYs List
subject: U.S. House Republicans are scared
body:
They won't show up for town halls in their own communities, but they're working hard to unseat EMILYs List women. |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107144580/543736155/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE510_20250505_NRCCTargets_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1056044&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkODA3MDU0Ny1lYzI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rXIAKjd_xjiroiKArSUUt6GVahrMoxB8m_E4nyDRfEs=) Hi Peter,

Republicans know that Democrats have a really good shot at taking back the U.S. House in 2026.

So while they won't show up for town halls in their own communities, U.S. House Republicans are working hard to throw everything they can at the Democratic pro-choice women running in tough districts.

[**We're not going to let them hold their slim majority. Peter, will you chip in today to make sure EMILYs List women can build strong campaigns and fight back against anti-choice Republicans?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107144581/543736156/-1082512980?refcode=WUEFE510_20250505_NRCCTargets_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1056044&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkODA3MDU0Ny1lYzI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rXIAKjd_xjiroiKArSUUt6GVahrMoxB8m_E4nyDRfEs=) | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107144582/543736157/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE510_20250505_NRCCTargets_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1056044&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkODA3MDU0Ny1lYzI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rXIAKjd_xjiroiKArSUUt6GVahrMoxB8m_E4nyDRfEs=)
---
The National Republican Congressional Committee (NRCC) released its list of top targets for the 2026 cycle, and unsurprisingly, it's filled with EMILYs List women. These women stood strong and won tough races in the face of extremist attacks last year, and now, the GOP is coming after them with everything they've got — misleading attack ads, dark money campaigns, and an aggressive ground game — all to unseat our women and cling to their razor-thin majority.

Republicans are counting on voters to forget what they've done. They're pushing unpopular legislation, backing the Trump agenda without question, and ignoring the will of the American people over and over again. They know their policies are unpopular — but they're doubling down anyway, hoping that billionaire donors and scorched-earth campaign tactics will push them over the finish line.

That's why we're not waiting until 2026. We're building our movement now — to protect our pro-choice champions in Congress and elect even more women ready to fight for our rights, our families, and our future.

clean_body: They won't show up for town halls in their own communities, but they're working hard to unseat EMILYs List women. | = Hi Peter, Republicans know that Democrats have a really good shot at taking back the U.S. House in 2026. So while they won't show up for town halls in their own communities, U.S. House Republicans are working hard to throw everything they can at the Democratic pro-choice women running in tough districts. = | = The National Republican Congressional Committee (NRCC) released its list of top targets for the 2026 cycle, and unsurprisingly, it's filled with EMILYs List women. These women stood strong and won tough races in the face of extremist attacks last year, and now, the GOP is coming after them with everything they've got — misleading attack ads, dark money campaigns, and an aggressive ground game — all to unseat our women and cling to their razor-thin majority. Republicans are counting on voters to forget what they've done. They're pushing unpopular legislation, backing the Trump agenda without question, and ignoring the will of the American people over and over again. They know their policies are unpopular — but they're doubling down anyway, hoping that billionaire donors and scorched-earth campaign tactics will push them over the finish line. That's why we're not waiting until 2026. We're building our movement now — to protect our pro-choice champions in Congress and elect even more women ready to fight for our rights, our families, and our future. **= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | =** Thank you for standing with us, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Al Franken
subject: The “Powwow” Story
body:
Hi folks,
I want to tell you about someone who’s unbelievably great. Someone who understands what’s at stake in this moment, who listens, who leads, and who embodies the values that make Minnesota, well, Minnesotan . That person is Peggy Flanagan. And she’s running to represent our great state in the U.S. Senate.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-flanagan?refcode=250505em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-flanagan?refcode=250505em]
I’ve known Peggy for a long time. We both grew up in St. Louis Park (not at the same time — she’s young, and I’m old). Raised by a single mom, Peggy knows the impact of federal programs that help working families. They had a home because of a Section 8 housing voucher. Other public programs helped her mom afford health insurance and child care. She gets it because she’s lived it. And that’s exactly the kind of leader we need in Washington. (And frankly, what we have way too little of.)
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-flanagan?refcode=250505em]
Peggy’s got the experience, too. She’s currently serving as Minnesota’s Lieutenant Governor — the highest-ranking Native American woman ever elected to executive office in the U.S. Before that, she was a state legislator, a school board member, an executive director for the Children’s Defense Fund–Minnesota, she even worked for ME!
But most importantly — she worked for my friend and hero, the late Senator Paul Wellstone. She and I both believe in his guiding principle: We all do better when we all do better. (Yes, it’s that simple. No, Republicans still don’t get it.)
While Republicans hide from their constituents, Peggy is doing the opposite. She’s traveling the state hosting ‘Kitchen Table Conversations’—actually listening to real Minnesotans, having real conversations, and working to bring people together. She doesn't just want to fight against bad stuff. She wants to build something, too.
Oh, and in case you’re wondering — yes, Peggy will be the first Native American woman ever elected to the U.S. Senate. It’s about time. She is Ojibwe and a member of White Earth, and her Anishinaabe name, Gizhiiwewidamookwe, means ‘speaks in a loud and clear voice woman.’ If there’s one thing I know about Peggy, it’s that she’ll use that voice to fight for all Minnesotans — whether they agree with her or not.
So let’s make it happen. Split a donation with Tobias Read today, and help send Peggy Flanagan to the Senate, where she’ll keep speaking up, fighting hard, and making sure we all have a good hotdish recipe. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-flanagan?refcode=250505em]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-flanagan?refcode=250505em]
- Al
P.S. I could go on and on about how great Peggy is. But instead, let me share a quick story from my first Senate campaign in 2008. Peggy was an organizer working with Native American communities and my advisor on Indian Affairs, and she told me if I went to the powwow at Red Lake, I should NOT dance. Good advice. But then a young man invited me to join in. So, I addressed the crowd and said, “Only if you don’t make fun of me after I leave.” They laughed and agreed, I danced, and a woman danced up to me and said, “You dance like a white guy.” I reminded her of the conditions of my dancing — to which she replied, “You said after you leave.” That was my first lesson in Ojibwe humor.
Tobias Read is Oregon's Secretary of State. To support him directly, please donate here. [https://www.tobiasread.com]
Peggy Flanagan is running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota. You can support her directly here. [https://peggyflanagan.com/]

clean_body: Hi folks, I want to tell you about someone who’s unbelievably great. Someone who understands what’s at stake in this moment, who listens, who leads, and who embodies the values that make Minnesota, well, Minnesotan . That person is Peggy Flanagan. And she’s running to represent our great state in the U.S. Senate. [] I’ve known Peggy for a long time. We both grew up in St. Louis Park (not at the same time — she’s young, and I’m old). Raised by a single mom, Peggy knows the impact of federal programs that help working families. They had a home because of a Section 8 housing voucher. Other public programs helped her mom afford health insurance and child care. She gets it because she’s lived it. And that’s exactly the kind of leader we need in Washington. (And frankly, what we have way too little of.) DONATE NOW [] Peggy’s got the experience, too. She’s currently serving as Minnesota’s Lieutenant Governor — the highest-ranking Native American woman ever elected to executive office in the U.S. Before that, she was a state legislator, a school board member, an executive director for the Children’s Defense Fund–Minnesota, she even worked for ME! But most importantly — she worked for my friend and hero, the late Senator Paul Wellstone. She and I both believe in his guiding principle: We all do better when we all do better. (Yes, it’s that simple. No, Republicans still don’t get it.) While Republicans hide from their constituents, Peggy is doing the opposite. She’s traveling the state hosting ‘Kitchen Table Conversations’—actually listening to real Minnesotans, having real conversations, and working to bring people together. She doesn't just want to fight against bad stuff. She wants to build something, too. Oh, and in case you’re wondering — yes, Peggy will be the first Native American woman ever elected to the U.S. Senate. It’s about time. She is Ojibwe and a member of White Earth, and her Anishinaabe name, Gizhiiwewidamookwe, means ‘speaks in a loud and clear voice woman.’ If there’s one thing I know about Peggy, it’s that she’ll use that voice to fight for all Minnesotans — whether they agree with her or not. So let’s make it happen. Split a donation with Tobias Read today, and help send Peggy Flanagan to the Senate, where she’ll keep speaking up, fighting hard, and making sure we all have a good hotdish recipe. [] DONATE NOW [] - Al P.S. I could go on and on about how great Peggy is. But instead, let me share a quick story from my first Senate campaign in 2008. Peggy was an organizer working with Native American communities and my advisor on Indian Affairs, and she told me if I went to the powwow at Red Lake, I should NOT dance. Good advice. But then a young man invited me to join in. So, I addressed the crowd and said, “Only if you don’t make fun of me after I leave.” They laughed and agreed, I danced, and a woman danced up to me and said, “You dance like a white guy.” I reminded her of the conditions of my dancing — to which she replied, “You said after you leave.” That was my first lesson in Ojibwe humor. Tobias Read is Oregon's Secretary of State. To support him directly, please donate here. [] Peggy Flanagan is running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota. You can support her directly here. [] [] [] [] Friends of Tobias Read (5208) PO Box 42307 Portland, OR 97242 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Mikie Sherrill
subject: I will not stay silent.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/88159?refcode=em20250505-8918&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8918%2E614464%2EGPS_d5)
I grew up during the Cold War. The Berlin Wall fell in my senior year of high school.
Then, I served in the Navy for almost ten years as a helicopter pilot and a Russian policy officer.
I served in places where people get disappeared off the streets for criticizing the regime. Where an offhand comment criticizing the leader lands you in prison.
**The Trump administration is taking us down a deeply dangerous path right now, and I refuse to stay silent.**
So I’m speaking out, and continuing to execute on our legislate, litigate, mobilize strategy. But I believe that one of the most important things we can do is show our dissent at the ballot box — and put Republicans on notice ahead of the midterms.
**We have to send a strong message here in New Jersey.**
That’s doubly true because GOP candidates in the race for New Jersey governor like Jack Ciattarelli aren’t just refusing to speak out against the Trump administration’s draconian actions — they’re promising to bring the MAGA agenda to New Jersey.
And given that the New Jersey governor’s race was decided by just three percentage points in 2021, sitting on the sidelines is off the table. The stakes are just too high.
**So here’s the bottom line: As governor, I’ll always defend the constitutional rights of New Jerseyans. I’ll never back down to a bully. And if you’re just as concerned as I am about the prospect of a Republican governor taking office next year, I need your help right now.**
**[Peter, will you please split any amount between my campaign for governor and Heartland Patriots today? New Jersey voters are already casting their ballots, and it’s going to take all of us to get our message out and win this race.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/88159?refcode=em20250505-8918&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=8918%2E614464%2EGPS_d5)**

name: Wes Moore
subject: We can’t let them skirt by with this.
body:
[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=33%2E1086043%2EHFFrBP ]Wes Moore for Governor[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1002&akid=33%2E1086043%2EHFFrBP ]Wes Moore for Governor

The beginning of the Trump administration has caused nothing but chaos for
Maryland.

If you’ve had trouble keeping up, let me break it down for you.

Just weeks after inauguration, Trump fired tens of thousands of our public
servants and cut more than $1.6 billion from critical programs Marylanders
rely on. That includes:

* Cuts to health and human services grants.
* Reduction in support for our farmers.
* Slashing over $480 million in public school funding.

These cuts aren’t just numbers. They’re attacks on anyone and everyone who
relies on basic public services in our state.

You’ll probably recall Trump’s lawless executive orders, from trying to
dismantle the Department of Education to end birthright citizenship. But
did you know that Trump and his Republican allies in Congress are
proposing cuts to Medicaid and the ACA that could jeopardize health
coverage for nearly 600K Maryland residents?

And thanks to Trump’s reckless on-again, off-again tariffs that push costs
onto the people, the average Maryland household could pay $4,900 more per
year just to get by.

This administration is threatening our rights, freedoms, and futures — and
we can’t let them just skirt by with it.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250505-1?refcode=em-20250505-1&refcode2=33_1086043_HFFrBP&akid=33%2E1086043%2EHFFrBP ]My administration is determined to stand up for Maryland families, no
matter what the Trump administration may throw our way. If you’re with me
and want to help me stay in this fight, please chip in anything you can to
my reelection campaign today.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250505-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250505-1&refcode2=33_1086043_HFFrBP&akid=33%2E1086043%2EHFFrBP ]Donate $5 ››

If you want to learn more about the toll Trump is taking on our state and
how to get involved, head to [ http://act.wesmoore.com/go/278?t=1003&akid=33%2E1086043%2EHFFrBP ]http://trumpattacksmd.com now.
Thank you for being part of this team.

Elevate,

Wes

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.wesmoore.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=33%2E1086043%2EHFFrBP

name: Americans for Responsible Innovation
subject: 📜 TAKE IT DOWN Act heads to the White House!
body:
Congress just passed its first substantial new regulatory law of the AI era.

| | | | | |
---
|
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=94f2b9000d&e=a07d75591f)
|
---
| | | | |
# Congress Passes First Tech Bill in Years
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
Last week, Congress passed its first substantial new law of the AI era. The TAKE IT DOWN Act cleared the House after having passed the Senate, putting it one signature away from becoming law.
Also in this week’s update, check out ARI’s latest virtual events on AI and the workforce and a debate over AI’s trajectory!
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
|
### House Passes the TAKE IT DOWN Act
---
| | | | | | | | | | | The TAKE IT DOWN Act, landmark legislation to combat the rise of non-consensual intimate images (NCII), including deepfakes, cleared the House of Representatives this month by a vote of 409 to 2. Having passed the Senate unanimously, the bill now heads to the White House, and all indications are that President Trump will sign it into law this month. ARI has been a vocal champion for the TAKE IT DOWN Act, and we [applauded House lawmakers](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=ef4ec899b9&e=a07d75591f) for taking swift action to protect Americans from non-consensual deepfakes.
---
| [POLITICO STORY](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=c3360f5873&e=a07d75591f)
---
| Although in retrospect it looks like the bill was destined to succeed, the Big Tech lobby and opponents threw up some significant roadblocks, raising concerns about whether it would withstand 1st Amendment challenges or even allow encrypted messaging apps to continue to exist. To quell these fears and lock down support before the vote, ARI hosted a virtual [press conference](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=d8c69ed88e&e=a07d75591f) featuring top legal scholars, including Tim Wu and Zephyr Teachout, who made the case for TAKE IT DOWN. Their message was clear: this bill is not only urgent, it’s constitutionally sound. Our press conference followed on [an op-ed](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=51be6112f9&e=a07d75591f) in Tech Policy Press and a [legal memo](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=9578a3e557&e=a07d75591f) from former DOJ official Slade Bond, who ARI tapped for assistance with the campaign.
[](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=f524211ab0&e=a07d75591f)_Watch the TAKE IT DOWN Act press conference with legal scholars and advocates._

---
|
---
|
### You're Invited: A Live Debate on AI's Future
---
| Are we on the verge of AI superintelligence or are we overhyping today’s tools? That’s the question we’ll tackle tomorrow, Tuesday, May 6 at 1pm ET, when the co-authors of two provocative papers — _AI 2027_ and _AI as a Normal Technology_ — will go head-to-head in a live debate hosted by ARI. [Register here](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=df5cc39378&e=a07d75591f) for the virtual event, or watch our livestream of the event on our [X](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=3745c365dd&e=a07d75591f), [LinkedIn](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=13885dd8a8&e=a07d75591f), or [YouTube](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=4591781fb6&e=a07d75591f)!
Eli Lifland of AI Futures Project and Sayash Kapoor of Princeton will share opposing views on the trajectory and impact of AI—and what it means for policy. The event is open to policymakers, the press, and the public as leaders think seriously about the road ahead.
[](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=d1ea80e24e&e=a07d75591f)
---
| [VIRTUAL EVENT REGISTRATION](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=03a85f7f99&e=a07d75591f)
---
|
---
|
### New White House AI in Education Executive Order
---
| | | | | | | | | | | The Trump Administration issued a new executive order on AI in education, with goals to increase U.S. AI fluency and encourage adoption of AI tools in classrooms. ARI [welcomed the move](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=1306742d68&e=a07d75591f) and noted that as AI reshapes jobs and the economy it’s a technology that students will need to become proficient in.
[](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=29cb515e75&e=a07d75591f)
---
|
---
|
### AI’s Workforce Disruption
---
| | | | | | | | | | | This month, ARI released a major new report— _The Technologist-Economist Disconnect_ —arguing that mainstream economic models have underestimated the scale and speed of AI-driven labor disruption. We examine how flexible, general-purpose systems like agentic AI could displace work across industries faster than many economists have predicted.
There are a range of forecasts on AI’s workforce impact, and we want to invite you to listen in on the debate. This Thursday, **ARI is hosting a virtual panel, AI’s Workforce Crossroads** , featuring policy thinkers and economists with differing perspectives. Moderated by ARI’s Senior Policy Director John Soroushian, the panel brings together leaders from across the political spectrum including Stanford University’s Erik Brynjolfsson, AFL-CIO Tech Institute leader Crystal Weise, and AEI’s Brent Orrell. [Register for the event here](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=7ad4b716fc&e=a07d75591f) or catch our livestream of the event on our [X](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=e65d70e350&e=a07d75591f), [LinkedIn](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=1e91165bd8&e=a07d75591f), or [YouTube](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=4df7c003f7&e=a07d75591f).
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=ea7672acea&e=a07d75591f)
| [REGISTER FOR THE PANEL](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=80797c90f3&e=a07d75591f)
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | [](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=5243e7e4bd&e=a07d75591f)
---
[](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=d0529c477c&e=a07d75591f)
---

body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23043%2E4449941%2EWv6tGR) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23043%2E4449941%2EWv6tGR) [**_NBC News_ : "Trump, asked if he has to 'uphold the Constitution,' says, 'I don't know'"](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61077?t=3&akid=23043%2E4449941%2EWv6tGR)** **Team – last Congress, Senator Chuck Schumer introduced the _No Kings Act_ to REVERSE the Supreme Court’s ruling that Donald Trump was immune from prosecution.

Now that Trump has been in office and repeatedly violated the Constitution but will face NO consequences, we NEED Congress to reintroduce the _No Kings Act_ ... before things get worse!!** [**1O,OOO SIGNATURES NEEDED: Demand Congress reintroduce the _No Kings Act_ and stop Donald Trump →**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61077?t=4&akid=23043%2E4449941%2EWv6tGR)
| [SIGN THE PETITION NOW →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61077?t=5&akid=23043%2E4449941%2EWv6tGR)
---
Here’s what the _No Kings Act_ would really do:

**ONE:** It would finally hold Trump accountable for his countless crimes — including his 34 felony convictions.

**TWO:** It would stop the conservative Supreme Court Justices’ corruption that they’ve gotten away with for far too long!

**THREE:** It would STOP Trump’s blatant disregard for the Constitution...including just last week when he threatened to ARREST judges!!

Take a look for yourself →
[](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61077?t=6&akid=23043%2E4449941%2EWv6tGR)
It’s so important that we reintroduce this bill ... there’s no telling what horrors Donald Trump could continue to unleash if we don’t stop him.
[**PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE: sign now to demand Congress reintroduce the _No Kings Act _→ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61077?t=7&akid=23043%2E4449941%2EWv6tGR)**
[SIGN THE PETITION NOW →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61077?t=8&akid=23043%2E4449941%2EWv6tGR)

Counting on you,

_Fight for Reform_

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: Make sense not cents
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
If we’re throwing money at something nobody uses, it’s time to put a stop to it. This legislation will save millions of dollars, and I’m ready to get it done.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107145430/543739126/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MzBmOWZlOS1lZjI5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Af8y3SZzU2u-5mel6_eG8yAGjeF-GbMYx9xEcScmY1M=&emci=8e0f9fe9-ef29-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=930f9fe9-ef29-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=7731215)
---

| | What if I told you we could save taxpayers over $85 million a year by eliminating the penny? **That might sound strange, but here's the truth: Each penny costs nearly 4 cents to make. That's right — we're spending four times its face value to mint a coin most Americans don't even use. And with material costs rising more than 20 percent year-over-year, it's only going to get more expensive to produce pennies. Ending production now could save the United States over $85 million annually.** Nearly two-thirds of pennies produced in the U.S. do not even recirculate. Billions of pennies disappear into couch cushions, junk drawers, and change jars or are simply lost, never to be used again. And, in an era of online and digital banking, only 16 percent of transactions are made with cash. **That's why I introduced****The Make Sense Not Cents Act,__****bipartisan legislation to retire the penny and start investing in what really matters.** Imagine what we could do with $85 million more in the federal budget each year:
* Feed hungry families and provide housing to those who need it most
* Jumpstart clean energy projects to protect our planet for generations to come
* Fund safer roads and bridges in your neighborhood
* And MORE!

It's the opposite of "common cents" for taxpayer dollars to fund wasteful penny production when we could put that money to work for you. The Make Sense Not Cents Act will save taxpayers millions, and tackling this kind of government waste is something both Democrats and Republicans can agree on. If we're throwing money at something nobody uses, it's time to put a stop to it. This legislation will save millions of dollars, and I'm ready to get it done. Onward! _Jeff_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Jeff Merkley instantly:
